Table vehicle.vin_registry {
  vin_registry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VIN registry record. Primary key for the VIN registry data product.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_configuration. Business justification: Each VIN corresponds to a single vehicle configuration; linking VIN to its configuration eliminates duplicated spec columns.']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Each VIN must be traceable to the homologation record under which it was approved for its destination market. Vehicle registration authorities require this link. vin_registry has homologation_market a']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Recall management, warranty analysis, and regulatory reporting require tracing each VIN directly to its originating engineering program. A domain expert would consider VIN-to-program traceability fund']
  battery_capacity_kwh decimal(8,2) [note: 'The total energy storage capacity of the high-voltage traction battery in kilowatt-hours. Applicable to HEV, PHEV, BEV, and FCEV vehicles. Null for pure ICE vehicles.']
  build_date date [note: 'The calendar date when the vehicle completed final assembly and rolled off the production line. Captured from Manufacturing Execution System (MES).']
  check_digit varchar [note: 'The 9th character of the VIN used to validate VIN authenticity through a mathematical algorithm. Required in North American markets. Value is 0-9 or X (representing 10).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this VIN registry record was first created in the enterprise data system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  curb_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The weight of the vehicle with standard equipment, full fluids, and a full tank of fuel, but without passengers or cargo. Measured in kilograms.']
  destination_market varchar [note: 'The intended sales and distribution market for this vehicle unit. May differ from homologation market for export vehicles. Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  emission_standard varchar [note: 'The regulatory emissions certification standard this vehicle complies with (e.g., EPA Tier 3, Euro 6d, China 6, BS VI). Defines allowable pollutant limits.']
  eop_date date [note: 'The planned or actual date when series production ceased for this vehicle\'s model and platform configuration. Null if still in production.']
  epa_combined_mpg decimal(6,2) [note: 'The EPA-certified combined city/highway fuel economy rating in miles per gallon. Applicable to ICE, HEV, and PHEV vehicles sold in the United States.']
  fuel_tank_capacity_liters decimal(8,2) [note: 'The total volume capacity of the primary fuel tank in liters. For electric vehicles, this represents the battery capacity equivalent or is null.']
  gvwr_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable total weight of the vehicle including chassis, body, engine, fluids, passengers, and cargo, as certified by the manufacturer. Measured in kilograms.']
  homologation_market varchar [note: 'The primary regulatory market for which this vehicle was homologated and certified (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN, JPN). Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this VIN registry record was most recently updated. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  line_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the vehicle exited the final assembly line and passed end-of-line quality inspection. Captured from MES.']
  model_year_decoded varchar [note: 'The decoded four-digit calendar year representing the model year (e.g., 2023, 2024). Derived from the 10th VIN character.']
  msrp_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the MSRP amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY, JPY).']
  obd_protocol varchar [note: 'The diagnostic communication protocol standard implemented in this vehicle\'s Electronic Control Units (ECUs) for emissions and diagnostics monitoring.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The 11th character of the VIN identifying the specific assembly plant where the vehicle was manufactured. Manufacturer-specific encoding.']
  production_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Characters 12-17 of the VIN representing the sequential production number assigned to this vehicle unit at the assembly plant. Resets annually per plant.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vehicle unit is currently subject to one or more open safety or compliance recalls issued by the manufacturer or regulatory authority.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'The overall safety assessment rating from regulatory crash testing programs (e.g., NHTSA 5-star, Euro NCAP 5-star). Reflects occupant protection performance.']
  sop_date date [note: 'The date when series production began for this vehicle\'s model and platform configuration. Indicates transition from pre-production to volume manufacturing.']
  telematics_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vehicle is equipped with connected vehicle telematics hardware and has an active data subscription for remote diagnostics, OTA updates, and mobility services.']
  vds varchar [note: 'Characters 4-9 of the VIN that describe vehicle attributes such as model, body style, engine type, and restraint system. Manufacturer-specific encoding.']
  vehicle_lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of this vehicle unit from manufacturing through end-of-life. Tracks the vehicle\'s journey from plant to customer to disposal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_production|in_production|quality_hold|in_transit|dealer_inventory|retail_sold|in_service|recalled|scrapped|exported|end_of_life — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vin varchar [note: 'The 17-character alphanumeric Vehicle Identification Number that uniquely identifies this physical vehicle unit worldwide. Excludes letters I, O, Q to avoid confusion with numbers.']
  vis varchar [note: 'Characters 10-17 of the VIN that include model year, plant code, and production sequence number. Uniquely identifies this specific vehicle unit.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'The date when the manufacturer\'s base warranty coverage expires for this vehicle unit. Calculated from warranty start date plus warranty duration.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'The date when the manufacturer\'s warranty coverage begins for this vehicle unit. Typically the retail delivery date or first registration date.']
  wltp_combined_consumption decimal(6,2) [note: 'The WLTP-certified combined fuel consumption in liters per 100 kilometers. Applicable to vehicles sold in markets requiring WLTP certification (EU, UK, etc.).']
  wmi varchar [note: 'The first three characters of the VIN that identify the manufacturer and country of origin. Assigned by SAE International.']

  Note: 'SSOT for every physical vehicle instance identified by its 17-character VIN per ISO 3779. Captures decoded VIN structure (WMI, VDS, VIS), manufacturing plant code, production sequence number, build date, model year (MY), line-off timestamp, homologation market, and current lifecycle status (pre-production, in-production, in-transit, in-service, end-of-life). This is the enterprise anchor record — the single identity key — that every downstream domain (sales, aftersales, logistics, mobility, finance) references to identify a specific vehicle unit.'
}

Automotive finance requires this link for product-line']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Required for Program Management report linking each vehicle model to its engineering program for budgeting, target metrics, and compliance.']

  model_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the model\'s positioning and key attributes.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the vehicle model (e.g., Model 3, F‑150).']
  model_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the model.']
  msrp_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base MSRP in US dollars.']
  ncap_safety_rating varchar [note: 'Euro NCAP safety rating.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain architecture category.']
  primary_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market where the model is primarily sold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: north_america|europe|asia|latin_america|middle_east|africa|australia — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  safety_standard varchar [note: 'Safety regulatory framework governing the model.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of occupants.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Market segment classification of the model.']
  sop_date date [note: 'Date when production of the model commenced.']
  transmission_gears varchar [note: 'Count of forward gears in the transmission.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of drivetrain transmission.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the model record.']
  variant_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct trim/option configurations derived from this model.']
  vehicle_class varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the vehicle purpose.']
  wheelbase_mm decimal(7,1) [note: 'Distance between front and rear axles.']
  width_mm decimal(7,1) [note: 'Overall vehicle width.']

  Note: 'Defines the commercial vehicle model (nameplate) as a business entity — e.g., F-150, Camry, Model 3. Captures model name, brand/marque, vehicle segment (sedan, SUV, pickup, commercial), body style, drive configuration (FWD, RWD, AWD, 4WD), primary market, launch MY, EOP MY, and program code. Serves as the top-level classification anchor for all vehicle configurations and trim hierarchies.'
}

Automotive program finance team']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Each model year program belongs to a specific vehicle model; adding model_id FK enables proper hierarchy and eliminates the siloed vehicle_model_year_program table.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: A vehicle model year program is the commercial execution of an engineering vehicle program for a specific model year. Program management, milestone tracking, and budget reporting require this direct l']

  Note: 'Governs the MY (Model Year) lifecycle for each model, from SOP (Start of Production) through EOP (End of Production). Tracks planned SOP date, actual SOP date, planned EOP date, actual EOP date, regulatory MY designation, CAFE compliance year, homologation status per market, and program phase (concept, development, launch, production, wind-down). Links model to its annual production program and drives downstream planning in manufacturing and supply chain.'
}

Table vehicle.trim_level {
  trim_level_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle_trim_level data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: Each trim level has a defined BOM specifying its standard content. Trim-level BOM management is a core product planning process for parts content definition, option pricing, and supplier sourcing deci']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Trim levels are defined per model; adding model_id FK removes duplicated model attributes and ties trim to its parent model.']
  model_year_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program. Business justification: vehicle_trim_level is described as \'ordered hierarchy of trim grades within a model-MY combination\'. Currently it only links to model via model_id, but the MY dimension is missing. Adding vehicle_mode']

  Note: 'Defines the ordered hierarchy of trim grades within a model-MY combination (e.g., Base, XL, XLT, Lariat, Platinum, Limited). Captures trim code, trim name, market positioning tier, MSRP base price, standard feature set description, target market region, and active/inactive status. Trim levels are the primary commercial differentiation unit used in sales, dealer ordering, and MSRP pricing.'
}

  wltp_range_km varchar [note: 'Estimated driving range under WLTP testing.']

  Note: 'Authoritative catalog of all powertrain configurations available across the vehicle lineup. Captures powertrain type (ICE, HEV, PHEV, BEV, FCEV), engine displacement, cylinder count, fuel type, electric motor peak power (kW), combined system power, transmission type, drive type, EPA/WLTP fuel economy or range rating, CO2 emissions (g/km), battery capacity (kWh for EV/PHEV), and charging standard (CCS, CHAdeMO, NACS). Used for homologation, CAFE compliance, and consumer-facing specifications.'
}

  width_max_mm varchar [note: 'Longest overall width (mm) that the platform can accommodate.']
  width_min_mm varchar [note: 'Shortest overall width (mm) that the platform can accommodate.']

  Note: 'Defines the vehicle platform (architecture) that underpins one or more models. Captures platform code, platform name, architecture generation, wheelbase range, track width range, structural material strategy (steel, aluminum, mixed), compatible powertrain families, maximum GVW rating, and platform owner business unit. Platforms are shared across multiple nameplates and model years, making this a critical cross-model reference for engineering, manufacturing, and supply chain.'

Table vehicle.configuration {
  configuration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each vehicle configuration record.']

  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_change. Business justification: Configuration traceability to the engineering change that introduced it is required for change impact analysis and audit.']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Each vehicle configuration follows a control plan that defines inspection and test requirements; FK links configuration to its quality control plan.']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: Each vehicle configuration must reference the design specification governing component requirements for compliance and validation.']
  ecu_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.ecu_catalog. Business justification: Vehicle configuration includes a set of ECUs; adding ECU catalog FK provides direct reference.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Vehicle configurations are market/jurisdiction-specific (different emissions standards, safety requirements per market). Configuration already has market_country_code as a code field; this FK to juris']
  model_id varchar [note: 'FK to vehicle.model']
  model_year_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program. Business justification: configuration.model_year is an INT denormalization of the authoritative vehicle_model_year_program record. The configuration product description explicitly states it combines \'model, MY, trim, powertr']
  platform_id varchar [note: 'FK to vehicle.platform']

Adding vehicle_trim_level_id as a FK normalizes this relationship, enabling proper referential int']

  wheel_size_inch varchar [note: 'Diameter of wheels in inches.']

  Note: 'The fully specified, buildable vehicle configuration combining model, MY, trim, powertrain, and market. Each record represents a unique orderable specification (analogous to a manufacturing SKU or dealer order code). Captures configuration code, market region, build feasibility status, MSRP, destination charge, fuel economy label values, emissions certification status, and production plant assignment. This is the master configuration record that bridges product planning to manufacturing BOM explosion and dealer order management.'
}

Business justification: Build specs must be validated against specific regulatory requirements (FMVSS, ECE regulations, emissions standards). Linking build_spec to regulatory_requirement enables production teams to verify ea']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Program-level production volume reporting, cost tracking, and regulatory compliance require linking each build spec directly to its originating vehicle program. Manufacturing engineers and program man']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: build_spec is described as \'the as-built specification record for a specific VIN\'. It currently has configuration_id (linking to the configuration template) but no FK to the specific VIN instance in v']
  build_date date [note: 'Calendar date the vehicle left the production line.']
  build_spec_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the build specification record.']
  co2_emissions_g_per_km varchar [note: 'Measured carbon dioxide emissions per kilometer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the build specification record was created in the data lake.']
  diagnostic_trouble_code_initial varchar [note: 'First DTC recorded at the end of production, if any.']
  emissions_rating varchar [note: 'Regulatory emissions classification applicable to the vehicle.']
  fleet_spec_flag boolean [note: 'True if the vehicle was built to a fleet or corporate specification.']
  fuel_efficiency_mpg decimal(4,2) [note: 'Manufacturer‑reported fuel economy in miles per gallon (city/highway combined).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the build specification record.']
  ncap_rating varchar [note: 'New Car Assessment Programme safety rating.']
  ota_updatable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle supports over‑the‑air updates.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the vehicle was assembled.']
  production_line varchar [note: 'Specific assembly line or bay within the plant.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approvals for the vehicle configuration.']
  safety_feature_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of safety system identifiers (e.g., ADAS, ESC).']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to the vehicle on the production day.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Baseline software version loaded on vehicle ECUs at build.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., MES, PLM) that supplied the as‑built data.']
  special_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was built to a special customer order.']
  v2x_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'True if vehicle‑to‑everything communication hardware is installed.']
  vehicle_weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Curb weight of the vehicle as built, in kilograms.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty period expires.']
  warranty_mileage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum mileage covered under the warranty.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty period begins.']

  Note: 'The as-built specification record for a specific VIN, capturing the exact combination of configuration, options, colors, powertrain, and features installed on that vehicle unit as it rolled off the production line. Captures VIN reference, configuration code, exterior color, interior trim selection, all installed option codes, actual build date, plant code, production sequence number, and any deviation from standard specification (e.g., special order, fleet spec, running change). This is the definitive as-built record used by aftersales, warranty, recall population identification, and residual value analytics.'
}

Table vehicle.homologation {
  homologation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each homologation record.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_configuration. Business justification: Homologation is tied to a specific vehicle configuration; FK eliminates duplicated regulatory fields.']
  homologation_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.homologation_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory compliance audit requires each homologation record to reference the specific requirement it satisfies.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Vehicle homologation is jurisdiction-specific — a vehicle approved in the EU requires separate homologation from US or China approval. Linking homologation to jurisdiction enables market-specific appr']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.standard. Business justification: Homologation certificates are issued against specific regulatory/quality standards (UN ECE regulations, FMVSS, Euro NCAP). Linking homologation records to quality.standard enables regulatory complianc']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Homologation activities are conducted under a specific vehicle program. Program-level regulatory compliance dashboards and type approval submission tracking require direct linkage of homologation reco']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: homologation.vehicle_identification_number is a STRING column storing a VIN for VIN-level homologation records. This should be normalized to a FK → vin_registry.vin_registry_id. While homologation is ']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the homologation was officially granted.']
  approval_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the approval applies to the entire vehicle or a specific component.']
  authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the government or standards authority that issued the approval.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official certificate or approval number assigned by the authority.']
  certification_status_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance status was last updated.']
  compliance_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital copy of the approval certificate or related documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the homologation record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the homologation becomes effective for production.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the homologation ceases to be effective (if different from expiry).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the homologation expires and must be renewed or re‑validated.']
  homologation_category varchar [note: 'Primary focus of the approval: safety, emissions, or both.']
  homologation_scope varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the record represents a whole‑vehicle type approval or a component‑level approval.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'True if the homologation is currently active and usable.']
  is_ota_updatable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved component can receive Over‑The‑Air updates.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance inspection associated with this homologation.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the homologation record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the homologation record.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next renewal or re‑certification of the homologation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations related to the homologation.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for when the record was initially loaded.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for the latest modification of the record.']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory regime under which the homologation was granted (e.g., FMVSS, ECE, NCAP, CARB, EPA).']
  regulatory_version varchar [note: 'Specific version or amendment of the regulatory framework applied (e.g., FMVSS 123‑2022).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the homologation data (e.g., SAP PLM, Teamcenter).']

  Note: 'Tracks the regulatory type-approval and homologation status of vehicle configurations for each target market. Captures homologation type (whole vehicle type approval, component approval), regulatory framework (FMVSS, ECE, NCAP, CARB, EPA), approval authority, certificate number, approval date, expiry date, applicable model/MY/market, emissions standard tier (e.g., EPA Tier 3, Euro 6d), and compliance status. Critical for market launch readiness and regulatory reporting.'
}

Table vehicle.ecu_catalog {
  ecu_catalog_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each ECU catalog entry.']

  ecu_catalog_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the ECU, including key capabilities and notes.']
  ecu_code varchar [note: 'Unique OEM‑assigned code that identifies the ECU across the enterprise.']
  ecu_function varchar [note: 'Primary functional domain of the ECU (e.g., engine control, transmission control, ADAS, body control, infotainment, OBD gateway).']
  ecu_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the electronic control unit.']
  end_of_life_date date [note: 'Planned date after which the ECU will no longer be produced or supported.']
  functional_safety_asil varchar [note: 'Automotive Safety Integrity Level assigned to the ECU (A‑D).']
  hardware_part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer part number for the ECU hardware module.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ECU entry is deprecated and should not be used for new vehicle builds.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ECU within the product portfolio.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ECU catalog record.']
  ota_updatable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ECU firmware can be updated over‑the‑air.']
  power_consumption_watts decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical steady‑state power draw of the ECU in watts.']
  regulatory_approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the ECU received regulatory approval.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the ECU.']
  software_baseline_version varchar [note: 'Baseline software version shipped with the ECU at start of production.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that supplied the ECU data (e.g., Teamcenter PLM, SAP, MES).']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external supplier that provides the ECU hardware.']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Supplier‑assigned part number for the ECU hardware.']
  v2x_communication_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the ECU supports vehicle‑to‑everything communication.']
  voltage_volts decimal(6,2) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage supplied to the ECU.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Standard warranty duration provided for the ECU.']
  weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Physical weight of the ECU unit.']

  Note: 'Catalog of all ECU (Electronic Control Unit) and embedded software modules installed across vehicle configurations. Captures ECU code, ECU name, ECU function (engine control, transmission control, ADAS domain controller, body control, infotainment, OBD gateway), supplier, hardware part number, software baseline version, OTA-updatable flag, communication protocol (CAN, LIN, Ethernet, AUTOSAR), and applicable vehicle configurations. Supports OTA update management, cybersecurity compliance, and aftersales diagnostics.'
}

Captures base MSRP, destination and delivery charge, gas guzzler tax (if applicable), federal EV tax credit eligibility, effective date, expiry date, currency, and market region. Provides the authoritative pricing reference for dealer ordering, window sticker generation, and sales analytics. Distinct from dealer invoice pricing (owned by billing domain).'

Table vehicle.lifecycle_event {
  lifecycle_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each vehicle lifecycle event record.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.change. Business justification: Lifecycle events such as recalls, field fixes, and OTA updates are triggered by engineering change orders. Field quality reporting and NHTSA recall traceability require linking each lifecycle event di']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Vehicle lifecycle events occurring at a dealership need a dealership_id FK to attribute service, warranty, and recall actions to the correct dealer.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Recall notification and delivery confirmation workflows require directly associating lifecycle events with the registered owner/party. OEM recall management systems track which party was notified at e']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Vehicle lifecycle events reference a VIN; linking to vin_registry provides a stable surrogate and removes the raw VIN column.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the event (e.g., production, logistics, sales, post‑sale, disposal). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production|logistics|sales|post_sale|disposal — promote to reference product]']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative describing the event details and context.']
  event_location_city varchar [note: 'City name where the event was recorded.']
  event_location_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the event took place.']
  event_location_latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the event location in decimal degrees.']
  event_location_longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the event location in decimal degrees.']
  event_location_state varchar [note: 'State or province abbreviation for the event location, if applicable.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or internal notes attached to the event.']
  event_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of events for the same VIN, starting at 1.']
  event_source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or component within the source system that emitted the event.']
  event_source_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the source system instance that emitted the event (e.g., message ID, log ID).']
  event_source_system varchar [note: 'Higher‑level system or application that originated the event record.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Processing status of the event record within the data pipeline.']
  event_subtype varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the event category.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the lifecycle event actually occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of lifecycle transition (e.g., manufactured, pdi_completed, shipped_to_dealer, sold_retail, sold_fleet, exported, titled, re_acquired, scrapped, stolen, total_loss). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufactured|pdi_completed|shipped_to_dealer|sold_retail|sold_fleet|exported|titled|re_acquired|scrapped|stolen|total_loss — promote to reference product]']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event is considered critical for compliance or safety reporting.']
  odometer_reading_km varchar [note: 'Vehicle mileage at the time of the event, expressed in kilometers.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record.']
  triggering_system varchar [note: 'Name of the IT/OT system that generated the event (e.g., MES, DMS, Telematics Platform).']

  Note: 'Transactional log of significant lifecycle state transitions for a specific VIN throughout its life. Captures event type (manufactured, PDI-completed, shipped-to-dealer, sold-retail, sold-fleet, exported, titled, re-acquired, scrapped, stolen, total-loss), event timestamp, event location, responsible party, odometer reading at event, and triggering system. Provides the complete lifecycle audit trail for a vehicle unit from production to end-of-life, supporting title history, recall tracking, and residual value analytics.'
}

Table vehicle.ownership {
  ownership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the vehicle_ownership association']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to party']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vin_registry']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the party acquired the vehicle']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date the party disposed of the vehicle']
  ownership_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the ownership contract or agreement']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Type of ownership (e.g., lease, purchase, fleet)']

  Note: 'This association product represents the ownership relationship between a vehicle (vin_registry) and a party (customer). It captures acquisition and disposition dates, ownership type, and ownership number, tracking the history of which parties own which vehicles.. Existence Justification: A vehicle can be owned by multiple parties over its lifecycle (e.g., resale), and a party can own multiple vehicles simultaneously. The business actively records each ownership event with dates, type, and ownership number, and users query ownership history for warranty, service, recall, and resale analytics.'
}

Table vehicle.trim_option_availability {
  trim_option_availability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this trim-option availability record (primary key)']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: Option package definition relies on an engineering BOM that lists parts and quantities; linking enables traceability from package to BOM.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_configuration. Business justification: Option packages are assigned to specific vehicle configurations; FK creates the relationship.']
  primary_trim_level_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the trim level in this availability record']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current availability status of this option package for this trim level in the ordering system']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this trim-option combination was discontinued (null if still active)']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this trim-option combination became available for ordering']
  is_standard_inclusion boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this option package is standard equipment on this trim level (true) or an optional add-on (false)']
  option_package_code varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the option package in this availability record']
  price_uplift_amount decimal [note: 'MSRP price increase when this option package is added to this trim level (can be zero for standard equipment)']
  vehicle_option_package_code varchar [note: 'Foreign key to vehicle_option_package']

  Note: 'This association product represents the catalog-level compatibility matrix between vehicle option packages and trim levels. It captures which option packages are orderable with which trim levels, whether they are standard or optional equipment, pricing uplifts, and effective date ranges. Each record defines one orderable combination used in dealer order configuration, window sticker generation, and MSRP calculation. This is distinct from vehicle_configuration (which represents a specific built vehicle) — this product represents the orderable catalog space managed by product planning.. Existence Justification: In automotive manufacturing, OEMs maintain an explicit catalog-level compatibility matrix defining which option packages can be ordered with which trim levels. A single option package (e.g., \'Technology Package\') is orderable across multiple trim levels (Base, XLT, Lariat) with different pricing and standard/optional status per trim. Conversely, each trim level has dozens to hundreds of available option packages. This matrix is actively managed by product planning teams and drives dealer order configuration, window sticker generation, and MSRP calculation.'
}

Table engineering.vehicle_program {
  vehicle_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for vehicle_program']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Program budgeting uses a Cost Center; finance cost center reports expenses per vehicle program.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Program profitability analysis assigns each vehicle program to a Profit Center for revenue and margin reporting.']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project. Business justification: Vehicle programs in automotive OEMs are directly managed as finance projects for program P&L, ROI reporting, and capital allocation. Program controllers and finance teams require this join for program']

  budget_allocation decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated to the program (currency defined by currency_code).']
  cad_release_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CAD data set released for the program.']
  cae_release_version varchar [note: 'Version of the CAE simulation package released for the program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vehicle program record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for monetary values.']
  digital_twin_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a digital twin is maintained for the vehicle program.']
  drivetrain varchar [note: 'Drive configuration of the vehicle.']
  emission_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory emissions standard the vehicle must meet.']
  end_date date [note: 'Target date for End of Production (EOP) of the program.']
  engineering_change_order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of ECOs/ECNs issued for the program.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for market launch of the vehicle.']
  model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year covered by the program.']
  model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year covered by the program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, observations, or comments captured by engineering.']
  ota_update_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle will support Over‑The‑Air software updates.']
  platform_architecture varchar [note: 'Technical platform on which the vehicle is built (e.g., "Modular Electric Architecture").']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Primary propulsion technology of the vehicle.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used across systems to reference the program (e.g., "P1234").']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the vehicle program (e.g., "All‑New X5").']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the program as a nameplate, platform, or concept.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approvals for the program.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Market segment the vehicle belongs to (e.g., SUV, sedan).']
  start_date date [note: 'Target date for Start of Production (SOP) of the program.']
  target_cost_per_vehicle decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target average manufacturing cost per vehicle (currency defined by currency_code).']
  target_emissions_g_per_km decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target CO₂ emissions in grams per kilometer.']
  target_fuel_efficiency_mpg decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target fuel economy in miles per gallon for ICE/HEV variants.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Primary geographic market(s) for the vehicle (e.g., "North America").']
  target_production_volume varchar [note: 'Planned total number of vehicles to be produced for the program.']
  target_range_km varchar [note: 'Target all‑electric driving range in kilometers for EV variants.']
  target_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target curb weight of the vehicle in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the vehicle program record.']
  vehicle_class varchar [note: 'Regulatory vehicle class (e.g., "Passenger", "Light Commercial").']
  vehicle_program_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the program objectives and scope.']
  vehicle_program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the program.']

  Note: 'Master record for a vehicle development program (nameplate/platform program), capturing program code, program name, vehicle segment, platform architecture, SOP (Start of Production) target date, EOP (End of Production) date, MY (Model Year) scope, program phase (concept, development, validation, launch), program director, budget allocation, and program status. Serves as the top-level anchor for all engineering activities within a development cycle. Owned by Siemens Teamcenter PLM.'
}

Table engineering.bom {
  bom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the engineering bill of materials record.']
  apqp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: APQP plans govern product quality planning for specific BOM configurations. Launch readiness assessments, PPAP submissions, and quality gates are BOM-specific. Core APQP-to-product structure linkage.']
  bom_change_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change order linked to this BOM revision.']
  bom_engineering_change_order_change_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change order linked to this BOM revision.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: BOM cost allocation is performed via a Cost Center; replace plain cost_center attribute with FK for accurate finance reporting.']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: BOMs specify parts that must be sourced from suppliers. Procurement teams link engineering BOMs to supplier-specific inbound parts for make-vs-buy decisions, sourcing strategies, and cost rollups. Cri']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Needed for BOM‑to‑inventory allocation process, enabling the production scheduling system to reserve SKUs for each BOM.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: An engineering BOM belongs to a specific vehicle program. The bom table currently stores program_name as a denormalized STRING. Adding vehicle_program_id FK normalizes this relationship and allows joi']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the BOM revision was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the BOM revision.']
  bom_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier that uniquely identifies the BOM within a program.']
  bom_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the BOM purpose and scope.']
  bom_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the engineering BOM.']
  bom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM: engineering (eBOM), manufacturing (mBOM), or service (sBOM).']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason documented for the latest BOM revision.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard to which the BOM complies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the BOM record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the BOM expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the BOM becomes effective for production.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the BOM is locked for further changes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent engineering review of the BOM.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the BOM record.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle for which the BOM is defined.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Engineering department responsible for the BOM.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the BOM will be used.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Current release state of the BOM.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the BOM (e.g., A, B, C).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'PLM system where the BOM originates.']
  total_parts_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct part numbers referenced in the BOM.']
  total_quantity varchar [note: 'Sum of all component quantities across the BOM.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the BOM record.']
  vehicle_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific vehicle variant (e.g., Sport, Luxury).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Internal version counter for the BOM record.']

  Note: 'Engineering Bill of Materials (eBOM) header record representing the complete structured parts list for a vehicle program or variant at a specific revision level. Captures BOM type (eBOM, mBOM, sBOM), program reference, model year, configuration context, effectivity dates, release status, BOM owner, and PLM system source. The eBOM is the authoritative design-intent parts structure managed in Siemens Teamcenter and ENOVIA before handoff to manufacturing BOM.'
}

Table engineering.bom_line {
  bom_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the engineering_bom_line data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_bom. Business justification: A line item belongs to a single engineering BOM; adding engineering_bom_id creates the required parent link.']
  parent_assembly_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Parent assembly reference is a part_master; replace with descriptive FK to part_master.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Each BOM line item maps to a specific procurable SKU for MRP explosion and procurement planning. Automotive MRP runs require line-level SKU references to generate purchase requisitions and stock requi']

  Note: 'Individual line item within an engineering BOM, representing a single part-to-parent relationship in the BOM hierarchy. Captures parent assembly reference, child part number, find number, quantity, unit of measure, effectivity start/end dates, variant applicability, substitution flags, BOM level, and engineering change reference. Supports multi-level BOM explosion and where-used analysis. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter BOM Management.'
}

Table engineering.part_master {
  part_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for part_master']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Part cost accounting charges material cost to a Cost Center; finance tracks part expenses per cost center.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Parts must be linked to applicable regulatory requirements for compliance tracking during part engineering.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Supplier Management Process: engineering part master must be linked to the external supplier record for PPAP, cost, and lead‑time tracking.']
  cad_model_reference varchar [note: 'Path or identifier of the CAD 3D model stored in the PLM system.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of the part in US dollars as defined in the ERP system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the part master record was first created in the system.']
  criticality varchar [note: 'Criticality of the part to vehicle safety or performance.']
  drawing_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the engineering drawing document for the part.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current part version became effective.']
  eol_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for part end‑of‑life (e.g., technology superseded, supplier discontinued).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the part is scheduled to be retired or become obsolete, if applicable.']
  height_mm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical height of the part in millimetres.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest inspection.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is currently active for use in new designs.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality inspection of the part.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the part master record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average supplier lead time in days for this part.']
  length_mm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical length of the part in millimetres.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the part within PLM.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the part (e.g., steel, aluminum, plastic).']
  obsolescence_notice boolean [note: 'True if an official obsolescence notice has been issued for the part.']
  part_classification varchar [note: 'High‑level functional classification of the part.']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the part\'s function, features, and application.']
  part_family varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of related parts sharing common characteristics.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Unique part number assigned by the organization, used to identify the part across systems.']
  part_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the part by its role in the product structure.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Quality rating based on internal quality metrics (e.g., PPAP status).']
  reach_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part complies with REACH chemical regulations (True=Compliant).']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Current revision identifier of the part. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: A|B|C|D|E|F|G|H|I|J — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rohs_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part complies with RoHS restrictions (True=Compliant).']
  supplier_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number used by the supplier for this component.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Numeric version of the part master record for internal version control.']
  volume_cm3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Calculated volume of the part in cubic centimetres.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the part in kilograms.']
  width_mm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical width of the part in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Engineering part master record representing a unique part or component managed within the PLM system. Captures part number, part name, part classification, material type, weight, dimensions, drawing number, CAD model reference, lifecycle state (in-work, released, obsolete), revision level, owning engineer, supplier part number, REACH/RoHS compliance flag, and part family. Authoritative source for engineering part identity in Siemens Teamcenter / PTC Windchill.'
}

  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the record.']

  Note: 'Engineering design specification document record capturing the technical requirements and design intent for a vehicle system, subsystem, or component. Includes specification number, title, specification type (system, subsystem, component, interface), applicable program, revision history, author, approval status, linked requirements, target performance values, and regulatory references (FMVSS, UNECE, NCAP). Managed in Teamcenter or ENOVIA document management.'
}

  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the engineering change.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the engineering change record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the change record, incremented on each revision.']

  Note: 'Engineering Change Order/Notice (ECO/ECN) record capturing a formal request to modify a part, assembly, design specification, or BOM. Includes change number, change type (ECR/ECO/ECN), title, reason for change (cost reduction, quality, regulatory, customer request), affected parts list, affected programs, initiator, approver chain, priority, implementation date, cost impact estimate, and change status (draft, under review, approved, implemented, rejected). Managed in Siemens Teamcenter Change Management.'
}

Table engineering.fmea_record {
  fmea_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the FMEA record.']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: FMEA records analyze the risks associated with specific design specifications. Linking fmea_record to design_specification creates traceability between the design intent (spec) and the risk analysis (']
  dvp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.dvp_plan. Business justification: FMEA records feed directly into DVP plans — identified failure modes and recommended actions drive the test cases in the DVP. Linking fmea_record to dvp_plan enables traceability from risk identificat']
  primary_fmea_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the part (or component) that the FMEA analyzes.']

  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: FMEA records are conducted for specific vehicle programs. Linking fmea_record to vehicle_program enables program-level risk reporting and ensures FMEA coverage can be tracked per program. This is a st']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the recommended action was actually completed.']
  analysis_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the cross‑functional team that performed the FMEA.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the FMEA record received formal approval.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FMEA record was first created in the system.']
  current_controls varchar [note: 'Existing controls or safeguards that address the failure mode.']
  detection_rating varchar [note: 'Detection score (1‑10) representing the ability of current controls to detect the failure before it reaches the customer.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed FMEA PDF or digital twin document stored in the repository.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the FMEA analysis is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the FMEA analysis is considered active.']
  failure_effect varchar [note: 'Potential effect on the vehicle or process if the failure mode occurs.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Textual description of the way the part or process could fail.']
  fmea_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the FMEA record, often used in reports and traceability.']
  fmea_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FMEA record.']
  fmea_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the analysis is a Design FMEA (DFMEA) or Process FMEA (PFMEA).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the FMEA.']
  occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Occurrence score (1‑10) indicating how likely the failure mode is to happen.']
  process_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing process step when the FMEA is a PFMEA.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Proposed corrective or preventive action to reduce risk.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the FMEA record.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High‑level risk classification derived from the RPN.']
  rpn varchar [note: 'Calculated risk priority number (S × O × D) used to prioritize mitigation actions.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Severity score (1‑10) reflecting the seriousness of the failure effect.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source application (e.g., Teamcenter, ENOVIA) that originated the record.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the recommended action should be completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the FMEA record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric version label (e.g., v1.0, v2.1) for the FMEA record.']

  Note: 'FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) record capturing the systematic risk analysis of a design or process. Includes FMEA ID, FMEA type (DFMEA — Design FMEA, PFMEA — Process FMEA), associated part or process, failure mode description, potential effect of failure, severity rating, potential cause, occurrence rating, current controls, detection rating, RPN (Risk Priority Number), recommended actions, responsible engineer, and completion date. Managed per IATF 16949 and AIAG-VDA FMEA methodology.'
}

  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation.   sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Linking dvp_plan to sku_master enables test readiness planning — program managers verify that requi']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle program to which the plan belongs.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: DVP (Design Verification Plan) test programs are funded through WBS elements under R&D projects in automotive OEMs. dvp_plan.cost_estimate_usd must be tracked against a WBS element for program cost co']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the last test in the plan was completed.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the plan received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the verification plan.']
  completed_test_count varchar [note: 'Number of test cases that have been completed.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of tests completed (0‑100).']
  cost_estimate_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to execute the verification plan, expressed in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plan record was created.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Web link or repository path to the full DVP document.']
  dvp_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the verification plan.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the plan is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the plan becomes active for execution.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test procedures are automated.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is locked from further edits.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the plan.']
  last_status_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the plan status.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle variant the plan applies to.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments about the verification plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business-visible alphanumeric code used to reference the DVP plan.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the scope and objectives of the plan.']
  plan_title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the verification plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plan indicating the level of the product it validates.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for completing all tests in the plan.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the plan.']
  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory sign‑off is required for the plan.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of regulatory approval for the plan.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the verification plan.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the record (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter PLM).']
  system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vehicle system (e.g., powertrain, ADAS) the plan validates.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual environment where testing occurs.']
  test_phase varchar [note: 'Lifecycle phase during which the tests are performed.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the tests in the plan.']
  total_test_count varchar [note: 'Total number of test cases defined in the plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plan record.']
  vehicle_variant varchar [note: 'Specific vehicle variant or trim level associated with the plan.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Semantic version identifier for the plan (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']

  Note: 'Design Verification Plan (DVP) record defining the complete test plan for validating a vehicle system or component against its design specifications. Captures DVP ID, plan title, associated part or system, program reference, total test count, completion percentage, responsible engineer, approval status, linked specification, and planned vs. actual completion dates. Acts as the master test planning document that governs all validation_test records for a given scope.'
}

  Note: 'Homologation and regulatory requirement record capturing the specific regulatory standards and type-approval requirements that a vehicle or component must satisfy for a target market. Includes requirement ID, regulation name (FMVSS, ECE-R, CARB, Euro NCAP, WLTP, EPA), regulation number, market/region applicability, requirement description, compliance method (test, calculation, declaration), linked validation tests, compliance status, and submission deadline. Supports regulatory compliance and homologation engineering activities.'
}

The material_specification has compliance_reach and compliance_rohs attributes confirming regulatory dependency. Substance compliance ']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to external standards governing the material (e.g., SAE, ASTM, DIN).']
  application_restriction varchar [note: 'Business rules limiting where the material may be used (e.g., high‑temperature zones).']

  thermal_conductivity_w_per_mk decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rate at which heat passes through the material.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the specification for change tracking.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Manufacturer warranty period applicable to the material.']
  yield_strength_mpa decimal(10,2) [note: 'Stress at which material begins to deform plastically.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Engineering material specification record defining the approved materials for use in vehicle components. Captures spec ID, material name, material class (steel, aluminum, polymer, composite, glass), grade or alloy designation, mechanical properties (tensile strength, yield strength, elongation), thermal properties, density, surface treatment requirements, applicable standards (SAE, ASTM, DIN), REACH/RoHS compliance status, approved supplier list, and application restrictions. }

Table engineering.test_requirement_compliance {
  test_requirement_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the test_requirement_compliance association']
  homologation_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the homologation requirement being satisfied']
  validation_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the validation test that provides compliance evidence']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory authority approved this compliance evidence']
  approved_by_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or certification body that approved this compliance mapping (e.g., TÜV SÜD, NHTSA, CARB)']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes documenting how the test satisfies the requirement, any deviations, or conditional approval terms']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this test-requirement mapping in the approval lifecycle']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance mapping record was created in the system']
  evidence_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the formal compliance evidence document or test report submitted to authorities']
  mapped_by_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the homologation or validation engineer who created this compliance mapping']
  mapping_date date [note: 'Date when this test-to-requirement mapping was formally established']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when this compliance evidence was submitted to the regulatory authority']
  submission_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory submission package containing this compliance evidence']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compliance mapping record']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance evidence mapping between validation tests and homologation requirements in the type-approval process. It captures the formal traceability required by regulatory authorities (TÜV, NHTSA, JAMA) documenting which test results satisfy which regulatory requirements. Each record links one validation test execution to one homologation requirement with compliance status, submission tracking, and regulatory approval metadata that exist only in the context of this compliance relationship.. Existence Justification: In automotive homologation, validation tests and regulatory requirements have a genuine many-to-many operational relationship. A single validation test (e.g., a crash test) can provide compliance evidence for multiple regulatory requirements across different markets (FMVSS 208, Euro NCAP frontal impact, China C-NCAP). Conversely, a single regulatory requirement (e.g., CO2 emissions limit) must be satisfied by multiple test protocols (WLTP, EPA FTP-75, CARB LEV III). Type approval authorities require explicit documentation of these mappings with submission tracking and approval status.'
}

Table manufacturing.plant {
  plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each manufacturing plant or assembly facility. Primary key for the plant master record, referenced across MES, SAP PP, and logistics domains.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Each manufacturing plant operates under a legal entity (company code) for statutory financial reporting, intercompany transactions, and SAP FI/CO integration. Automotive OEMs always map plant-to-compa']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Plant regulatory reporting requires knowing the jurisdiction (country/region) governing emissions and safety permits.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the plant facility. Used for logistics, regulatory filings, and official correspondence. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the plant facility (e.g., building number, gate, or suite). Supplements address_line1 for complete facility location identification.']
  agv_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant is equipped with Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) systems for material transport on the shop floor. Used for automation capability classification and MES integration configuration.']
  annual_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicles or assemblies the plant is designed to produce per year under normal two-shift operations. Used for capacity planning, CapEx justification, and production scheduling in SAP PP.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the plant is physically located. Used for logistics planning, workforce commute analysis, and regulatory jurisdiction determination.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'The date on which the plant was officially commissioned and handed over for production operations. Distinct from SOP date; represents the physical readiness milestone. Used for asset lifecycle management and depreciation calculations in SAP AM.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP Controlling (CO) cost center code assigned to the plant for financial cost allocation, CapEx tracking, and R&D expenditure reporting. Used in management accounting and EBITDA analysis.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code indicating the country where the plant is physically located (e.g., \'USA\', \'DEU\', \'CHN\'). Used for regulatory compliance, CAFE reporting, and logistics routing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plant master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local currency used in plant-level financial reporting and cost accounting (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'CNY\'). Used in SAP FI/CO for cost center reporting and CapEx tracking.']
  daily_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicles or assemblies the plant can produce per day (Jobs Per Day - JPD). Derived from takt time and shift configuration. Used for daily production scheduling and MES throughput planning.']
  eop_date date [note: 'The official End of Production (EOP) date when the plant is scheduled to cease production of the current model or permanently close. Used for capacity planning, workforce transition, and asset disposal planning.']
  epa_facility_code varchar [note: 'EPA-assigned facility identifier for emissions and environmental compliance reporting. Required for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) reporting and Clean Air Act compliance.']
  floor_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total manufacturing floor area of the plant in square meters. Used for facility planning, CapEx allocation, insurance valuation, and ISO 14001 environmental footprint reporting.']
  iatf_16949_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant holds a current IATF 16949 automotive quality management system certification. Mandatory for OEM supplier qualification and customer-specific requirements compliance.']
  iso_14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant holds a current ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. Required for EPA compliance reporting and sustainability disclosures.']
  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant holds a current ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Required for supplier qualification and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the plant in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for geospatial analytics, logistics network optimization, and supply chain mapping.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the plant in decimal degrees (WGS 84). Used for geospatial analytics, logistics network optimization, and supply chain mapping.']
  manager varchar [note: 'Full name of the plant manager or general manager responsible for overall plant operations. Used for escalation routing, regulatory correspondence, and organizational reporting. Not classified as PII as this is a business role reference.']
  mes_plant_code varchar [note: 'The plant identifier as registered in the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system reconciliation between SAP PP production orders and MES shop floor execution records. Enables traceability from ERP to shop floor.']
  model_year_current varchar [note: 'The current Model Year (MY) of vehicles being produced at the plant (e.g., \'2025\'). Used for production scheduling, regulatory compliance submissions, and CAFE reporting alignment.']
  nhtsa_facility_code varchar [note: 'NHTSA-assigned facility identifier for the plant. Required for vehicle recall management, safety defect reporting, and Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards (FMVSS) compliance submissions.']
  number_of_assembly_lines boolean [note: 'Total count of active production or assembly lines within the plant. Used for capacity planning, MES line configuration, and production scheduling.']
  number_of_shifts boolean [note: 'Number of production shifts operated per day at the plant (typically 1, 2, or 3). Drives capacity calculations, workforce planning in SuccessFactors, and MES scheduling.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the plant. \'active\' indicates full production; \'idle\' indicates temporary suspension; \'ramp_up\' indicates pre-SOP commissioning phase; \'ramp_down\' indicates post-EOP wind-down; \'shutdown\' indicates planned or unplanned stoppage; \'decommissioned\' indicates permanently closed.']
  ota_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant has the infrastructure to perform OTA (Over-the-Air) software updates on vehicles during the final assembly or PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection) process. Relevant for connected vehicle production.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the plant facility. Used for operational communications, emergency contact, and supplier/logistics coordination. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the plant within SAP S/4HANA and MES systems (e.g., \'DETF01\', \'USNA02\'). Used as the business key across ERP, MES, and logistics integrations. Maps to SAP Organizational Unit - Plant Code.']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Official full name of the manufacturing plant or assembly facility (e.g., \'Detroit Final Assembly Plant No. 1\'). Used in reporting, regulatory submissions, and communications.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the plant\'s primary manufacturing function. Values include stamping (metal forming), body_shop (body-in-white welding), paint (paint application), final_assembly (vehicle assembly), ckd (Completely Knocked Down assembly), skd (Semi Knocked Down assembly), powertrain (engine/transmission), logistics (parts distribution). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint|final_assembly|ckd|skd|powertrain|logistics — promote to reference product]']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the plant location. Used for logistics routing, tax jurisdiction assignment, and regulatory reporting.']
  powertrain_types_produced varchar [note: 'Classification of powertrain technologies assembled or manufactured at this plant. ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), EV (Electric Vehicle), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle), or mixed. Used for EPA emissions compliance, CAFE reporting, and R&D investment tracking.']
  primary_vehicle_segment varchar [note: 'The primary vehicle segment produced at this plant. Values: car (passenger cars), truck (light/heavy trucks), suv (Sport Utility Vehicles), commercial_vehicle (vans/buses/heavy commercial), ev (Battery Electric Vehicle), hev (Hybrid Electric Vehicle - HEV), phev (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle - PHEV), mixed (multiple segments). Drives model year planning and regulatory compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: car|truck|suv|commercial_vehicle|ev|hev|phev|mixed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic or operational region in which the plant is located (e.g., \'North America\', \'Europe\', \'Asia Pacific\'). Used for regional production planning, capacity allocation, and management reporting.']
  scada_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant operates a SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system for real-time monitoring and control of manufacturing processes, utilities, and equipment.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or commonly used name for the plant used in dashboards, shop floor displays, and internal communications (e.g., \'Detroit FA1\').']
  sop_date date [note: 'The official Start of Production (SOP) date when the plant commenced or is scheduled to commence volume production. Critical milestone for capital planning, workforce ramp-up, and supplier readiness. Aligns with APQP phase-gate milestones.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative subdivision where the plant is located (e.g., \'Michigan\', \'Bavaria\', \'Ontario\'). Used for local regulatory compliance, tax jurisdiction, and workforce reporting.']
  takt_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The rate at which vehicles must be produced to meet customer demand, expressed in seconds per unit. Calculated as available production time divided by customer demand rate. Fundamental to JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling and line balancing in MES.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the plant location (e.g., \'America/Detroit\', \'Europe/Berlin\'). Used for MES shift scheduling, production timestamp normalization, and cross-plant reporting in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  union_represented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant workforce is represented by a labor union. Impacts workforce planning, shift scheduling, overtime policies, and collective bargaining agreement compliance in SuccessFactors.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plant master record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and the Databricks lakehouse, and audit compliance.']
  workforce_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of employees (direct and indirect labor) currently employed at the plant. Used for workforce planning, capacity analysis, and regulatory headcount reporting in SuccessFactors.']

  Note: 'Master record for each manufacturing plant or assembly facility operated by Automotive. Captures plant identity, location, type (stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly, CKD/SKD), production capacity, SOP/EOP dates, plant code, and operational status. SSOT for plant-level identity referenced across MES, SAP PP, and logistics domains.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_line {
  production_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each production line record within the manufacturing data platform. Serves as the primary key for the production_line entity. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line‑level cost allocation report requires linking each production line to its finance cost center; experts expect a cost_center_id instead of the denormalized code.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production line is physically located. Links to the plant master record for capacity planning, OEE reporting, and regulatory compliance reporting by facility.']
  sap_work_center_id varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA PP (Production Planning) work center identifier corresponding to this production line. Enables direct linkage between the lakehouse production_line master and SAP production orders, capacity planning, and cost center assignments in SAP CO (Controlling).']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Line scheduling and line‑to‑program assignment use vehicle_program_id to match line capabilities with program requirements.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element.
