Used to enforce JIS (Just-in-Sequence) ordering, production line scheduling, and WIP (Work in Progress) traceability. Lower values indicate earlier stages.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern under which this build stage operates: single (one shift per day), double (two shifts), triple (three shifts), or continuous (24/7 operation). Used for capacity planning, labor scheduling in SuccessFactors, and production throughput calculations.']
  sop_date date [note: 'The SOP (Start of Production) date on which this build stage was first commissioned and became operational for regular production. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, warranty baseline calculations, and production history reporting.']
  spc_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether SPC (Statistical Process Control) monitoring is active for this build stage. When true, process parameters are continuously monitored against control limits and deviations trigger automated alerts in the MES and SAP QM systems per IATF 16949 requirements.']
  stage_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the build stage within the manufacturing process (e.g., STAMP, BODY, ECOAT, PAINT, TRIM1, CHAS, TRIM2, FINAL, PDI). Used as the business key in MES (Manufacturing Execution System) integrations and production reporting.']
  stage_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the manufacturing activities, operations, and objectives performed at this build stage. Provides context for production engineers, quality auditors, and training documentation. Sourced from PLM and MES configuration documentation.']
  stage_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the build stage as used in production planning and reporting (e.g., Stamping, Body Shop, E-Coat, Paint, Trim-1, Chassis, Trim-2, Final Assembly, PDI — Pre-Delivery Inspection). Displayed in MES dashboards and production schedules.']
  stage_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the build stage definition: active (in production use), inactive (temporarily not in use), pilot (under validation/trial), suspended (halted due to quality or safety issue), or decommissioned (permanently retired from the process). Controls whether the stage is available for new production orders.']
  stage_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the build stage by its primary manufacturing function: assembly (joining components), fabrication (forming/stamping metal), coating (E-Coat/paint application), inspection (quality gate checks), testing (functional/NVH testing), or logistics (material staging/sequencing).']
  standard_cycle_time_sec decimal(10,2) [note: 'The engineered standard cycle time for this build stage measured in seconds. Represents the designed takt-aligned duration for one vehicle unit to complete the stage under normal operating conditions. Used for production scheduling, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation, and bottleneck analysis.']
  succeeding_stage_code varchar [note: 'Stage code of the immediately succeeding build stage in the standard production sequence. Defines the downstream handoff point for production flow control and vehicle build traceability. Null for the final stage (PDI) in the sequence.']
  takt_time_sec decimal(10,2) [note: 'The customer-demand-driven takt time for this build stage in seconds, calculated as available production time divided by required production volume. Defines the rhythm of production and is used to balance line capacity and identify over/under-capacity stages.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this build stage record. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines, audit trail maintenance, and engineering change management workflows per IATF 16949 documented information control.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA work center identifier associated with this build stage. Links the build stage to capacity planning, cost center allocation, and production order routing in the ERP system. Enables integration between MES stage execution and SAP PP/CO modules.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the standard build stages in the vehicle manufacturing process (e.g., Stamping, Body Shop, E-Coat, Paint, Trim-1, Chassis, Trim-2, Final, PDI). Captures stage code, stage name, sequence order, process area, standard cycle time, and applicable vehicle types. Used to structure vehicle_build traceability and production reporting.'
}

Table manufacturing.shop_floor_event {
  shop_floor_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shop floor event record generated by MES, PLC, or SCADA systems during production operations.']
  build_stage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_stage. Business justification: Shop floor events (MES/PLC/SCADA) occur at specific build stages (stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly). Linking shop_floor_event to build_stage enables stage-level OEE analysis, quality gate ev']
  characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.characteristic. Business justification: Shop floor events capture measured_value against control limits — these measurements are for specific quality characteristics. Linking to characteristic enables proper SPC data traceability and charac']
  defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect_record. Business justification: IATF 16949 MES traceability: shop floor events with quality_result=\'FAIL\' or fault_code directly generate defect records. Linking enables MES-to-quality defect traceability required for process audit ']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Quality gate shop floor events trigger inspection lots. MES quality gate events must reference the resulting inspection lot for in-process quality disposition traceability per IATF 16949 unit-level tr']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where the event occurred. Supports multi-plant OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Shop floor events are generated on specific production lines. The production_line_code STRING is a denormalized reference. Adding production_line_id FK enables line-level event aggregation for OEE cal']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PP production order under which this shop floor event was generated. Links the event to the scheduled manufacturing work order.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this event was recorded. Enables shift-level OEE and throughput analysis.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle unit being processed at the time of this event. Enables full build traceability from stamping through final assembly per NHTSA VIN requirements.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PP/MES work center (station, cell, or line segment) where this event occurred. Identifies the physical production resource.']
  agv_unit_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) unit involved in this event. Populated only for AGV arrival, departure, or fault events. Enables AGV fleet utilization and route efficiency analysis.']
  assembly_area varchar [note: 'Major manufacturing area where the event occurred. Supports area-level OEE reporting and quality gate management across stamping, body shop, paint, and final assembly. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint_shop|final_assembly|powertrain|chassis|trim — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shop floor event record was first ingested into the Databricks Silver layer. Distinct from event_timestamp (real-world event time). Used for data pipeline latency monitoring and audit trail.']
  event_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the event type for reporting and analytics. Enables aggregated OEE loss categorization (availability, performance, quality) and safety incident tracking.']
  event_duration_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Duration of the shop floor event in seconds (e.g., station cycle time, weld duration, paint cure time). Principal quantitative measurement for cycle time analysis and OEE performance rate calculation.']
  event_payload varchar [note: 'Structured JSON string containing the full event-specific payload from the MES, PLC, or SCADA system. Stores additional context-specific parameters not captured in dedicated columns (e.g., weld schedule parameters, paint recipe ID, torque angle). Stored as STRING to comply with Silver layer no-complex-type constraint.']
  event_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating control system that generated this event (MES, PLC, SCADA, AGV controller, or manual entry). Critical for data lineage and root-cause analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MES|PLC|SCADA|AGV|TPMS|OBD|MANUAL — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shop floor event record. Indicates whether the event has been acknowledged, confirmed by the operator, placed on hold, or escalated for quality review.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the shop floor event occurred as recorded by the MES, PLC, or SCADA system. This is the principal real-world event time used for OEE calculation and production sequencing.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Discriminator classifying the kind of shop floor event. Drives downstream routing, OEE calculation, and quality gate logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: station_entry|station_exit|torque_confirmation|weld_completion|paint_cure|agv_arrival|hold_trigger|alarm|inspection_pass|inspection_fail|rework_start|rework_complete — promote to reference product]']
  fault_code varchar [note: 'Machine or process fault code generated by the PLC or SCADA system when an alarm or hold event occurs. Used for root-cause analysis, FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) updates, and maintenance work order creation.']
  fault_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the fault or alarm condition associated with this event. Populated from the PLC/SCADA alarm dictionary. Null for non-alarm events.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for a production hold triggered by this event (e.g., QUAL-FAIL, TORQUE-OOC, WELD-MISS, PART-MISSING). Populated only for hold_trigger event types. Drives quality containment and corrective action workflows.']
  is_rework boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this event is associated with a rework operation (True) rather than a first-pass production operation (False). Used to calculate first-time quality (FTQ) and rework cost metrics.']
  is_within_spec boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value falls within the defined process control limits (True) or represents an out-of-specification condition (False). Drives automatic quality hold triggers and PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower statistical process control limit for the measured value at this station. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) to detect process drift and trigger quality holds.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Principal numeric measurement captured during the event (e.g., torque value in Nm, weld current in A, paint film thickness in µm, temperature in °C, pressure in bar). Null when the event type does not produce a measurement.']
  measured_value_unit varchar [note: 'Engineering unit of measure for the measured_value field (e.g., Nm for torque, A for weld current, µm for paint film thickness). Ensures dimensional correctness in SPC and quality analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Nm|A|V|bar|°C|µm|mm|kg|kN|rpm|% — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mes_event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique event reference number assigned by the Apriso/Dassault MES system. Used for cross-system traceability and audit correlation.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit model year (MY) of the vehicle being produced at the time of this event. Supports model-year-level production analytics and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  oee_loss_category varchar [note: 'Classifies this event\'s contribution to OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) loss: availability loss (unplanned downtime), performance loss (speed reduction), quality loss (defects/rework), or no_loss (normal production). Enables direct OEE waterfall analysis per ISO 22400.']
  planned_cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard planned cycle time in seconds for this process step as defined in the SAP PP routing. Compared against event_duration_seconds to calculate performance rate for OEE.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain technology type of the vehicle being produced (ICE – Internal Combustion Engine, HEV – Hybrid Electric Vehicle, PHEV – Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle, BEV – Battery Electric Vehicle, FCEV – Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives process routing differences and regulatory compliance checks.']
  process_step_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific manufacturing process step (e.g., STAMP-001, BODY-WELD-045, PAINT-CURE-010, ASSY-TORQUE-120) within the routing. Aligns with SAP PP routing operations.']
  program_number varchar [note: 'Version-controlled program number loaded on the PLC or robot controller at the time of this event. Critical for traceability of process parameter changes and engineering change management.']
  quality_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quality check associated with this event. Determines whether the vehicle unit proceeds to the next station, requires rework, or is scrapped. Feeds into IATF 16949 quality records and PPM reporting.']
  rework_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of rework attempts for this vehicle unit at this station. A value greater than 1 indicates repeated failures and triggers escalation per IATF 16949 nonconforming product procedures.']
  scrap_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for scrapping a component or vehicle unit at this event. Populated only when quality_result is \'scrap\'. Feeds into SAP CO scrap cost reporting and IATF 16949 quality records.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Production sequence number assigned to the vehicle unit in the JIS (Just-in-Sequence) build schedule. Used to validate correct part delivery sequencing and detect sequence deviations.']
  station_code varchar [note: 'Unique code of the specific work station or assembly point within the production line where the event was triggered. Enables station-level cycle time and downtime analysis.']
  station_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the work station (e.g., \'Underbody Weld Station 3\', \'E-Coat Oven Exit\', \'Engine Dress Final\'). Used in operator dashboards and quality reports.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this shop floor event record in the Silver layer (e.g., when quality result or hold status was updated post-event). Supports change data capture and audit compliance.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper statistical process control limit for the measured value at this station. Used in SPC (Statistical Process Control) to detect process drift and trigger quality holds.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the vehicle model or platform being assembled (e.g., F-150, RAV4, Model-3-LR). Enables model-mix production analysis and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) validation.']
  vin_number varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number of the vehicle unit being processed. Denormalized here for direct event-level traceability without requiring a join. Conforms to ISO 3779 and NHTSA 49 CFR Part 565.']

  Note: 'Granular transactional record capturing real-time shop floor events generated by MES, PLC, and SCADA systems during production. Includes event type (station entry, station exit, torque confirmation, weld completion, paint cure, AGV arrival, hold trigger, alarm), event timestamp, work center, production order reference, VIN reference, operator ID, and event payload. Enables real-time production monitoring and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_schedule {
  production_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the master production schedule (MPS) record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  budget_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_plan. Business justification: Production volume schedules are the operational input to financial budget plans (revenue, COGS, material spend). Automotive S&OP process explicitly links production schedule to financial budget; this ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Production schedules are aligned to fiscal periods for revenue recognition planning and financial forecasting. production_schedule.fiscal_period is a denormalized plain attribute; a proper FK enables ']
  model_year_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program. Business justification: Annual production schedules are structured around model year programs — freeze horizons, SOP/EOP dates, and regulatory market allocations are all model-year-program-driven. This FK enables production ']
  msrp_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.msrp_schedule. Business justification: Production planning teams validate scheduled variants against active MSRP schedules before freezing production plans — ensuring only market-priced configurations are scheduled. This supports the S&OP ']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where the scheduled production will occur. Corresponds to the SAP Plant organizational unit.']
  powertrain_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: Production schedules are planned at powertrain variant level for supplier capacity reservation, battery/engine procurement, and regulatory market allocation. production_schedule has powertrain_type as']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production line (e.g., final assembly, body shop, paint shop, stamping) within the plant where the scheduled build will take place.']
  production_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_variant. Business justification: A production schedule entry defines planned volumes for a specific production variant (model/option combination). Adding production_variant_id FK enables variant-level schedule planning, capacity allo']
  scheduling_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.scheduling_agreement. Business justification: Production schedules release call-offs against scheduling agreements. The scheduling agreement defines contractual delivery rhythm and volumes; the production schedule triggers actual releases. S&OP a']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Production scheduling in automotive MRP requires linking each schedule to the finished-goods SKU to trigger material requirements planning, safety stock checks, and supplier calloffs. A production pla']
  vehicle_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vehicle model (e.g., sedan, SUV, truck, EV) being scheduled for production. Links to the vehicle master product record.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Production schedules are generated per vehicle program; schedule analytics filter by vehicle_program_id.']
  actual_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual number of vehicles produced and confirmed as good units by the MES (Manufacturing Execution System) during the schedule period. Populated upon schedule completion from Apriso/Dassault MES.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the production planning manager who formally approved this schedule version for release to the shop floor. Required for IATF 16949 document control and audit trail.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the production schedule was formally approved for release. Marks the transition from \'draft\' to \'released\' status and triggers supplier call-off generation.']
  assembly_process_stage varchar [note: 'Manufacturing process stage covered by this schedule record. Reflects the major shop areas in automotive assembly: stamping (metal forming), body shop (welding/joining), paint (surface treatment), final assembly (powertrain/trim installation), or trim-chassis-final (TCF integrated line).']
  available_capacity_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total available production capacity in hours for this schedule period on the assigned production line, accounting for planned downtime, maintenance windows, and shift patterns.']
  body_style varchar [note: 'Body style classification of the vehicles scheduled for production. Used for production line configuration, tooling setup, and capacity planning by body type.']
  build_sequence_end varchar [note: 'Ending sequence number for the JIS (Just-in-Sequence) build order within this schedule period. Together with build_sequence_start defines the range of vehicle sequence numbers assigned to this schedule.']
  build_sequence_start varchar [note: 'Starting sequence number for the JIS (Just-in-Sequence) build order within this schedule period. Used by MES and AGV systems to sequence vehicle bodies through the production line in the correct order.']
  build_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production build type. \'standard\' = full series production; \'ckd\' = Completely Knocked Down (CKD) kit assembly; \'skd\' = Semi Knocked Down (SKD) assembly; \'pilot\' = pre-production pilot build; \'pre_series\' = pre-series validation build.']
  calloff_transmission_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when supplier delivery call-offs were transmitted to the supply base for this production schedule. Used for OTD (On-Time Delivery) tracking and supplier performance measurement.']
  capacity_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned capacity utilization percentage for this schedule period, calculated as (confirmed_quantity × takt_time) / available_capacity_hours × 100. Stored as a planning input from SAP PP capacity leveling, not a derived KPI.']
  confirmed_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles confirmed for production after capacity leveling, constraint resolution, and production planning approval. May differ from planned_quantity due to capacity constraints or material availability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production schedule record was first created in the system, either by an MRP run or manual entry. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance.']
  eop_date date [note: 'Official End of Production (EOP) date for the vehicle model/model year. Schedules beyond this date are invalid. Used for phase-out planning, last-time-buy procurement, and regulatory deregistration.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year (FY) to which this production schedule belongs. Used for financial planning, CapEx allocation, and EBITDA reporting alignment between production volumes and financial periods.']
  freeze_horizon_date date [note: 'Date up to which the production schedule is frozen and cannot be changed without formal change control. Protects supplier call-offs and JIT/JIS delivery commitments within the frozen window.']
  is_frozen boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this schedule record is currently within the freeze horizon and locked against changes. True = frozen (no modifications allowed without change control); False = open for revision.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this production schedule record. Updated on every revision, status change, or quantity adjustment. Essential for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The automotive model year (MY) designation for the vehicles being scheduled. Used for regulatory compliance (CAFE, EPA), BOM versioning, and sales planning. Typically one year ahead of the calendar year for SOP planning.']
  planned_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of vehicles planned for production in this schedule period as determined by the MRP run. Represents the gross requirement before capacity and constraint adjustments.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain technology classification for the vehicles in this schedule. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine; HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle; PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle; EV = Battery Electric Vehicle; FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Drives regulatory reporting (CAFE, EPA, CARB) and BOM variant selection.']
  regulatory_market varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the primary regulatory market for which these vehicles are being produced. Determines applicable homologation standards (NHTSA/FMVSS for USA, UNECE for Europe, etc.), emissions certification (EPA, CARB, Euro 6), and CAFE compliance tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|DEU|GBR|CHN|JPN|KOR|AUS|BRA — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  schedule_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason code or description for the most recent schedule revision. Examples: material shortage, capacity constraint, demand change, quality hold, tooling failure, model mix adjustment. Required for change control audit trail per IATF 16949.']
  schedule_date date [note: 'The principal planned production date (or start date of the weekly/monthly bucket) for this schedule record. Represents the real-world business event date when production is planned to occur.']
  schedule_end_date date [note: 'End date of the production schedule period. For daily schedules this equals schedule_date; for weekly/monthly buckets this marks the last day of the planning period.']
  schedule_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for production planners to record contextual notes, exceptions, or special instructions associated with this schedule record. Examples: tooling changeover notes, quality hold references, special customer order constraints.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the production schedule record, sourced from SAP PP (Production Planning). Used for cross-system reference and communication with supply chain and manufacturing teams.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the production schedule record. \'draft\' = initial planning; \'released\' = approved for execution; \'frozen\' = within freeze horizon, no changes allowed; \'in_execution\' = active shop floor production; \'completed\' = all planned units built; \'cancelled\' = schedule voided.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the production schedule record. \'daily\' = day-level MPS; \'weekly\' = weekly bucket plan; \'monthly\' = monthly capacity plan. Determines the planning horizon and supplier call-off frequency.']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of the production schedule. Each MRP (Material Requirements Planning) run or manual revision increments this counter, enabling version comparison and rollback analysis.']
  scheduled_end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when production is scheduled to complete for this schedule record. Used for shift planning, takt time validation, and downstream logistics coordination.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when production is scheduled to begin on the line for this schedule record. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) sequencing, AGV routing, and supplier call-off timing.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Shift configuration planned for this production schedule period. Determines available production hours and workforce requirements. Single = 1 shift/day; Double = 2 shifts/day; Triple = 3 shifts/day; Weekend = weekend-only production.']
  sop_date date [note: 'Official Start of Production (SOP) date for the vehicle model/model year being scheduled. Marks the formal launch of series production. Used to validate that schedule dates fall within the active production window.']
  supplier_calloff_generated boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether supplier delivery call-offs (JIT/JIS delivery schedules) have been generated and transmitted to suppliers based on this production schedule. True = call-offs sent; False = pending call-off generation.']
  takt_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Planned takt time in seconds per vehicle unit for this production schedule. Takt time = available production time divided by customer demand rate. Drives line balancing, AGV pacing, and JIS sequencing intervals.']

  Note: 'Master production schedule (MPS) record defining planned vehicle production volumes by plant, production line, model, MY (Model Year), and time period (daily, weekly, monthly). Captures scheduled quantity, confirmed quantity, schedule version, freeze horizon, schedule status, and MRP run reference. Integrates with SAP PP MRP and supports capacity planning and supplier call-off generation.'
}

Table manufacturing.shift {
  shift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each shift definition record in the manufacturing plant reference master.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant at which this shift definition applies. Used to scope shift schedules to specific production facilities.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production line (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly) to which this shift definition is assigned. Enables line-level OEE and scheduling.']
  agv_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Automated Guided Vehicle (AGV) operations are active during this shift. Used for shop floor automation scheduling and safety protocol management in MES and SCADA integrations.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total scheduled break time in minutes for the shift (e.g., 30 minutes for a single meal break, 50 minutes for two breaks). Used to reconcile gross shift hours against planned production hours.']
  break_schedule varchar [note: 'Textual description of the break schedule for the shift, including break times and durations (e.g., \'10:00–10:15 AM rest break; 12:30–13:00 PM meal break\'). Supports workforce planning and ergonomic compliance documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift definition record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and IATF 16949 document control compliance.']
  day_of_week_mask varchar [note: 'Seven-character binary string (Mon–Sun) indicating which days of the week this shift runs (e.g., \'1111100\' = Monday to Friday, \'0000011\' = Saturday and Sunday). Used by MES and SAP PP for automated shift calendar generation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Calendar date from which this shift definition becomes valid and available for production scheduling. Supports versioned shift master management and seasonal schedule changes.']
  energy_consumption_target_kwh decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned energy consumption target in kilowatt-hours for the shift. Used for energy efficiency monitoring, ISO 14001 environmental reporting, and CAFE/EPA compliance cost tracking.']
  eop_date date [note: 'End of Production (EOP) date associated with this shift definition, indicating when the shift schedule is retired for a vehicle programme. Null if no planned end. Used for production wind-down planning.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Calendar date after which this shift definition is no longer valid for production scheduling. Null indicates an open-ended shift definition with no planned end. Supports model year changeovers and SOP/EOP transitions.']
  gross_shift_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total clock hours from shift start to shift end including all breaks and non-production time (e.g., 8.0 hours). Used to distinguish gross capacity from net available production time.']
  handover_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned duration in minutes allocated for shift handover activities between outgoing and incoming crews (e.g., 15 minutes). Included in shift scheduling to ensure continuity of production and quality checks.']
  is_template boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift record is a reusable template definition (True) rather than a plant/line-specific instantiation (False). Templates are used to standardise shift configurations across multiple plants and lines.']
  mes_shift_code varchar [note: 'Native shift identifier in the Apriso/Dassault MES system used for shop floor execution and traceability. Enables cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the MES source of record.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Automotive model year (MY) for which this shift definition is applicable (e.g., 2025). Aligns shift scheduling with SOP (Start of Production) and EOP (End of Production) milestones for specific vehicle programmes.']
  number_of_breaks varchar [note: 'Count of distinct scheduled break periods within the shift (e.g., 2 for a morning rest break and a meal break). Used in ergonomic compliance reporting and workforce scheduling.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OEE percentage for this shift, representing the combined availability, performance, and quality targets (e.g., 85.00%). Used as the benchmark for shift-level OEE reporting and continuous improvement initiatives.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift definition is classified as an overtime shift, triggering premium pay calculations and overtime reporting in SAP HR and SuccessFactors payroll.']
  pattern_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the rotating shift pattern or schedule template to which this shift belongs (e.g., 3SHIFT_ROTATING, 2SHIFT_FIXED, CONTINENTAL). Used for workforce rostering and long-term capacity planning.']
  planned_end_time timestamp [note: 'Planned clock time at which the shift ends production activities. Combined with planned_start_time to derive gross shift duration for capacity and OEE calculations.']
  planned_production_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net planned production hours for the shift after deducting all scheduled breaks and non-production time (e.g., 7.5 hours for an 8-hour shift with 30-minute break). Core input for OEE denominator and capacity planning in SAP PP.']
  planned_start_time timestamp [note: 'Planned clock time at which the shift begins production activities (e.g., 2024-01-15T06:00:00.000+00:00). Used for production order scheduling, JIS sequencing, and workforce rostering.']
  premium_pay_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Pay rate multiplier applied to base wages for this shift (e.g., 1.5000 for time-and-a-half overtime, 1.2500 for night shift differential). Used in payroll cost calculations and labour cost reporting. Classified confidential as it contains compensation data.']
  production_model varchar [note: 'Shift operating model classification indicating the overall production schedule structure at the plant (e.g., single_shift, two_shift, three_shift, four_shift_continental). Drives capacity planning and workforce rostering strategy.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a mandatory quality inspection (e.g., PDI, SPC check, FMEA-driven inspection point) is required at the start or end of this shift. Drives SAP QM inspection lot creation and MES quality gate activation.']
  sap_shift_code varchar [note: 'Shift identifier as defined in SAP S/4HANA PP module (Schicht). Used for production order scheduling, capacity planning, and cross-system reconciliation with the ERP source of record.']
  scada_zone_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the SCADA monitoring zone associated with this shift definition. Used to correlate shift-level production data with real-time SCADA telemetry for process control and quality monitoring.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the shift within a 24-hour production cycle at the plant/line (e.g., 1 = first shift, 2 = second shift, 3 = third shift). Used for JIS sequencing and handover management.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the shift definition within the plant (e.g., DAY1, AFT2, NGT3, WKD1). Used as the business key in SAP PP and MES integrations.']
  shift_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the shift definition, including special operating conditions, seasonal variations, or programme-specific notes (e.g., \'Reduced capacity shift during model year changeover\').']
  shift_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the shift (e.g., Day Shift, Afternoon Shift, Night Shift, Weekend Shift). Used in production scheduling dashboards, workforce planning reports, and OEE calculations.']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift definition. Active shifts are available for production order scheduling; inactive or suspended shifts are excluded from planning runs.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift by its operational period. Drives workforce planning, premium pay calculations, and production capacity modelling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: day|afternoon|night|weekend|overtime|shutdown — promote to reference product if additional types are required]']
  sop_date date [note: 'Start of Production (SOP) date associated with this shift definition, indicating when the shift schedule becomes operational for a new vehicle programme or model year. Used for production ramp-up planning.']
  supervisor_role_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the required supervisor role classification for this shift (e.g., PROD_SUPV, AREA_LEAD). Used for workforce planning and shift handover accountability. References the HR role catalogue in SuccessFactors.']
  target_takt_time_seconds decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned takt time in seconds per unit for this shift, calculated as net available production time divided by customer demand rate. Fundamental metric for line balancing and JIS sequencing in automotive assembly.']
  target_units_per_shift varchar [note: 'Planned number of vehicle units (or assemblies) to be produced during this shift. Used as the production target for OEE performance ratio calculation and daily production reporting.']
  timezone_code varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the plant location where this shift operates (e.g., America/Detroit, Europe/Berlin). Ensures accurate timestamp interpretation for global multi-plant production scheduling and reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shift definition record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Lakehouse silver layer.']
  workforce_headcount_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of direct production workers required for this shift. Used for workforce planning, labour cost budgeting, and integration with SuccessFactors workforce scheduling.']

  Note: 'Reference master for production shift definitions at each plant and production line. Captures shift code, shift name (Day, Afternoon, Night, Weekend), planned start and end times, break schedule, planned production hours, and applicable plant and line assignments. Used for production order scheduling, OEE calculation, and workforce planning integration.'
}

Table manufacturing.downtime_event {
  downtime_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each downtime event record in the manufacturing execution system. Primary key for the downtime_event data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: OEE-related downtime costs (lost production, emergency maintenance labor) are allocated to cost centers for manufacturing overhead variance analysis and plant P&L reporting. Automotive plant controlle']
  defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.defect_record. Business justification: Downtime events with is_quality_hold_triggered=true reference a defect record. Linking enables root cause analysis connecting equipment failure to quality impact, supporting OEE and quality KPI report']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Supplier-caused downtime events must be traceable to the specific inbound part for supplier corrective action requests, scorecard impact calculation, and warranty cost recovery. This is a standard aut']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where the downtime event occurred. Corresponds to the SAP Plant (Werk) organizational unit.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production line (e.g., stamping line, body shop line, paint line, final assembly line) affected by the downtime event. Enables line-level OEE and throughput impact analysis.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP PP Production Order (Fertigungsauftrag) that was active or impacted at the time of the downtime event. Used to quantify production order impact and schedule deviation.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.shift. Business justification: Downtime events occur during a specific production shift. The shift_code STRING column is a denormalized reference to the shift master. Replacing it with shift_id FK enables proper shift-level downtim']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific work center or production cell where the downtime event was recorded. Maps to SAP PP Work Center (Arbeitsplatz) or MES work station definition. Critical for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis at the work center level.']
  affected_process_area varchar [note: 'The manufacturing process area impacted by the downtime event. Reflects the major shop segments in automotive assembly: stamping (press shop), body shop (BIW - Body in White), paint shop, final assembly, powertrain assembly, chassis, trim, or general assembly. Used for plant-level downtime distribution analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: stamping|body_shop|paint|final_assembly|powertrain|chassis|trim|general_assembly — promote to reference product]']
  corrective_action_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code from the SAP PM activity type catalog describing the corrective action taken to resolve the downtime event (e.g., part replacement, lubrication, calibration, software reset, tooling adjustment). Supports FMEA corrective action tracking and spare parts consumption analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the downtime event record was first created in the source system (MES, SAP PM, or SCADA). Represents the audit creation time, distinct from start_timestamp which is the actual business event time. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the maintenance_cost_local field (e.g., USD, EUR, MXN, CNY). Required for multi-plant, multi-currency cost consolidation in global automotive manufacturing operations.']
  downtime_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the downtime event type. \'mechanical_failure\' covers equipment breakdowns; \'tooling_change\' covers die/tool swap activities; \'material_shortage\' covers JIT/JIS supply disruptions; \'quality_hold\' covers production stops due to quality issues; \'scheduled_maintenance\' covers planned PM activities; \'changeover\' covers model or variant changeover activities. Drives OEE availability loss categorization.']
  downtime_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime event was planned (scheduled maintenance, changeover, tooling change per production schedule) or unplanned (unexpected breakdown, quality hold, material shortage). Critical for separating planned vs. unplanned OEE availability losses.']
  duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed duration of the downtime event in minutes, calculated as the difference between end_timestamp and start_timestamp. Stored as a business field for direct use in OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) availability calculations and SCADA-integrated downtime reporting. Null if the event has not yet ended.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the downtime event ended and production resumed, as recorded by the MES or SCADA system. Null if the event is still active. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the management escalation level reached during the downtime event based on duration thresholds or safety/quality triggers. \'none\' = resolved at operator level; \'supervisor\' = shift supervisor notified; \'manager\' = production manager engaged; \'plant_director\' = plant director escalation triggered. Supports escalation SLA compliance reporting.']
  event_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the downtime event started. Derived from start_timestamp for day-level partitioning, reporting, and trend analysis. Stored as a separate DATE field to support efficient date-range queries and daily OEE dashboard aggregations. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the downtime event as entered by the operator, technician, or supervisor in the MES or SAP PM notification. Captures contextual details not covered by structured codes, such as observed symptoms, environmental conditions, or operator observations at the time of failure.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the downtime event, typically generated by the MES (Manufacturing Execution System) or SAP PM (Plant Maintenance) module. Format: DT-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used for cross-system referencing and shop floor communication.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the downtime event record. \'open\' indicates the event is active and unresolved; \'in_progress\' indicates a repair or corrective action is underway; \'resolved\' indicates the root cause has been addressed; \'closed\' indicates the event is fully documented and closed; \'cancelled\' indicates the event was logged in error.']
  failure_mode_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the specific failure mode that caused the downtime event, aligned with the FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) failure mode library. Examples: electrical fault, hydraulic leak, sensor failure, conveyor jam. Enables FMEA-driven root cause trending and PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) corrective action tracking.']
  fault_location_code varchar [note: 'SAP PM object part / location code identifying the specific component or sub-assembly on the equipment where the fault occurred (e.g., spindle, conveyor belt, welding gun tip, paint nozzle). Enables component-level failure frequency analysis and spare parts demand forecasting.']
  is_quality_hold_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime event triggered a quality hold on WIP (Work in Progress) or finished vehicle inventory. True = quality hold was activated; False = no quality hold. Links to SAP QM quality notification and SPC (Statistical Process Control) out-of-control action plans.']
  is_repeat_failure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this downtime event is a recurrence of a previously recorded failure on the same equipment or work center within a defined lookback period (typically 30 days). True = repeat failure; False = first occurrence. Used to identify chronic equipment issues requiring escalated corrective action under IATF 16949 Section 10.2.']
  is_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime event involved a safety incident, near-miss, or safety-critical equipment failure (e.g., AGV collision, robot safety stop, lockout/tagout activation). True = safety-related; False = non-safety. Triggers mandatory safety reporting and OSHA/DOT compliance escalation workflows.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the downtime event record was most recently modified in the source system, such as when resolution notes were added, status was updated, or cost data was posted. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail in the Silver layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  maintenance_cost_local decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total direct maintenance cost incurred to resolve the downtime event, including labor, spare parts, and external service costs, expressed in the plant\'s local currency. Sourced from SAP PM actual cost settlement. Used for cost-of-downtime analysis and CapEx vs. OpEx maintenance budget reporting.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The vehicle model year (MY) of the production program running on the affected line at the time of the downtime event. Used to correlate downtime patterns with specific model year launches, SOP (Start of Production) ramp-up periods, and EOP (End of Production) transitions.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'The SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) notification number (Meldungsnummer) created in SAP S/4HANA to formally record the maintenance request or breakdown notification associated with this downtime event. Enables traceability between the MES downtime record and the SAP PM work order lifecycle.']
  oee_availability_loss_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of scheduled production time lost due to this downtime event, calculated as (duration_minutes / scheduled_production_time_minutes) × 100. Represents the availability loss component of the OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) metric for the affected work center during the event period. Stored as a business field for direct use in OEE dashboards.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'The powertrain type of the vehicle being produced at the time of the downtime event. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine, HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle, PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle, EV = Electric Vehicle, FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Used to analyze downtime patterns by powertrain technology, particularly relevant for EV/PHEV line ramp-up monitoring.']
  production_loss_units varchar [note: 'Number of vehicle units or production units lost due to the downtime event, based on the planned production rate for the affected work center. Used to quantify the production order impact and schedule recovery requirements.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing the maintenance technician\'s description of the actions taken to resolve the downtime event and restore production. Complements corrective_action_code with detailed narrative. Stored in the Silver layer for use in AI/ML-based predictive maintenance model training.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the confirmed root cause of the downtime event after investigation, aligned with the SAP PM cause code catalog. Distinct from failure_mode_code which captures the symptom; root_cause_code captures the underlying reason (e.g., inadequate lubrication, worn tooling, incorrect setup, supplier part defect).']
  scada_alarm_code varchar [note: 'The alarm or fault code generated by the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) or PLC (Programmable Logic Controller) system at the time the downtime event was triggered. Provides the raw machine-level signal that initiated the downtime record. Used for SCADA-integrated downtime reporting and automated event correlation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which the downtime event was originated or captured. MES = Apriso/Dassault Manufacturing Execution System (automated); SAP_PM = SAP Plant Maintenance notification (manual or integrated); SCADA = SCADA system automated alarm; PLC = PLC-generated fault signal; MANUAL = manually entered by operator. Used for data lineage and source system reconciliation in the Silver layer.']
  spare_parts_consumed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether spare parts or materials were consumed from inventory to resolve the downtime event. True = spare parts were issued via SAP MM goods issue; False = no parts consumed (e.g., adjustment or reset only). Drives spare parts demand planning and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) replenishment.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the downtime event began, as recorded by the MES or SCADA system. This is the principal real-world event time used for duration calculation and shift-level reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  time_to_repair_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elapsed time in minutes from when the maintenance team began active repair work to when the equipment was restored to operational status. Distinct from duration_minutes (total downtime) and time_to_respond_minutes (response lag). Core component of MTTR (Mean Time to Repair) KPI calculation.']
  time_to_respond_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Elapsed time in minutes between the downtime event start_timestamp and the time the responsible maintenance or production team acknowledged and began responding to the event. Key metric for maintenance response SLA (Service Level Agreement) compliance and MTTR (Mean Time to Repair) component analysis.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'The internal vehicle model or platform code (e.g., F-150, Silverado, Model 3 equivalent) being produced on the affected line at the time of the downtime event. Enables model-specific downtime analysis and supports PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) integration.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'The SAP PM maintenance work order number (Auftragsnummer) issued to address the downtime event. Distinct from the production order; this is the corrective or preventive maintenance order raised by the maintenance team in SAP PM.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing unplanned and planned production downtime events at the work center or line level. Tracks downtime start and end timestamps, duration (minutes), downtime category (mechanical failure, tooling change, material shortage, quality hold, scheduled maintenance, changeover), affected work center, production order impact, and responsible team. Critical for OEE analysis and SCADA-integrated downtime reporting.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_bom {
  production_bom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the manufacturing Bill of Materials (mBOM) record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.']
  apqp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: Production BOMs are released through APQP gates; ppap_status and apqp_phase on production_bom require traceability to the governing APQP plan. IATF 16949 APQP requires BOM-to-APQP-plan linkage for des']
  assembly_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: The production BOM header references the top-level assembly part (finished vehicle or major subassembly) in part_master — standard SAP MBOM pattern. Required for cost rollup, PPAP submission, and engi']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this manufacturing BOM (mBOM) to the originating engineering BOM (eBOM) in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or PTC Windchill. Enables traceability from shop floor execution back to the engineering design intent. The eBOM-to-mBOM transformation is managed via the PLM-SAP integration.']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Production BOMs must reflect homologation-approved component specifications for regulatory markets. Parts substitution or BOM changes require homologation validation. Real business process: homologati']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Production BOM must reference the approved inbound part to support supplier calloff generation, PPAP validation against BOM, and sourcing coverage checks during APQP. Manufacturing engineers and suppl']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: BOM Management Report requires each BOM to be tied to a vehicle model to ensure correct component list per model.']
  model_year_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program. Business justification: Production BOMs are versioned and effectivity-dated by model year program. APQP phase gates and PPAP submissions are organized per model year program. This FK enables BOM effectivity management and PP']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: A manufacturing BOM is plant-specific — the same vehicle model may have different production BOMs at different plants due to local tooling, supplier sourcing, or process differences. The plant_code ST']
  powertrain_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: Production BOMs are powertrain-variant-specific — EV, PHEV, and ICE variants require fundamentally different BOMs. PPAP and APQP processes require BOM validation against the certified powertrain_varia']
  primary_production_ebom_reference_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this manufacturing BOM (mBOM) to the originating engineering BOM (eBOM) in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or PTC Windchill. Enables traceability from shop floor execution back to the engineering design intent. The eBOM-to-mBOM transformation is managed via the PLM-SAP integration.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: A production BOM is specific to a production line (e.g., a stamping line BOM differs from a final assembly BOM). The production_line_code STRING is a denormalized reference. Adding production_line_id ']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.routing. Business justification: A production BOM is associated with a specific routing that defines the sequence of operations for manufacturing. The routing_number STRING is a denormalized reference. Adding routing_id FK normalizes']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Production BOMs define the finished assembly material number used for inventory valuation and MRP. material_number is a denormalized SKU reference. This FK is fundamental to SAP PP-MM integration for ']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this manufacturing BOM received formal approval from the designated authority. Precedes the release_date in the BOM lifecycle workflow. Required for IATF 16949 documented information control.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or employee identifier of the approving authority (e.g., Chief Engineer, Manufacturing Manager) who approved this BOM for production release. Distinct from released_by — approval may be a separate workflow step in Teamcenter PLM or SAP workflow.']
  apqp_phase varchar [note: 'Current APQP phase associated with this BOM\'s program lifecycle. Phase 1=Plan and Define, Phase 2=Product Design and Development, Phase 3=Process Design and Development, Phase 4=Product and Process Validation, Phase 5=Launch/Feedback. Tracks BOM maturity relative to the program development gate.']
  assembly_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle is assembled as a Completely Built Unit (CBU), Completely Knocked Down (CKD) kit for overseas assembly, or Semi Knocked Down (SKD) kit. Determines the BOM structure, component packaging, and applicable customs/import regulations for the target market.']
  base_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The base quantity of the header material for which the BOM component quantities are defined (e.g., 1 vehicle). All component quantities in the BOM are expressed relative to this base quantity. Corresponds to the SAP BOM header base quantity field.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the BOM base quantity (e.g., \'EA\' for each/vehicle, \'PC\' for piece, \'SET\' for set). Corresponds to the SAP material master base unit of measure. Ensures consistent quantity calculations during MRP explosion and production order processing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|L|M|M2|M3|SET — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  bom_alternative varchar [note: 'SAP BOM alternative identifier (e.g., \'01\', \'02\') distinguishing multiple valid BOM configurations for the same material and plant. Supports variant BOMs for different production lines or regional configurations (e.g., LHD vs RHD, CKD vs SKD).']
  bom_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the manufacturing BOM, typically including the vehicle model, model year, plant, and key configuration attributes (e.g., \'2025 F-150 XLT 3.5L EcoBoost AWD – Detroit Assembly Plant – MY2025 Production BOM\'). Used for display in SAP, Teamcenter PLM, and reporting tools.']
  bom_level varchar [note: 'The maximum depth/level of the BOM hierarchy for this manufacturing BOM (e.g., 1 = single-level, 5 = five levels deep). Used to understand BOM complexity and to control multi-level BOM explosion in MRP and production scheduling.']
  bom_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the manufacturing BOM. \'draft\' indicates the BOM is under engineering review. \'released\' means it has been authorized for production use. \'frozen\' indicates no further changes are permitted (e.g., during active production run). \'superseded\' means a newer BOM version has replaced it. \'obsolete\' means the BOM is no longer valid.']
  bom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM by its intended use in the manufacturing process. \'production\' is the standard mBOM used on the shop floor. \'phantom\' represents sub-assemblies that are not stocked. \'sales\' is used for variant configuration. \'spare_parts\' is for after-sales service. \'co_product\' is for by-products in manufacturing.']
  bom_usage varchar [note: 'SAP BOM usage indicator defining the organizational context in which the BOM is applied. \'production\' (SAP usage 1) is the standard for shop floor manufacturing. \'engineering\' (SAP usage 2) is for design/development. \'universal\' (SAP usage 3) applies across all usages. Determines which BOM is selected during MRP and production order processing.']
  bom_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the manufacturing BOM (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.3\'). Incremented each time the BOM is revised through an approved Engineering Change Order. Enables version history tracking and rollback capability for quality investigations.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the business reason for creating or modifying this BOM version (e.g., \'Cost reduction – substitute supplier for part X\', \'Regulatory compliance – CARB Tier 3 emission update\', \'Quality improvement – NVH fix per TSB-2024-001\'). Required for IATF 16949 change control documentation.']
  component_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct component line items (BOM positions) in this manufacturing BOM. Provides a quick indicator of BOM complexity and is used for completeness validation during BOM release. Does not include phantom sub-assembly components.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this manufacturing BOM record was first created in the source system (SAP PP or Teamcenter PLM). Represents the business event of BOM creation, not the ETL load time. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.']
  eco_number varchar [note: 'The Engineering Change Order (ECO) number that authorized the creation or last modification of this manufacturing BOM. Provides full traceability from the shop floor BOM back to the formal engineering change management process in Teamcenter PLM. Required for IATF 16949 change control compliance.']
  effectivity_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this manufacturing BOM version is no longer valid for production use. Corresponds to the SAP BOM \'Valid To\' date. Null indicates the BOM is open-ended with no planned expiry. Used to manage BOM transitions during model year changeovers and End of Production (EOP).']
  effectivity_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this manufacturing BOM version becomes effective and valid for production use. Corresponds to the SAP BOM \'Valid From\' date. Used for date-effective BOM explosion in MRP and production order creation. Aligns with Start of Production (SOP) planning.']
  eop_date date [note: 'The planned or actual End of Production (EOP) date for the vehicle program associated with this BOM. Marks the milestone when serial production of this vehicle configuration ceases. Used for last-time-buy planning, spare parts provisioning, and regulatory compliance archiving.']
  homologation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the regulatory homologation (type approval) for the vehicle configuration defined by this BOM in the target regulatory market. \'approved\' is required before vehicles built to this BOM can be legally sold in the target market. Managed through the Regulatory Compliance and Homologation process.']
  is_configurable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this BOM uses SAP Variant Configuration (VC) to support multiple vehicle variants from a single configurable BOM structure. True = configurable BOM with characteristic-based component selection. False = fixed BOM for a single specific vehicle configuration.']
  is_phantom_assembly boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the header material of this BOM is a phantom assembly — a logical grouping of components that is not physically stocked or produced as a discrete unit. Phantom assemblies are \'exploded through\' during MRP and production order processing. Common for sub-assemblies in JIS manufacturing.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this manufacturing BOM record in the source system. Used to detect changes during incremental data loads into the Databricks Silver Layer and to support BOM change history auditing required by IATF 16949.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The four-digit automotive Model Year (MY) for which this manufacturing BOM is valid (e.g., 2025). Determines the applicable regulatory standards, feature content, and homologation requirements. Aligns with NHTSA and EPA model year definitions.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle powertrain technology for which this BOM is defined. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine, HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle, PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle, BEV = Battery Electric Vehicle, FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Determines applicable emissions regulations (EPA, CARB, WLTP) and BOM component sets.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) for the components in this BOM. PPAP is the AIAG/IATF 16949 process that verifies supplier components meet design and specification requirements before production launch. \'approved\' is required before the BOM can be released to production.']
  predecessor_bom_number varchar [note: 'BOM number of the previous manufacturing BOM version that this BOM supersedes. Enables backward navigation in the BOM version chain for change impact analysis and quality investigations. Null for the initial BOM version.']
  regulatory_market_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the target regulatory market for which this BOM is homologated (e.g., \'US-FEDERAL\', \'US-CARB\', \'EU-EURO6\', \'JP-JC08\', \'CN-CHINA6\'). Determines applicable emissions standards (EPA, CARB, Euro 6, WLTP), safety regulations (NHTSA FMVSS, UNECE), and CAFE compliance requirements.']
  release_date date [note: 'The date on which this manufacturing BOM was formally released to production (status changed to \'released\'). Marks the point at which the BOM is authorized for use in production orders and MRP planning. Corresponds to the SAP BOM release date.']
  released_by varchar [note: 'User ID or employee identifier of the manufacturing engineer or release authority who formally released this BOM to production in SAP PP or Teamcenter PLM. Required for audit trail and change control compliance under IATF 16949.']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric revision level of the BOM (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'C\' or \'01\', \'02\'). Used in conjunction with bom_version to track incremental changes within a version. Aligns with SAP BOM revision level and Teamcenter PLM revision management.']
  sop_date date [note: 'The planned or actual Start of Production (SOP) date for the vehicle program associated with this BOM. Marks the milestone when serial production begins using this BOM configuration. Critical for program management, supplier readiness, and PPAP completion tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this manufacturing BOM record was sourced (e.g., \'SAP_PP\' for SAP Production Planning, \'TEAMCENTER\' for Siemens Teamcenter PLM). Required for data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and for resolving conflicts during BOM synchronization between PLM and ERP.']
  superseded_by_bom_number varchar [note: 'BOM number of the newer manufacturing BOM that has replaced this one (populated when bom_status = \'superseded\'). Enables forward navigation in the BOM version chain for traceability and historical analysis. Null when this is the current active BOM.']
  vehicle_configuration_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific vehicle configuration (variant) for which this BOM is defined, including body style, drivetrain, trim level, and market variant (e.g., \'SUV-AWD-PREMIUM-NA\'). Used in SAP Variant Configuration (VC) to select the correct BOM components for a specific vehicle order.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'Internal OEM code identifying the vehicle model (nameplate) for which this mBOM is defined (e.g., \'F150\', \'RAV4\', \'MODEL3\'). Used to associate the BOM with the correct vehicle program in PLM and SAP.']

  Note: 'Manufacturing Bill of Materials (BOM) record representing the production-level BOM used on the shop floor for a specific vehicle configuration and model year. Distinct from the engineering BOM (eBOM) in the engineering domain — this is the manufacturing BOM (mBOM) as released to production. Captures BOM header (vehicle model, MY, plant, effectivity dates), BOM status, and reference to the engineering change order that authorized it. Managed via SAP PP and Teamcenter PLM BOM transfer.'
}

Table manufacturing.bom_component {
  bom_component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for a single line-item record within a manufacturing Bill of Materials (BOM). Serves as the primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.characteristic. Business justification: Each BOM component has associated quality characteristics (dimensional, functional, safety) per IATF 16949. Linking component to characteristic enables component-level quality control planning and sup']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Each BOM component maps to a specific supplier inbound part for MRP calloff generation, PPAP status tracking per component, and supplier corrective action traceability. supplier_part_number is a denor']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Component Specification Process tracks components per vehicle model, enabling model‑specific quality and cost analysis.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: BOM components need a direct link to part_master for detailed specs; part_number/description are denormalized and will be removed.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or assembly facility where this BOM component is consumed. Determines plant-specific BOM validity and material availability.']
  production_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent BOM header to which this component line-item belongs. Links the component to its governing vehicle or sub-assembly BOM structure.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Each BOM component must map to an inventory SKU for procurement planning, backflushing, and MRP explosion. This is a foundational link in automotive manufacturing — component-level inventory managemen']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: BOM components are staged at specific lineside supply areas / storage locations for JIT/JIS delivery to assembly stations. supply_area is a denormalized storage location reference. This FK enables lin']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shop floor work center (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly station) where this component is installed or consumed during vehicle build.']
  alternate_part_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the alternate item group to which this component belongs in the BOM. Components within the same alternate group are interchangeable. Used in SAP BOM alternative item management for production flexibility.']
  alternate_part_number varchar [note: 'OEM part number of an approved alternate or substitute component that can be used in place of the primary part when the primary is unavailable. Supports supply chain continuity and JIT material substitution.']
  backflush_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is backflushed (automatically goods-issued upon production order confirmation) rather than requiring a discrete goods movement transaction. True = backflush; False = manual goods issue required. Common for bulk and low-value components.']
  bom_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical depth of this component within the multi-level BOM structure. Level 0 is the finished vehicle; level 1 is a major assembly; deeper levels represent sub-assemblies and individual parts. Used for BOM explosion and cost rollup.']
  bulk_material_indicator boolean [note: 'Flags this component as a bulk material (e.g., adhesives, lubricants, fasteners) that is issued to the production order without discrete picking or goods movement. Bulk materials are typically excluded from MRP individual requirements.']
  co_product_indicator boolean [note: 'Flags this BOM item as a co-product or by-product that is produced alongside the primary output (e.g., stamping offcuts, recycled materials). Co-products receive a portion of the production order cost allocation.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Procurement commodity classification code grouping this component into a spend category (e.g., \'ELEC-ECU\', \'BODY-STAMPING\', \'POWERTRAIN-EV-BATTERY\'). Used in supply chain management (SCM), RFQ processes, and supplier performance reporting.']
  component_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this BOM component line. \'superseded\' indicates the part has been replaced by an alternate; \'pending_approval\' indicates the component is awaiting PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) sign-off before use in production.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM item indicating how it is sourced and consumed in the manufacturing process. \'phantom\' denotes a logical grouping with no physical stock; \'bulk_material\' denotes items issued without discrete picking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: raw_material|purchased_part|sub_assembly|phantom|co_product|bulk_material — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this BOM component record was first created in the source system (SAP or Teamcenter PLM). Provides audit trail for BOM change history and regulatory traceability.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard cost value (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'JPY\'). Enables multi-currency BOM cost analysis across global manufacturing plants.']
  drawing_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAD/CAE engineering drawing or 3D model document number associated with this component, as managed in CATIA/ENOVIA or PTC Windchill. Enables traceability from the manufacturing BOM back to the design definition.']
  drawing_revision varchar [note: 'Revision level of the engineering drawing or CAD model applicable to this BOM component (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'01\'). Ensures the correct design revision is used in production and supports PPAP documentation.']
  effectivity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this BOM component line is no longer valid and should be excluded from production builds. Null indicates an open-ended validity. Supports EOP (End of Production) transitions and engineering change cutover.']
  effectivity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this BOM component line is valid and should be included in production builds. Supports engineering change management and model year (MY) transitions. Components before this date are not called off.']
  engineering_change_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that introduced or last modified this BOM component line. Provides full traceability of design changes from PLM through to the manufacturing BOM.']
  fmea_reference varchar [note: 'Document reference number for the Design FMEA (DFMEA) or Process FMEA (PFMEA) associated with this component. Supports APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) traceability and IATF 16949 risk management requirements.']
  hazardous_material_indicator boolean [note: 'Flags this component as containing hazardous materials subject to regulatory controls (e.g., lead, cadmium, hexavalent chromium under EU ELV Directive, or lithium battery cells under UN 38.3). Triggers special handling, storage, and disposal procedures.']
  installation_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence defining the order in which this component is installed relative to other components at the same work center. Critical for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) material call-offs and shop floor assembly instruction generation.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'SAP BOM item category code indicating how the component is managed in inventory and procurement (e.g., stock item \'L\', non-stock item \'N\', variable-size item \'R\'). Drives material requirements planning (MRP) and goods movement behavior.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this BOM component record in the source system. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and BOM audit trail compliance.']
  lead_offset_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before or after the parent order start date that this component must be available at the work center. Negative values indicate the component must arrive before the order start. Used in JIT/JIS scheduling and MRP net requirements calculation.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit vehicle model year (MY) for which this BOM component is applicable (e.g., \'2025\'). Used to filter BOM components during model year changeover, SOP (Start of Production) planning, and regulatory homologation.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit SAP routing operation number to which this BOM component is assigned. Links the component to the specific assembly step in the production routing, enabling shop floor assembly instruction generation and MES work order sequencing.']
