  phantom_assembly_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is a phantom assembly — a logical grouping used for BOM structuring that has no physical stock or production order. MRP explodes through phantom assemblies to plan the underlying components directly.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Current PPAP approval status for this component, indicating whether the supplier has received production part approval from the OEM quality team. Components without approved PPAP status cannot be used in series production. Aligns with IATF 16949 requirements.']
  priority_in_alt_group varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank of this component within its alternate item group. Lower numbers indicate higher preference. MRP uses this priority to determine which alternate to consume first when multiple substitutes are available.']
  quantity_per_vehicle decimal(18,6) [note: 'Number of units of this component required to build one complete vehicle or parent assembly. Supports fractional quantities for bulk materials (e.g., 0.5 litres of adhesive). Used in MRP material call-off calculations and JIT/JIS scheduling.']
  reach_svhc_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component contains a Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) as defined under EU REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006. Required for regulatory compliance reporting and customer disclosure obligations.']
  scrap_factor_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Expected percentage of this component that will be scrapped during the manufacturing process, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 2.5000 = 2.5%). Used to inflate the planned component quantity in MRP to account for anticipated process losses. Supports PPM (Parts Per Million) defect tracking.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost per unit of this component in the plant currency, as maintained in the SAP material master cost estimate. Used in production order cost rollup, BOM cost analysis, and EBITDA reporting. Classified confidential as it contains sensitive pricing data.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'ISO unit of measure code for the component quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for litre, M for metre, M2 for square metre). Aligns with SAP base unit of measure in the material master.']
  usage_probability_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Probability (0–100%) that this component will actually be consumed in a given build, used for optional or variant-dependent components. Supports MRP planning for configurable vehicles where not every component is installed on every unit.']
  variant_condition varchar [note: 'Boolean or characteristic-based condition expression that determines when this component is included in a configurable BOM (e.g., \'ENGINE_TYPE = V8 AND MARKET = USA\'). Used in SAP Variant Configuration and Teamcenter product configuration management.']
  vehicle_program_code varchar [note: 'Internal OEM code identifying the vehicle program or platform to which this BOM component applies (e.g., \'F150-GEN14\', \'EV-PLATFORM-X\'). Supports multi-platform BOM management and cross-program parts commonality analysis.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Net weight of a single unit of this component in kilograms. Used in vehicle weight calculations, CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) compliance modelling, NVH (Noise Vibration Harshness) analysis, and logistics planning.']

  Note: 'Line-item record within a manufacturing BOM representing a single component or sub-assembly required to build a vehicle. Captures component part number, description, quantity per vehicle, unit of measure, installation work center, installation sequence, alternative part references, effectivity start/end dates, and scrap factor. Supports JIT/JIS material call-offs and shop floor assembly instructions.'
}

Table manufacturing.material_consumption {
  material_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each material consumption (goods issue) transaction record on the shop floor. Primary key of this table.']
  bom_component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_bom_component. Business justification: Material consumption records capture actual goods issues against specific BOM components. The bom_item_number STRING is a denormalized reference to the manufacturing_bom_component. Adding manufacturin']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Material consumption postings (goods issues to production) generate FI documents against specific GL accounts (raw material consumption, WIP inventory accounts). SAP MM-FI integration mandates GL acco']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Material consumption reports must reference the specific inbound part receipt to calculate usage rates and supplier performance metrics.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Material consumption references the inspection lot for the consumed batch/component. Existing \'quality_inspection_lot\' is denormalized. Proper FK enables incoming material quality traceability during ']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where the material consumption occurred. Aligns with SAP organizational unit for plant.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order against which this material was consumed. Links consumption to the manufacturing work order in SAP PP.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the consumed part or component. Corresponds to the SAP Material Master (MM) object.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location (bin, supermarket, line-side buffer) from which the material was issued for consumption.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Material consumption traceability per VIN is a regulatory requirement under IATF 16949 and NHTSA recall regulations — manufacturers must identify which specific components were consumed in building ea']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center (assembly station, stamping cell, paint booth, etc.) on the shop floor where the material was consumed.']
  assembly_station_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific assembly station or line position (JIS – Just-in-Sequence station) where the material was consumed. More granular than work center; used for shop floor traceability.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Supplier or internal batch/lot number of the consumed material. Critical for traceability, recall management, and IATF 16949 batch traceability requirements. Nullable for non-batch-managed materials.']
  bom_version varchar [note: 'Version or revision of the BOM used at the time of consumption. Supports engineering change management and ensures consumption is reconciled against the correct BOM revision.']
  consumption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material consumption record. \'posted\' = goods issue confirmed in SAP; \'reversed\' = goods issue reversed (movement type 262); \'blocked\' = held for quality review; \'pending\' = awaiting MES confirmation.']
  consumption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the material was physically issued from stock and consumed at the work center on the shop floor. This is the principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  cost_variance_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary value of the consumption variance (actual cost minus planned cost) in plant currency. Positive = cost overrun; negative = cost saving. Key metric for production cost variance reporting in SAP CO.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this material consumption record was first created in the source system (SAP MM or MES). Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the material cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY). Supports multi-currency manufacturing operations across global plants.']
  goods_movement_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible SAP material document number generated when the goods issue (movement type 261) is posted. Serves as the business-facing transaction identifier traceable in SAP MM.']
  goods_movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the goods movement (e.g., 261 = goods issue to production order, 262 = reversal, 291 = goods issue to cost center). Drives inventory and cost accounting postings.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this material consumption record in the source system. Used for incremental data load detection and audit trail in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  material_cost_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost value of the consumed material quantity at the time of goods issue, expressed in the plant currency. Calculated as quantity consumed × standard price from SAP Material Master. Feeds production cost accounting and variance reporting.']
  mes_transaction_code varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier from the Apriso/Dassault MES system that triggered or confirmed this material consumption event. Enables cross-system reconciliation between MES shop floor execution and SAP MM goods movements.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The model year (MY) of the vehicle being assembled (e.g., \'2025\'). Used for model-year-level consumption analysis, BOM alignment, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'The routing operation number within the production order at which the material was consumed (e.g., operation 0010 = body shop, 0020 = paint, 0030 = final assembly). Enables process-step-level consumption analysis.']
  original_goods_movement_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the original SAP material document number that this record reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Enables audit trail for reversed consumption transactions.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The planned quantity of this material as specified in the Bill of Materials (BOM) for the production order. Used to calculate consumption variance against actual quantity consumed.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting posting date for the goods issue in SAP, which may differ from the physical consumption timestamp. Used for period-end cost accounting and production variance reporting.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain type of the vehicle being assembled: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), EV (Electric Vehicle), FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives BOM variant selection and consumption expectations.']
  production_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the production line (e.g., \'LINE-A\', \'TRIM-2\', \'PAINT-EAST\') where the material was consumed. Used for line-level consumption analysis and capacity planning.']
  quantity_consumed decimal(18,6) [note: 'Actual quantity of the material consumed (goods issued) against the production order. Expressed in the base unit of measure of the material. Core quantitative fact for this transaction.']
  quantity_variance decimal(18,6) [note: 'Difference between actual quantity consumed and planned BOM quantity (actual minus planned). Positive value indicates over-consumption; negative indicates under-consumption. Key input for production variance reporting and cost accounting.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this consumption record is a reversal (cancellation) of a previously posted goods issue. True = this is a reversal document; False = original consumption posting.']
  scrap_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the consumed material was scrapped (True) rather than incorporated into the vehicle assembly. Scrap consumption is tracked separately for waste reporting, IATF 16949 scrap analysis, and environmental compliance.']
  scrap_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for material scrap when scrap_indicator is True (e.g., \'DAMAGE\', \'DEFECT\', \'WRONG_PART\', \'PROCESS_LOSS\'). Used for scrap root cause analysis and PPM defect rate reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DAMAGE|DEFECT|WRONG_PART|PROCESS_LOSS|HANDLING_ERROR|SUPPLIER_DEFECT|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Individual serial number of the consumed component where serial number management is active (e.g., ECU, battery module, transmission). Enables unit-level traceability for safety-critical parts. Nullable for non-serialized materials.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Production shift during which the material was consumed (DAY, AFTERNOON, NIGHT). Used for shift-level consumption analysis, variance trending, and workforce productivity reporting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this consumption record originated (SAP_MM = SAP S/4HANA MM goods movement, MES_APRISO = Apriso/Dassault MES, SAP_PP = SAP Production Planning, MANUAL = manual entry). Supports data lineage in the lakehouse.']
  supplier_number varchar [note: 'SAP vendor/supplier number of the supplier who provided the consumed material. Used for supplier-level consumption analysis, PPAP traceability, and supply chain variance reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for the consumed quantity (e.g., EA = each, KG = kilogram, L = litre, M = metre, PC = piece). Aligns with SAP base UoM from Material Master. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|PC|SET|ROL|BOX — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  variance_percentage decimal(10,4) [note: 'Quantity variance expressed as a percentage of the planned BOM quantity ((actual - planned) / planned × 100). Used for threshold-based alerting and PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate analysis.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'Internal OEM code identifying the vehicle model or platform being assembled (e.g., \'F150\', \'CAMRY-HEV\', \'SILVERADO-EV\'). Used for model-level consumption aggregation and BOM variance analysis.']
  wip_order_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this consumption event within the production order\'s WIP (Work in Progress) processing sequence. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) ordering analysis and shop floor flow traceability.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing actual material consumption (goods issue) against a production order on the shop floor. Tracks consumed part number, quantity consumed, unit of measure, consumption timestamp, work center, production order reference, storage location, batch number, and variance from planned BOM quantity. Feeds SAP MM goods movement and cost accounting for production variance reporting.'
}

Table manufacturing.rework_order {
  rework_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the rework order record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  build_stage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_stage. Business justification: Rework orders are initiated at a specific build stage where the defect was detected or where rework is performed. The assembly_stage_code STRING is a denormalized reference to the build_stage master. ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Rework labor and material costs must be posted to a cost center for quality cost reporting, warranty reserve calculations, and IATF 16949 quality cost analysis. Automotive quality controllers require ']
  defect_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality defect record in the quality domain that triggered this rework order. Links rework traceability to the originating inspection finding or defect notification.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Rework costs (labor and material) are posted to specific GL accounts (quality cost expense, scrap/rework accounts) for P&L reporting and cost-of-poor-quality analysis. Automotive finance requires GL-l']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Rework orders caused by defective inbound parts require traceability to the specific supplier part for SCAR issuance, PPAP re-submission triggers, and warranty cost recovery from suppliers. This is a ']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: After rework completion, a re-inspection lot is created to verify rework effectiveness. re_inspection_required field on rework_order indicates this need. Linking to inspection_lot enables rework verif']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where the rework operation is being executed. Aligns with SAP Plant (Werk) organizational unit.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating production order from which this rework order was raised. Links the rework event back to the scheduled manufacturing work order in SAP PP or MES.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Rework orders consume replacement parts that must be issued from inventory and tracked against specific SKUs for cost accounting, material replenishment triggering, and IATF 16949 quality cost reporti']
  vehicle_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.vehicle_build. Business justification: A rework_order is initiated on a specific vehicle_build that failed quality inspection. The vin STRING column is a denormalized vehicle identifier; replacing it with vehicle_build_id FK provides direc']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or repair station assigned to execute the rework operation. Corresponds to SAP PP Work Center or MES work station definition.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the rework operation was completed and the unit was released for re-inspection or returned to the production line. Recorded by MES upon order closure.']
  actual_rework_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Actual labor hours consumed to complete the rework operation, recorded upon order completion in MES or SAP PM. Compared against planned hours for efficiency analysis and cost variance reporting.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the rework technician began executing the rework operation, as recorded by MES shop floor data collection or operator confirmation.']
  bom_component_number varchar [note: 'The BOM (Bill of Materials) component or part number of the specific sub-assembly or component requiring rework. References the engineering BOM managed in Siemens Teamcenter or SAP MM.']
  corrective_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the corrective action (CAR) or 8D problem-solving report raised in response to this rework event. Links the rework order to the formal quality improvement process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rework order record was first created in the source system (MES or SAP QM). Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary cost fields on this rework order (rework_labor_cost, rework_material_cost). Typically the plant\'s local operating currency (e.g., USD, EUR, MXN).']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized defect classification code identifying the type of nonconformance that triggered the rework order. Aligns with the plant defect code catalogue maintained in SAP QM or MES quality module.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the defect or nonconformance identified during in-process quality inspection that initiated this rework order. Captured by the quality inspector or MES operator.']
  defect_location_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the physical location or zone on the vehicle or sub-assembly where the defect was found (e.g., body zone, paint zone, chassis area). Used for spatial defect analysis and NVH (Noise Vibration Harshness) correlation.']
  detection_method_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the method by which the defect was detected prior to rework order creation (e.g., visual inspection, automated gauge check, functional test, quality audit, customer feedback). Supports FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) detection ranking analysis.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the nonconformance was originally detected during in-process quality inspection, triggering the creation of this rework order. The principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the nonconforming unit after rework evaluation. Repaired indicates successful correction; scrapped indicates the unit is unrecoverable; concession (use-as-is) indicates acceptance of the nonconformance with documented deviation; return to supplier applies to purchased components.']
  inspection_point_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific quality inspection checkpoint or gate (e.g., body-in-white audit, paint final audit, end-of-line check) at which the defect was detected and the rework order was initiated.']
  is_regulatory_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle or sub-assembly is placed on a regulatory hold pending rework completion and re-inspection. A regulatory hold prevents the unit from advancing to the next production stage or shipping.']
  is_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the defect triggering this rework order is classified as safety-critical, requiring escalation per NHTSA, DOT, or internal safety reporting protocols. Safety-related rework orders may trigger FMEA review and potential field action assessment.']
  line_stoppage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rework event caused a production line stoppage (Andon pull or line stop). Used for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) impact analysis and production downtime reporting.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The vehicle model year (MY) of the unit undergoing rework. Used for regulatory compliance reporting, warranty analysis, and production quality trending by model year.']
  planned_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the rework operation is planned to be completed. Used to calculate planned rework cycle time and assess impact on downstream production sequencing.']
  planned_rework_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated labor hours planned to complete the rework operation, as defined in the rework routing or standard repair time (SRT) catalogue. Used for capacity planning and cost estimation.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the rework operation is planned to begin, as determined by the MES scheduler or production planner. Used for shop floor capacity planning and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the rework order to drive scheduling and resource allocation on the shop floor. Critical priority typically indicates a safety-related or regulatory nonconformance requiring immediate resolution.']
  production_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific production line (e.g., stamping, body shop, paint, final assembly) where the defect was originally detected and the rework order was raised.']
  re_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal re-inspection is required after rework completion before the unit can be released to the next production stage or shipped. Mandatory for safety-related or regulatory nonconformances.']
  re_inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quality re-inspection performed after rework completion. Pass indicates the unit meets specification; fail triggers a secondary rework or scrap decision; conditional pass indicates acceptance with documented deviation.']
  rework_attempt_count varchar [note: 'Number of rework attempts made on this unit or component for the same defect. Multiple rework attempts on the same unit indicate a chronic quality issue and may trigger escalation or scrap decision per quality policy.']
  rework_instruction_reference varchar [note: 'Document number or reference to the approved rework instruction, repair standard, or Technical Service Bulletin (TSB) that governs the rework procedure. Ensures traceability to authorized repair methods.']
  rework_labor_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total internal labor cost incurred for the rework operation, calculated from actual rework hours and the applicable labor rate. Expressed in the plant\'s operating currency. Used for quality cost (Cost of Poor Quality - COPQ) reporting.']
  rework_material_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Cost of replacement parts and materials consumed during the rework operation. Sourced from SAP MM goods issue postings against the rework order. Contributes to total Cost of Poor Quality (COPQ) calculation.']
  rework_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the rework order, typically generated by the MES (Manufacturing Execution System) or SAP QM module. Format: RWK-{PlantCode}-{Year}-{Sequence}.']
  rework_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rework order within the shop floor execution workflow. Tracks progression from creation through completion or cancellation as managed in MES or SAP QM.']
  rework_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rework operation method. Inline repair is performed on the moving assembly line; offline rework removes the unit from the line; teardown involves partial or full disassembly; subassembly rework targets a component before vehicle integration; cosmetic repair addresses surface or finish defects. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inline_repair|offline_rework|teardown|subassembly_rework|cosmetic_repair|field_return_rework — promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the root cause of the defect as determined through root cause analysis (e.g., 5-Why, Ishikawa). Populated upon rework completion. Supports FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) and SPC (Statistical Process Control) improvement actions.']
  scrap_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of components or sub-assemblies scrapped as a result of this rework order where repair was not feasible. Used for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation and scrap cost reporting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this rework order record was sourced (e.g., SAP QM, Apriso/Dassault MES, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion traceability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the rework order record was last modified in the source system. Supports incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer and audit trail requirements.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'Internal product/model code identifying the vehicle nameplate or platform (e.g., truck, SUV, EV, HEV) associated with the rework order. Aligns with PLM product structure in Siemens Teamcenter.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for rework or repair operations initiated on a vehicle or sub-assembly that failed an in-process quality check. Captures rework order number, originating production order, VIN reference, defect description, rework type (in-line repair, offline rework, teardown), assigned work center, planned and actual rework hours, rework status, and disposition (repaired, scrapped, concession). Integrates with quality domain defect records.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_confirmation {
  production_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the production confirmation record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity. Entity Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete business event (completion of a production order or partial operation) with a clear lifecycle.']
  build_stage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_stage. Business justification: A production_confirmation records completion of a specific operation at a build stage. The assembly_stage STRING is a denormalized reference to the build_stage master. Adding build_stage_id FK enables']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Activity confirmations post actual labor and machine costs to cost centers in the SAP CO module. production_confirmation.cost_center_code is a denormalized plain attribute; replacing it with a proper ']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Production confirmations trigger goods receipt and activity cost postings in SAP; they must be tied to fiscal periods for period-end cutoff, production cost settlement, and monthly closing. Automotive']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: SAP QM standard: production confirmations create inspection lots for in-process quality. Existing \'quality_inspection_lot\' is a denormalized lot number; proper FK to inspection_lot.inspection_lot_id e']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production confirmation was posted. Enables plant-level production output reporting, capacity utilization analysis, and multi-plant benchmarking.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: A production_confirmation confirms the completion of a production_order. The production_order_number STRING column is a denormalized reference; replacing it with a proper FK production_order_id normal']
  reversed_confirmation_production_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original production confirmation record that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Enables audit trail reconstruction and net quantity calculation when reversals are present. Self-referencing within the production_confirmation product.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this confirmation was recorded. Enables shift-level production output analysis, yield comparison across shifts, and workforce productivity reporting.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Production confirmations post goods receipts to specific storage locations (SAP GR posting). storage_location_code is a denormalized reference. This FK is required for accurate inventory posting, WIP-']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center (SAP ARBPL) where the confirmed operation was executed. Work centers represent physical production resources such as stamping presses, welding stations, paint booths, or assembly lines. Used for capacity reporting and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis.']
  activity_type_code varchar [note: 'SAP CO activity type (LSTAR) used to record the confirmed actual activities (setup, machine, labor) against the production order. Drives cost settlement rates and actual cost calculation in SAP CO-PC (Product Costing). Required for standard vs. actual cost variance analysis.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which yield, scrap, and rework quantities are expressed. Typically \'EA\' (Each) for vehicles and assemblies, \'PC\' (Piece) for components, \'KG\' for weight-based materials. Maps to SAP MM/PP base unit of measure (MEINS). Required for correct inventory posting and quantity reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|L|M|SET|VH — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'SAP batch number (CHARG) assigned to the confirmed production output. Used for batch-managed materials to enable full forward and backward traceability of components and finished vehicles. Critical for recall management, warranty analysis, and regulatory compliance with NHTSA traceability requirements.']
  confirmation_entry_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which this production confirmation was entered into SAP PP. \'manual\' = entered by operator via CO11N; \'automatic\' = system-generated via PP-PI or SCADA integration; \'mes_interface\' = posted via Apriso/Dassault MES interface; \'backflush\' = automatic backflush confirmation triggered by goods issue. Used for data quality auditing and process automation tracking.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric confirmation number assigned by SAP PP (CO11N) at the time of posting. This is the business-facing identifier used in shop floor reporting, MES integration, and order settlement reconciliation. Maps to SAP field RUECK (Confirmation Number).']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production confirmation record. \'created\' = confirmation entered but not yet posted; \'posted\' = successfully posted to SAP PP and inventory updated; \'reversed\' = confirmation reversed via CO13; \'cancelled\' = voided before posting; \'partial\' = partial quantity confirmed, order still open; \'final\' = final confirmation closing the production order.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the production operation or order was confirmed as complete on the shop floor. This is the business event time (not the system posting time) and represents the actual completion moment. Maps to SAP PP fields BUDAT (posting date) + ISDD/ISDZ (actual execution date/time). Used for takt time analysis, shift output reporting, and production scheduling.']
  confirmation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the confirmation indicating whether it is a partial confirmation (interim progress), final confirmation (order completion), automatic system-generated confirmation, milestone-based confirmation, or a reversal entry. Determines downstream SAP PP processing logic including order closure and goods receipt.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this production confirmation record was first created in the source system (SAP PP or MES). Represents the system audit creation time, distinct from the confirmation_timestamp (business event time). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of confirmation posting latency.']
  downtime_duration_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total unplanned downtime duration recorded during this production confirmation period, in minutes. Captured from SCADA/PLC systems or MES downtime tracking. Used for OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) availability calculation, maintenance planning, and production loss analysis.']
  downtime_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for any downtime recorded during this confirmation period. Maps to the plant\'s downtime reason catalog (e.g., \'MECH\' = Mechanical Failure, \'MATL\' = Material Shortage, \'QUAL\' = Quality Hold, \'CHNG\' = Changeover). Used for root cause analysis, maintenance prioritization, and production loss reporting.']
  final_confirmation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this confirmation is the final confirmation for the production order (SAP PP IEDD — Final Confirmation Indicator). When True, SAP PP automatically closes the production order, triggers goods receipt for the finished product, and initiates order settlement to CO (Controlling). Critical for order lifecycle management and inventory accuracy.']
  goods_receipt_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the automatic goods receipt (GR) to inventory has been successfully posted as a result of this production confirmation. True = inventory has been updated with the confirmed yield quantity. Used for inventory reconciliation and to identify confirmations where GR posting failed or is pending.']
  labor_time_actual_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual direct labor time consumed for this production operation, recorded in minutes. Maps to SAP PP activity type for labor (ISRLA — Actual Labor Time). Used for labor cost settlement to the production order, workforce productivity analysis, and standard vs. actual cost variance reporting.']
  labor_time_planned_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned direct labor time for this operation as defined in the SAP PP routing, recorded in minutes. Baseline for labor efficiency variance analysis and standard cost calculation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this production confirmation record was last modified in the source system. Tracks reversals, corrections, and status updates. Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and change data capture (CDC) processing.']
  machine_time_actual_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual machine runtime consumed for this production operation, recorded in minutes. Maps to SAP PP activity type for machine time (ISRÜT — Actual Machine Time). Core input for OEE calculation, takt time variance analysis, and capacity utilization reporting. Compared against planned machine time from the routing to identify bottlenecks.']
  machine_time_planned_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned machine time for this operation as defined in the SAP PP routing, recorded in minutes. Baseline for actual vs. planned machine time variance analysis and takt time adherence reporting.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material number (MATNR) of the finished or semi-finished product being confirmed. Identifies the vehicle model, sub-assembly, or component produced in this confirmation. Essential for inventory goods receipt posting and BOM (Bill of Materials) reconciliation.']
  mes_confirmation_reference varchar [note: 'The confirmation or transaction reference number from the Apriso/Dassault MES system that corresponds to this SAP PP production confirmation. Enables cross-system traceability between the MES shop floor execution record and the SAP ERP confirmation posting. Critical for audit trails and discrepancy resolution.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'The vehicle model year (MY) of the product being confirmed. Used for production output reporting by model year, regulatory compliance tracking (CAFE, EPA emissions), and alignment with SOP (Start of Production) and EOP (End of Production) milestones. Retained as a denormalized attribute for direct reporting without joining to vehicle configuration.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Routing operation number (VORNR) within the production order being confirmed. Identifies the specific manufacturing step (e.g., 0010 = Stamping, 0020 = Body Weld, 0030 = Paint, 0040 = Final Assembly) for partial confirmation scenarios. Maps to SAP PP routing operation.']
  order_settlement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the SAP CO production order settlement triggered by this final confirmation. \'pending\' = settlement not yet executed; \'settled\' = actual costs fully settled to cost objects; \'partially_settled\' = partial settlement completed; \'not_required\' = non-final confirmation, settlement not applicable. Used for period-end closing and financial reporting.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting/inventory posting date (SAP BUDAT) on which the production confirmation is financially posted. May differ from the confirmation timestamp when confirmations are entered retroactively. Drives inventory valuation, cost object settlement, and financial period assignment.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'The powertrain technology type of the vehicle or assembly being confirmed. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine, HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle, PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle, BEV = Battery Electric Vehicle, FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Used for regulatory reporting (CAFE, EPA, CARB), EV production mix analysis, and R&D investment tracking.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this confirmation record is a reversal entry (CO13 reversal in SAP PP). True = this record reverses a previously posted confirmation. Used to identify correction entries in production output reporting and to exclude reversed confirmations from yield and time aggregations.']
  rework_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units requiring rework (reprocessing to meet specification) identified during this confirmation. Distinct from scrap (which is permanently rejected). Used for quality cost analysis, rework labor tracking, and process capability assessment per APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) requirements.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units scrapped (rejected as non-conforming) during this production operation. Maps to SAP PP field XMNGA (Scrap Quantity). Used for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect rate calculation, scrap cost reporting, and FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) input. Triggers quality notification creation when thresholds are exceeded.']
  scrap_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for scrap recorded against this confirmation. Maps to SAP QM defect codes or MES scrap reason catalog (e.g., \'DIM\' = Dimensional Non-conformance, \'WLD\' = Weld Defect, \'PNT\' = Paint Defect, \'ASY\' = Assembly Error). Required for PPM (Parts Per Million) defect analysis, FMEA updates, and quality improvement initiatives per IATF 16949.']
  setup_time_actual_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual machine/workstation setup time consumed for this operation, recorded in minutes. Maps to SAP PP activity type for setup (ISRST — Actual Setup Time). Used for capacity planning, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation, and comparison against planned setup time from the routing.']
  setup_time_planned_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned setup time for this operation as defined in the SAP PP routing, recorded in minutes. Provides the baseline for actual vs. planned setup time variance analysis. Sourced from the routing master data at the time of production order creation.']
  takt_time_actual_sec decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual takt time achieved for this production confirmation, expressed in seconds per unit. Calculated as total operation time divided by yield quantity. Used for production line balancing, bottleneck identification, and comparison against the planned takt time to assess line efficiency. Key metric in JIS (Just-in-Sequence) production management.']
  teardown_time_actual_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual teardown/cleanup time consumed after the production operation, recorded in minutes. Maps to SAP PP activity type for teardown. Included in total operation time for accurate takt time and capacity utilization calculations. Relevant for paint booth cleaning, tooling changeover, and AGV (Automated Guided Vehicle) repositioning cycles.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number assigned to the vehicle unit confirmed in this production record. Populated for final assembly confirmations where a specific vehicle unit is produced. Null for sub-assembly or component-level confirmations. Enables full build traceability from production confirmation to vehicle delivery.']
  yield_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The confirmed good quantity (yield) produced in this confirmation, expressed in the base unit of measure of the material. Represents the number of units that passed quality inspection and are accepted into inventory. Maps to SAP PP field LMNGA (Yield Quantity). Core metric for production output reporting and OTD (On-Time Delivery) tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record confirming the completion of a production order or a partial operation within a production order. Captures confirmation number, production order reference, confirmed quantity (yield and scrap), actual activity times (setup, machine, labor), confirmation timestamp, shift, work center, and operator. Triggers SAP PP order settlement and inventory goods receipt. SSOT for actual production output reporting.'
}

Table manufacturing.capacity_plan {
  capacity_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each capacity plan record in the manufacturing domain. Primary key for the capacity_plan data product.']
  bottleneck_work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center identified as the capacity bottleneck constraining overall line throughput during this planning period. Used to prioritize capacity improvement investments and focus S&OP constraint management.']
  budget_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_plan. Business justification: Capacity plans drive headcount and capital budgets; linking capacity_plan to budget_plan enables integrated S&OP-to-financial-plan alignment. Automotive OEMs reconcile capacity plans with financial bu']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Capacity plans are time-bounded by fiscal periods for quarterly and annual planning cycles. Automotive OEMs align capacity utilization reporting to fiscal periods for integrated financial-operational ']
  fleet_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.fleet_contract. Business justification: Fleet contracts with large committed volumes are primary inputs to capacity planning. Automotive capacity planners must know which fleet contracts are driving capacity requirements to justify headcoun']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Capacity plans are built per vehicle model to determine line investment, headcount, and takt time requirements. S&OP and capital expenditure approval processes require capacity plans linked to specifi']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant for which this capacity plan is defined. Links to the manufacturing.plant master record.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production line (e.g., final assembly line, body shop line, paint line) within the plant for which capacity is being planned. Links to the manufacturing.work_center or production line master.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shift pattern (e.g., 1-shift, 2-shift, 3-shift) applied in this capacity plan. Shift pattern determines available production hours and directly impacts rated capacity calculation.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Capacity planning aligns plant capacity with each vehicle program; capacity_plan needs vehicle_program reference.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Capacity expansion plans requiring CAPEX investment are linked to WBS elements for investment program controlling. capacity_plan.capex_amount is a plain attribute indicating CAPEX relevance; the WBS F']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the capacity plan was formally approved by the designated authority. Marks the transition from \'submitted\' to \'approved\' status in the S&OP governance process.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the S&OP or manufacturing leadership authority who approved this capacity plan. Required for audit trail and governance compliance under IATF 16949.']
  available_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total scheduled production hours available in the planning period after accounting for planned downtime, shift breaks, and scheduled maintenance. Used in conjunction with rated_capacity_jph to compute rated capacity in units.']
  bottleneck_constraint_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the primary capacity constraint at the bottleneck work center (e.g., \'Paint oven cycle time limits throughput to 58 JPH\', \'Robotic welding cell maintenance window reduces available hours\'). Supports root cause analysis and CapEx justification.']
  capacity_gap_units varchar [note: 'Difference between planned_production_units and demonstrated_capacity_units, representing the capacity shortfall or surplus for the planning period. Positive value indicates a capacity gap requiring corrective action (overtime, additional shifts, CapEx); negative value indicates surplus capacity.']
  capacity_plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capacity planning methodology used. \'rough_cut\' (RCCP — Rough Cut Capacity Planning) for high-level S&OP; \'detailed\' for work-center-level CRP (Capacity Requirements Planning); \'constraint_based\' for Theory of Constraints analysis; \'what_if\' for scenario planning and CapEx evaluation.']
  capacity_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned capacity utilization rate expressed as a percentage, calculated as (planned_production_units / rated_capacity_units) × 100. Drives S&OP decisions on overtime, additional shifts, and capital investment. Target thresholds are typically 85–95% for automotive assembly.']
  capex_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated capital expenditure amount required to address the capacity gap identified in this plan. Expressed in the plant\'s operating currency. Used for CapEx budget submissions and investment approval processes.']
  capex_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this capacity plan identifies a requirement for capital investment (CapEx) to close a capacity gap (e.g., new tooling, additional robots, facility expansion). Triggers CapEx approval workflow in SAP FI/CO.']
  changeover_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hours allocated for model changeovers, tooling changes, and line reconfigurations during the planning period. Relevant for mixed-model assembly lines producing multiple vehicle variants (e.g., ICE and BEV on the same line).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this capacity plan record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and compliance purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this capacity plan record (e.g., USD, EUR, MXN, CNY). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-plant, multi-currency manufacturing operations.']
  daily_production_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of vehicles to be produced per working day during the planning period, derived from planned_production_units / working_days. Used for daily MES scheduling and shift-level production targets.']
  demonstrated_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Actual production output achieved in a comparable prior period, used as the realistic baseline for capacity planning. Reflects real-world constraints such as quality losses, minor stoppages, and changeovers. Key input to APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning) capacity studies.']
  eop_date date [note: 'Planned End of Production (EOP) date for the vehicle model or program covered by this capacity plan. Marks the milestone when production of the model is scheduled to cease. Used for capacity wind-down planning and asset redeployment decisions.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year (FY) to which this capacity plan belongs, used for CapEx (Capital Expenditure) alignment, budget planning, and financial reporting. Format: FY followed by 4-digit year (e.g., FY2025).']
  headcount_required varchar [note: 'Total direct labor headcount required to execute the planned production volume during the planning period. Used for workforce planning, hiring decisions, and labor cost budgeting in SuccessFactors and SAP CO.']
  is_bottleneck_constrained boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the capacity plan is constrained by a bottleneck work center that limits overall line throughput below the planned production target. When True, the bottleneck_work_center_id and bottleneck_constraint_description fields are populated.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Automotive model year (MY) for which this capacity plan is prepared. Capacity plans are typically aligned to MY production programs, SOP (Start of Production), and EOP (End of Production) milestones.']
  number_of_shifts varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts planned per working day for this capacity plan (e.g., 1, 2, or 3 shifts). Directly determines available production hours and total capacity for the planning period.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the planning period covered by this capacity plan record. Together with plan_start_date, defines the planning window for capacity supply and demand balancing.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the capacity plan record, used in S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) communications, SAP PP capacity planning transactions (CM01/CM21), and cross-functional reporting. Format: CP-{PLANT}-{YEAR}-{MONTH}-{SEQ}.']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the planning period covered by this capacity plan record. Used to align capacity supply with demand signals from S&OP and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) runs.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the capacity plan record. \'draft\' indicates work-in-progress; \'submitted\' indicates pending approval; \'approved\' indicates sign-off by S&OP leadership; \'active\' indicates the operative plan driving production scheduling; \'superseded\' indicates replaced by a newer version; \'archived\' indicates historical record.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the capacity plan, enabling management of multiple planning iterations (e.g., v1.0, v2.0) within the same planning period. Supports S&OP cycle versioning and plan-over-plan variance analysis.']
  planned_downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned downtime hours for the production line during the planning period, including scheduled maintenance, tooling changeovers, model changeovers, and planned shutdowns. Reduces available production hours from the theoretical maximum.']
  planned_maintenance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Subset of planned downtime hours specifically allocated to preventive and predictive maintenance activities on production equipment. Tracked separately from changeover downtime for maintenance KPI reporting and SAP PM integration.']
  planned_overtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours planned for the production line during the planning period to meet production targets beyond standard shift capacity. Feeds into workforce cost planning and labor budget in SAP CO.']
  planned_production_units varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles planned for production in the planning period as agreed in the S&OP process. Represents the demand signal that capacity must satisfy. Compared against rated and demonstrated capacity to identify gaps.']
  planning_period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the capacity planning horizon for this record. \'weekly\' supports short-term MES scheduling; \'monthly\' supports S&OP cycles; \'quarterly\' and \'annual\' support CapEx and strategic workforce planning.']
  planning_scenario varchar [note: 'Demand scenario underpinning this capacity plan. \'base\' reflects the consensus S&OP forecast; \'optimistic\' reflects upside demand; \'pessimistic\' reflects downside risk; \'stretch\' reflects maximum achievable output. Enables scenario comparison for executive decision-making.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Powertrain technology type planned for production in this capacity plan. ICE = Internal Combustion Engine; HEV = Hybrid Electric Vehicle; PHEV = Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle; BEV = Battery Electric Vehicle; FCEV = Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle. Impacts tooling, line configuration, and capacity requirements.']
  rated_capacity_jph decimal(10,2) [note: 'Theoretical maximum production rate expressed in Jobs Per Hour (JPH) for the production line under ideal conditions. Derived from takt time and line design specifications. Used as the baseline for capacity utilization calculations in S&OP.']
  rated_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Theoretical maximum number of vehicles that can be produced in the planning period, calculated as rated_capacity_jph × available_hours. Represents the upper bound of production capacity for S&OP and CapEx planning.']
  sap_capacity_plan_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this capacity plan record in SAP S/4HANA PP (Production Planning) module, used for data lineage traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational ERP system.']
  sop_date date [note: 'Planned Start of Production (SOP) date for the vehicle model or program covered by this capacity plan. Marks the milestone when full-rate production is scheduled to begin. Critical for capacity ramp-up planning and APQP milestone tracking.']
  takt_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Planned takt time in seconds for the production line during this planning period, representing the rate at which vehicles must be produced to meet customer demand. Takt time = available production time / planned production units. Core metric in JIS (Just-in-Sequence) and line balancing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this capacity plan record was last modified. Tracks plan revisions through the S&OP cycle and supports change management auditing.']
  working_days varchar [note: 'Number of scheduled working days in the planning period after excluding weekends, public holidays, and planned plant shutdowns. Used to compute daily production targets and available hours.']

  Note: 'Master record for plant and line capacity planning, capturing planned production capacity by plant, production line, shift pattern, and planning period (weekly/monthly). Includes rated capacity (JPH × available hours), demonstrated capacity, capacity utilization percentage, bottleneck work center, planned overtime hours, and capacity plan version. Supports S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) and capital investment decisions.'
}

Table manufacturing.production_variant {
  production_variant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for production_variant']
  apqp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: Each production variant (market-specific vehicle configuration) has an APQP plan governing its launch quality gates. APQP plans are variant-specific per IATF 16949 APQP requirements; ppap_status on pr']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Each production variant is governed by a variant-specific control plan (different markets/configurations have different quality controls). PPAP approval requires a control plan per variant. ppap_statu']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Each production variant has a standard cost tracked against a cost center for product costing and variant-level profitability analysis. Automotive OEMs assign cost centers to production variants to en']
  ecu_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecu_specification. Business justification: Each production variant requires a specific ECU specification governing software version, calibration dataset, and OTA capability for end-of-line programming and homologation. Manufacturing and softwa']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Variant definitions are approved via homologation; linking ensures variant builds only when approved.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Variant Definition Process links each variant to its vehicle model for configuration management and regulatory compliance.']
  model_year_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program. Business justification: Production variants are scoped to model year programs — SOP/EOP dates, regulatory variants, and PPAP status are all model-year-program-specific. Manufacturing variant lifecycle management requires thi']
  msrp_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.msrp_schedule. Business justification: Production variant-to-MSRP alignment is required for margin analysis and launch readiness: manufacturers validate that every production variant has an active published MSRP schedule before SOP. This l']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production variant is authorized to be built. Determines line compatibility, tooling availability, and regulatory homologation scope.']
  powertrain_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.powertrain_spec. Business justification: Each production variant is governed by a specific engineering powertrain_spec defining assembly parameters, emissions compliance, and PPAP requirements. Manufacturing engineers and APQP teams require ']
  powertrain_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: Manufacturing production variants must trace to certified vehicle powertrain_variants for emissions compliance validation, PPAP approval, and regulatory homologation. Production engineers verify each ']
  price_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_price_condition. Business justification: Variant-level pricing drives dealer invoice amounts and MSRP in automotive billing. Each production variant (trim, powertrain, options) maps to specific SAP SD price conditions used to generate the ve']
  production_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing BOM (Bill of Materials) version applicable to this production variant. Links to the BOM master in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or SAP MM (Materials Management) to resolve component lists for production planning and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) execution.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production line within the plant on which this variant is authorized to be assembled. Drives JIS (Just-in-Sequence) feasibility validation and takt time allocation.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing routing (operation sequence) applicable to this production variant in SAP PP (Production Planning). Defines the sequence of work centers, operations, and standard times required to build this variant from stamping through final assembly.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Each production variant maps to a finished-goods SKU for inventory valuation, dealer stock management, and SAP material master integration. sap_material_number is a denormalized SKU reference that thi']
  trim_level_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_trim_level. Business justification: Production variants are built to specific trim levels, driving component allocation, labor standards, and JIS sequencing. Manufacturing scheduling systems require the trim_level FK to determine correc']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Variants are defined under a vehicle program; variant management reports require vehicle_program_id.']
  adas_level varchar [note: 'SAE J3016 automation level of the ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) suite fitted to this production variant: L0 (no automation), L1 (driver assistance), L2 (partial automation), L2_PLUS (enhanced partial automation), L3 (conditional automation). Drives ECU (Electronic Control Unit) software configuration, OTA (Over-the-Air Update) eligibility, and UNECE UN R157 regulatory compliance.']
  assembly_complexity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1–10) representing the relative assembly complexity of this production variant compared to the base variant on the same production line. Higher scores indicate more assembly operations, longer cycle times, or specialized tooling requirements. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) sequencing optimization to balance line loading and avoid consecutive high-complexity builds.']
  battery_capacity_kwh decimal(8,2) [note: 'Usable battery pack capacity in kilowatt-hours (kWh) for BEV, PHEV, and HEV variants. Null for pure ICE variants. Drives assembly sequencing for battery marriage station, WLTP (Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure) range certification, and EPA electric range labelling.']
  body_style_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the vehicle body configuration for this production variant (e.g., SEDAN, SUV, TRUCK, COUPE, HATCHBACK, MINIVAN). Determines stamping die sets, body shop tooling, and paint booth configuration requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SEDAN|SUV|TRUCK|COUPE|HATCHBACK|MINIVAN|CONVERTIBLE|WAGON|VAN|CROSSOVER — promote to reference product]']
  co2_emission_g_per_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Certified CO2 tailpipe emission in grams per kilometre (g/km) under WLTP or NEDC test cycle for this production variant. Used for EU CO2 fleet average compliance (Regulation (EU) 2019/631), CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) calculations, and vehicle taxation band classification in destination markets.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production variant record was first created in the manufacturing master data system. Provides audit trail for data governance and IATF 16949 documented information control requirements.']
  destination_market_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code identifying the primary market for which this production variant is homologated and destined (e.g., \'USA\', \'DEU\', \'JPN\'). Governs regulatory compliance requirements (NHTSA FMVSS, Euro NCAP, UNECE), emission standards, and right-hand vs left-hand drive configuration.']
  drive_configuration varchar [note: 'Drivetrain configuration specifying which axles receive motive power: FWD (Front-Wheel Drive), RWD (Rear-Wheel Drive), AWD (All-Wheel Drive), or 4WD (Four-Wheel Drive). Determines chassis assembly routing, driveshaft fitment, and NVH (Noise Vibration Harshness) validation requirements.']
  engine_displacement_cc varchar [note: 'Total engine displacement in cubic centimetres (cc) for ICE (Internal Combustion Engine) and HEV/PHEV variants. Null for BEV and FCEV variants. Used for regulatory classification under EPA, CARB, and UNECE emissions standards, and for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) fleet averaging calculations.']
  engineering_change_level varchar [note: 'Current engineering change revision level for this production variant as managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM or PTC Windchill (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'C1\', \'REV3\'). Tracks design maturity and ensures the correct BOM (Bill of Materials) version is applied during production. Incremented upon each approved engineering change notice (ECN).']
  eop_date date [note: 'Date on which this production variant is scheduled to cease production (EOP — End of Production). Drives last-order scheduling, tooling decommissioning planning, and spare parts provisioning for after-sales service obligations.']
  fuel_economy_mpg decimal(6,2) [note: 'EPA-certified combined fuel economy in miles per gallon (MPG) for ICE, HEV, and PHEV variants in NAFTA markets. Null for BEV/FCEV variants. Used for CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) fleet compliance reporting to EPA and consumer window sticker labelling under 49 CFR Part 575.']
  gross_vehicle_weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Gross Vehicle Weight Rating (GVWR) in kilograms for this production variant. Used for regulatory classification under DOT FMVSS, UNECE weight regulations, and CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) fleet averaging. Also drives chassis assembly torque specifications and suspension tuning parameters.']
  hand_of_drive varchar [note: 'Steering wheel position for this production variant: LHD (Left-Hand Drive) for markets such as USA, Germany; RHD (Right-Hand Drive) for markets such as UK, Japan, Australia. Determines instrument panel assembly variant, steering column routing, and body shop tooling configuration.']
  homologation_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory type-approval and homologation status for this production variant in the target destination market: PENDING (application submitted), CERTIFIED (full approval granted), CONDITIONAL (provisional approval with conditions), EXPIRED (certification lapsed), NOT_REQUIRED. Governs whether the variant can be legally sold in the destination market.']
  is_ckd_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant can be produced as a CKD (Completely Knocked Down) kit for assembly at an overseas plant (True = CKD eligible). Drives export documentation, customs tariff classification, and overseas plant BOM (Bill of Materials) configuration in SAP MM.']
  is_jis_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant is eligible for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) supplier call-off sequencing (True = JIS eligible). JIS-eligible variants require sequenced component delivery synchronized to the build sequence, impacting supplier logistics contracts and MES call-off trigger configuration.']
  is_line_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant has been validated as feasible for assembly on the assigned production line (True = compatible, False = not compatible). Serves as the primary gate flag in MES (Manufacturing Execution System) build sequence feasibility validation and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) call-off authorization.']
  is_ota_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant supports OTA (Over-the-Air) software updates for ECU (Electronic Control Unit) and infotainment systems (True = OTA capable). Drives connected vehicle service provisioning in Geotab/Bosch IoT platform and UNECE UN R156 software update regulatory compliance.']
  max_consecutive_builds varchar [note: 'Maximum number of consecutive units of this production variant that can be scheduled back-to-back in the build sequence without violating line balancing or ergonomic constraints. Enforced by MES (Manufacturing Execution System) JIS (Just-in-Sequence) sequencing rules to prevent operator fatigue and tooling overload.']
  mes_variant_reference varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier for this production variant in the Apriso/Dassault MES (Manufacturing Execution System). Used for real-time build sequence call-off, WIP (Work in Progress) tracking, and quality gate routing on the shop floor.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit model year (MY) for which this production variant is defined and authorized. Governs BOM (Bill of Materials) version applicability, regulatory homologation, and CAFE (Corporate Average Fuel Economy) compliance grouping. Range: 2000–2040.']
  ncap_safety_rating varchar [note: 'Overall NCAP (New Car Assessment Programme) safety star rating achieved by this production variant (1 to 5 stars, or NOT_RATED if not yet assessed). Applicable to Euro NCAP, NHTSA NCAP, or ANCAP depending on destination market. Used for regulatory compliance reporting and marketing communications.']
  option_package_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the bundled option package associated with this production variant (e.g., \'TECH_PKG\', \'SAFETY_PKG\', \'LUXURY_PKG\'). Maps to a specific set of BOM (Bill of Materials) components and assembly operations in Siemens Teamcenter PLM and SAP PP configurable material management.']
  paint_process_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the paint application process required for this production variant: STANDARD (solid colour), METALLIC, PEARL, MATTE, or TWO_TONE. Determines paint booth routing, cure cycle parameters, and paint material BOM (Bill of Materials) components in the body shop and paint shop assembly stages.']
  powertrain_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vehicle powertrain technology for this variant: ICE (Internal Combustion Engine), HEV (Hybrid Electric Vehicle), PHEV (Plug-in Hybrid Electric Vehicle), BEV (Battery Electric Vehicle), or FCEV (Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle). Drives assembly process routing, tooling requirements, and EPA/CARB emissions compliance classification.']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Current PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) approval status for this production variant. PPAP approval is a mandatory quality gate before SOP (Start of Production) under IATF 16949. Status values: NOT_REQUIRED, PENDING, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED. Drives production release authorization in MES.']
  regulatory_region varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance region grouping for this production variant, used to apply the correct homologation ruleset: NAFTA (North America), EU (European Union), APAC (Asia-Pacific), LATAM (Latin America), MEA (Middle East & Africa), CHINA. Determines applicable NCAP (New Car Assessment Programme) standards, emissions regulations, and safety certification requirements.']
  sop_date date [note: 'Date on which this production variant is authorized to enter volume production (SOP — Start of Production). Used for production planning gate reviews, PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) milestone tracking, and supplier readiness confirmation.']
  standard_build_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard labor hours required to complete the full assembly of this production variant from stamping through final assembly and PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection). Used for production cost standard setting, workforce planning, and SAP CO (Controlling) cost object calculation.']
  takt_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Allocated takt time in seconds for building this production variant on the assigned production line. Represents the available production time divided by customer demand rate. Used for JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling, capacity planning, and bottleneck identification in MES (Manufacturing Execution System).']
  teamcenter_variant_code varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier for this production variant in Siemens Teamcenter PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) variant configuration module. Enables traceability between the manufacturing master data record and the engineering variant definition in the PLM system of record.']
  transmission_type varchar [note: 'Type of transmission fitted in this production variant: AUTOMATIC (conventional automatic), MANUAL (manual gearbox), CVT (Continuously Variable Transmission), DCT (Dual-Clutch Transmission), or SINGLE_SPEED (for BEV/FCEV). Drives assembly routing to the correct powertrain marriage station and torque tooling specification.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production variant record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for engineering change audit trail compliance under IATF 16949 documented information control.']
  v2x_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production variant is equipped with V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) communication hardware (True = V2X capable). Drives telematics module assembly sequencing, spectrum licensing compliance, and connected mobility service activation in Geotab/Bosch IoT platform.']
  variant_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this production variant within the manufacturing system. Used as the primary business key in MES (Manufacturing Execution System), PLM (Product Lifecycle Management), and SAP PP (Production Planning) for variant identification and cross-system referencing. Sourced from Siemens Teamcenter PLM variant configuration.']
  variant_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the production variant (e.g., \'F-Series SuperCrew 4x4 BEV Long Range\'). Used in build sequence planning, shop floor documentation, and JIS (Just-in-Sequence) scheduling displays.']
  variant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production variant within the manufacturing master data: ACTIVE (currently buildable), PLANNED (approved for future SOP), INACTIVE (not currently scheduled), DISCONTINUED (past EOP), ON_HOLD (temporarily suspended pending engineering change or regulatory review). Drives MES build feasibility checks.']
  vehicle_model_code varchar [note: 'OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) internal model code identifying the vehicle platform or nameplate (e.g., \'F150\', \'RAV4\', \'MODEL3\'). Links the production variant to the vehicle program in PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) and SAP MM (Materials Management).']
  wltp_range_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Official WLTP (Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure) certified driving range in kilometres for BEV and PHEV variants. Null for ICE and HEV variants. Used for EU regulatory labelling, EPA range certification cross-reference, and consumer-facing range claims.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining the valid production variants (option combinations) that can be built on a specific production line for a given model year. Captures variant code, model, MY, powertrain type (ICE, HEV, PHEV, BEV), body style, trim level, market destination, and line compatibility flags. Supports build sequence feasibility validation and JIS sequencing rules. Distinct from the commercial product catalog — this is the manufacturing-feasible variant definition.'
}

Table manufacturing.routing {
  routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for routing']
  alternate_routing_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on routing (alternate_routing_id)']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: Manufacturing routings are paired with control plans in APQP/PPAP — the routing defines operation sequence while the control plan defines quality controls for those operations. IATF 16949 requires thi']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Routings are defined at the part/assembly level in automotive manufacturing — each routing governs how a specific part or subassembly is produced. Linking routing to part_master enables traceability f']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant to which the routing belongs.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Manufacturing routings define operation sequences for a specific vehicle program. Program management, launch readiness reviews, and capacity planning all require knowing which vehicle_program a routin']
  change_approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the routing change.']
  change_approved_date date [note: 'Date when the routing change was formally approved.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent change to the routing.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable industry or internal compliance standard (e.g., ISO/TS 16949).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the routing record was first created in the master system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the routing is retired or superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the routing becomes valid for production planning.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'True if this routing is the default for its product family.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the routing was last reviewed for accuracy or compliance.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Primary material category processed by the routing (e.g., steel, aluminum, composite).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments, special handling instructions, or exceptions.']
  operation_count varchar [note: 'Total count of discrete work‑center steps defined in the routing.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'High‑level product family (e.g., SUV, Truck, EV) that uses this routing.']
