  quality_check_required boolean [note: 'True if the routing mandates a quality inspection step.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the last review.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the routing used on shop floor displays and reports.']
  routing_code varchar [note: 'External code used in MES and shop‑floor systems to reference this routing.']
  routing_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the routing purpose, scope and special instructions.']
  routing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the routing.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Category of the routing based on the manufacturing process it supports.']
  safety_level varchar [note: 'Safety classification of the routing based on hazard analysis.']
  total_cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned total processing time for the routing, expressed in minutes.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the routing record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change management of the routing.']
  work_center varchar [note: 'Primary work‑center or cell where the routing is executed.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who originally created the routing.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for routing. Referenced by routing_id.'
}

Table manufacturing.routing_operation {
  routing_operation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the routing operation association record']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the routing master record']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the work center master record']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing operation record was created in the system.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this operation is on the critical path for this routing, affecting overall production lead time.']
  operation_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the work performed at this work center for this routing operation (e.g., \'Weld body panels\', \'Install engine assembly\', \'Quality inspection\').']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'Sequential operation number within the routing (e.g., 10, 20, 30). Defines the order of operations in the manufacturing process. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  sequence_order varchar [note: 'Execution sequence of this operation within the routing. Used for scheduling and shop floor sequencing. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  setup_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Setup or changeover time in minutes required at this work center for this routing operation. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  standard_cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,3) [note: 'Standard cycle time in seconds for this specific operation at this work center within this routing. This is operation-specific and may differ from the work center\'s general cycle_time_seconds. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this routing operation record.']
  work_center_category varchar [note: 'Category or role of the work center within this specific routing operation (e.g., primary, alternate, backup). Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational step between routing and work_center. It captures the assignment of a specific work center to a specific operation within a manufacturing routing. Each record links one routing to one work_center with operation-specific parameters that exist only in the context of this routing-work center assignment, such as operation sequence, cycle times, and setup requirements.. Existence Justification: In automotive manufacturing, a routing defines the complete manufacturing process for a product family, consisting of multiple sequential operations, each performed at a specific work center. One routing contains many operations across different work centers (e.g., stamping → welding → painting → assembly), and one work center participates in many different routings (e.g., a robotic welding station is used in SUV routing, truck routing, and sedan routing). The routing_operation is a standard business entity in SAP PP and MES systems, representing the assignment of a work center to a specific step in a routing with operation-specific parameters.'
}

Table supply.supplier_part_approval {
  supplier_part_approval_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the supplier part approval record.']
  apqp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: Supplier part approvals (PPAP) are governed by APQP plans and must be completed at specific APQP gates (typically Gate 4). This link enables tracking approval status against program milestones and ens']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: PPAP submissions require approved control plans as mandatory documentation. Linking supplier part approvals to control plans enables validation that the supplier\'s quality control methodology meets OE']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PPAP approval activities (testing, validation, travel) incur costs that must be allocated to engineering or quality cost centers for departmental budget tracking and variance analysis.']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: supplier_part_approval currently stores part_number and part_name as denormalized strings. Adding inbound_part_id FK normalizes this to the supply domain\'s part master. The part_number and part_name c']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: PPAP approvals must reference specific regulatory requirements (FMVSS 302 flammability, ECE R100 battery safety, etc.) that the part must meet. Critical for compliance traceability in automotive manuf']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: PPAP approval records must be tied to the supplier entity in the supply domain for audit and traceability.']
  supplier_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier_plant. Business justification: Part approvals are granted at the plant level — a supplier may have multiple plants and each plant\'s manufacturing process must be independently approved. This FK links the approval to the specific pl']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the approval decision was recorded.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the approval record, often used in audit and reporting.']
  approval_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the PPAP review: approved, interim approval, or rejected.']
  approval_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) reflecting overall assessment of the part\'s compliance.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or manager who approved the part.']
  capability_study_status varchar [note: 'Status of the capability study element.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification indicating the importance of the part.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by reviewers or approvers.']
  control_plan_status varchar [note: 'Status of the control plan element.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Approved cost of the part in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approval record was first created in the system.']
  design_records_status varchar [note: 'Status of the design records element within the PPAP package.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the approval is no longer valid.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days for the part from supplier to plant.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the approval record.']
  msa_status varchar [note: 'Status of the MSA element.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Aggregated status of the approval record.']
  pfmea_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Process FMEA element.']
  ppap_submission_level varchar [note: 'Level of PPAP submission (1‑5) indicating the depth of documentation provided.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Quality rating assigned to the part after approval.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for the creation event of the record.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp for the most recent modification of the record.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part meets all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk level associated with the part based on failure mode analysis.']
  sample_parts_status varchar [note: 'Status of the sample parts element.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the supplier submitted the PPAP package.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the approval record.']

  Note: 'PPAP (Production Part Approval Process) record capturing the formal approval of a supplier-produced part for use in production. Includes approval ID, part number, supplier reference, PPAP submission level (1–5), submission date, approval date, approver, PPAP elements status (design records, PFMEA, control plan, MSA, capability study, sample parts), approval outcome (approved, interim approval, rejected), and expiry date. Mandated by IATF 16949 and AIAG PPAP standards for all production parts.'
}

Table supply.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_supplier data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Automotive OEMs maintain billing accounts for suppliers to process supplier invoices, track payables, and manage payment processing. Essential for accounts payable operations and supplier payment reco']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Supplier Jurisdiction Registry links a supplier to the jurisdiction where it operates for tax and regulatory reporting.']

  Note: 'Master record for all tier-1 and tier-2 suppliers in the automotive supply chain. Captures supplier identity, classification (direct/indirect, tier level), IATF 16949 certification status, DUNS number, geographic footprint, commodity codes, preferred currency, payment terms, and supplier lifecycle status (active, probation, disqualified). SSOT for supplier identity within the supply domain; integrates with SAP MM vendor master and PTC Windchill supplier collaboration.'
}

Table supply.supplier_plant {
  supplier_plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_supplier_plant data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link child plant to its supplier master for in‑domain hierarchy']

  Note: 'Represents the specific manufacturing or distribution plant/site of a supplier that ships parts to an OEM assembly plant. Captures plant address, plant code, production capabilities, capacity constraints, dock-to-dock lead time, customs zone, and plant-level quality certifications. Enables JIT/JIS scheduling at the plant-to-plant level and supports CKD/SKD kit origin tracking.'
}

Business justification: Each inbound part must have an associated control plan defining inspection requirements, sampling frequency, and reaction plans. This link is essential for receiving inspection operations, enabling in']

  width_mm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical width of the part in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Master record for every purchased part number sourced from external suppliers. Captures OEM part number, supplier part number cross-reference, commodity group, material type (raw, sub-assembly, CKD kit), unit of measure, PPAP approval status, engineering change level, hazardous material flag, country of origin, and customs tariff code. Bridges SAP MM material master and PTC Windchill parts classification for supply-domain-owned purchased parts.'
}

Table supply.sourcing_nomination {
  sourcing_nomination_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each sourcing nomination record.']
  commodity_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.commodity_group. Business justification: sourcing_nomination has a commodity field (denormalized string). Nominations are made at the commodity level — linking to commodity_group normalizes this and enables commodity-level sourcing analytics']
  customer_organization_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.organization_account. Business justification: Fleet and government contracts often include customer-directed sourcing (e.g., fleet operator specifies tire brand, government contract mandates domestic suppliers). Tracks customer-driven supplier se']
  fleet_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.fleet_contract. Business justification: Fleet contracts drive sourcing nominations for high-volume part requirements. Enables contract-driven supplier selection, volume commitments, and ensures supply chain capacity aligns with multi-year f']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: sourcing_nomination currently stores part_number as a denormalized string. The nomination is for a specific purchased part in the supply domain. Adding inbound_part_id normalizes this to the part mast']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Sourcing nominations in automotive are issued per vehicle model/program. Commodity managers must link nominations to vehicle.model to produce program-level sourcing coverage reports and ensure all par']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Link sourcing nomination to internal supplier master']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks entered by the buyer or sourcing team.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the nomination record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the target piece price.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Optional date when the nomination expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the nomination becomes effective for planning purposes.']
  is_jis boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the nominated part is required under a JIS delivery strategy.']
  is_jit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the nominated part is required under a JIT delivery strategy.']
  kit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the part is supplied as a Completely Knocked Down (CKD) or Semi‑Knocked Down (SKD) kit, or not a kit.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year for which the part nomination applies.']
  nominated_volume decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total quantity of the part the supplier is expected to deliver for the program.']
  nomination_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OEM formally recorded the nomination.']
  nomination_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business code assigned to the nomination for tracking and reference.']
  nomination_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the nomination.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the nomination for execution planning.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the vehicle program or platform associated with the nomination (e.g., SUV2025, EVX).']
  region varchar [note: 'Region code indicating the primary supply geography for the nominated part.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of supply risk for the nominated part.']
  sor_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code linking to the SOR document that defines functional and performance requirements.']
  target_piece_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target unit price (per piece) the OEM aims to achieve with the nominated supplier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the nomination record.']

  Note: 'Records the formal OEM decision to nominate a specific supplier for a given part or commodity within a model year program. Captures nomination date, program/platform code, nominated supplier, awarded annual volume, target piece price, SOR (Statement of Requirements) reference, nomination status (nominated, confirmed, withdrawn), and the responsible commodity buyer. Precedes the RFQ and PPAP process. SSOT for sourcing award decisions; distinct from procurement domain\'s strategic sourcing strategy — this is the operational award record.'
}

Ad']

Linking rfq to sourcing_nomination establishes the procurement chain: nomination → RFQ → response']

  regulatory_approval_required boolean [note: 'True if the part requires regulatory sign‑off before purchase.']

  target_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum price the buyer is willing to pay for the quoted item (gross).']

  tooling_description varchar [note: 'Details of the tooling requirements, if any.']
  tooling_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new tooling is needed for the part.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the quantity is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the RFQ record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who created the RFQ.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a Request for Quotation event issued to one or more suppliers for a specific part, assembly, or service. Captures RFQ number, issue date, response due date, commodity, target price, annual volume estimate, program/model year, tooling requirements, and RFQ status (open, closed, awarded, cancelled). Integrates with SAP MM RFQ (ME41) and PTC Windchill supplier collaboration portal.'
}

  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the supplier needs to deliver the quoted items after order confirmation.']

  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quoted quantity.']

  validity_end_date date [note: 'Last date on which the quoted terms are valid.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'First date on which the quoted terms are valid.']

  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Type of warranty provided.']

  Note: 'Captures a supplier\'s formal quotation response to an RFQ. Records quoted unit price, tooling cost, amortization terms, lead time, capacity commitment, exceptions to SOR, validity period, and response status (submitted, under review, accepted, rejected). Supports competitive bid analysis and supplier selection decisions. One RFQ may have multiple responses from competing suppliers.'
}

  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the volume (e.g., pieces, kilograms).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the scheduling agreement record.']

  Note: 'Long-term supply agreement with a supplier defining the framework for JIT/JIS delivery of parts over a model year or contract period. Captures agreement number, validity start/end dates, target annual volume, release horizon (firm/forecast weeks), delivery rhythm (daily, weekly), Kanban flag, and agreement status. The scheduling agreement is the backbone of JIT supply in SAP MM (ME31L/ME32L).'
}

Table supply.inbound_shipment {
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the inbound shipment record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Carrier performance & cost reporting uses inbound shipment data; linking enables carrier KPI aggregation.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Shipments are received by legal entities (company codes) for customs clearance, import duty calculation, and VAT/tax determination based on receiving legal entity jurisdiction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Freight costs and receiving/inspection labor are allocated to logistics or receiving cost centers for transportation cost analysis and warehouse budget management.']
  destination_vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: JIT/JIS sequencing operations require inbound parts delivered to vehicle compounds adjacent to assembly plants for line-side staging before production consumption. Common in automotive for sequenced p']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: An inbound shipment is made against a purchase order. Linking inbound_shipment to supply_purchase_order enables PO-level goods receipt tracking, three-way matching (PO-shipment-invoice), and delivery ']
  receiving_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the OEM plant receiving the shipment.']
  receiving_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Inbound shipments must specify receiving storage location for dock-to-stock process, bin allocation, and warehouse management. Enables automated goods receipt posting and inventory placement tracking.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Shipment Regulatory Check links inbound shipments to the regulatory_requirement governing hazardous material or import/export rules.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate inbound shipment with internal supplier master']
  supplier_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier_plant. Business justification: An inbound shipment originates from a specific supplier plant. Linking inbound_shipment to supply_supplier_plant captures the origin plant for logistics tracking, customs documentation, and plant-leve']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: LOGISTICS & WARRANTY: Associating inbound shipments with vehicle orders allows tracking of part receipt dates for warranty and delivery performance reporting.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real date and time when the shipment was received at the dock.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier supplied by the supplier to announce the shipment in advance.']
  container_count varchar [note: 'Number of containers included in the shipment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the freight cost.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by customs for the import declaration.']
  delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the agreed delivery time window at the receiving dock.']
  delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the agreed delivery time window at the receiving dock.']
  departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shipment left the supplier\'s dock.']
  estimated_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time of arrival at the receiving dock (ETA).']
  freight_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost charged by the carrier for transporting the shipment.']
  hazardous_class varchar [note: 'Classification of hazardous material according to UN/ADR standards.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International commercial term defining responsibility and cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAT|DAP|DDP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is expedited (true) or standard (false).']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials.']
  last_status_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the shipment status.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Classification of the materials contained in the shipment.']
  mode_of_transport varchar [note: 'Primary mode used to move the shipment from supplier to plant.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets used to load the shipment.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment record.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes entered by logistics personnel.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the inbound shipment.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'True if the shipment must be kept within a temperature range.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the required temperature range for the shipment.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the required temperature range for the shipment.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate volume of the shipment, expressed in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all items in the shipment, expressed in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Tracks an inbound shipment of parts from a supplier plant to an OEM receiving dock. Captures ASN (Advance Shipping Notice) number, carrier, mode of transport (road, rail, air, sea), departure date/time, estimated arrival date/time, actual arrival date/time, total weight, total volume, number of containers/pallets, customs declaration number, and shipment status (in transit, arrived, cleared, received). Integrates with SAP MM inbound delivery (VL31N).'
}

Table supply.supplier_scorecard {
  supplier_scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each supplier scorecard record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Supplier performance management is performed by quality or procurement cost centers that need to track supplier development costs and performance monitoring activities.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Connect supplier scorecard to internal supplier master']
  supplier_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier_plant. Business justification: Supplier scorecards in automotive are often evaluated at the plant level — a supplier may have multiple plants with different performance profiles. Linking supplier_scorecard to supply_supplier_plant ']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score measuring adherence to regulatory and internal compliance requirements.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of actions the supplier must take to address deficiencies.']
  corrective_action_flag boolean [note: 'True when the supplier must undertake corrective actions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the scorecard record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_quantity_accuracy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ratio of delivered quantity to ordered quantity, expressed as a percent.']
  evaluation_date timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the scorecard was completed.']
  evaluation_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar day of the period covered by the scorecard.']
  evaluation_period_start date [note: 'First calendar day of the period covered by the scorecard.']
  evaluator_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the internal evaluator responsible for the scorecard.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations captured during the evaluation.']
  otd_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries that arrived on or before the agreed delivery date.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated weighted score combining all KPI values for the evaluation period.']
  performance_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical tier assigned based on the overall score.']
  ppap_on_time_completion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of PPAP submissions completed within the agreed timeframe.']
  ppm_defect_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Defect rate expressed in parts per million for supplied parts during the period.']
  responsiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s responsiveness to inquiries and change requests.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the scorecard is still being drafted, has been finalized, or has been reviewed by management.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk rating derived from financial, operational, and compliance factors.']
  scorecard_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the scorecard, used for reporting and audit trails.']
  scoring_methodology_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scoring model version used for this evaluation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Enterprise system that originated the scorecard data.']
  supplier_scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the scorecard in its lifecycle.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s environmental and sustainability initiatives.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the scorecard.']

  Note: 'Periodic (monthly/quarterly) performance evaluation record for a supplier across key KPIs including PPM (Parts Per Million defect rate), OTD (On-Time Delivery percentage), delivery quantity accuracy, PPAP on-time completion rate, responsiveness score, and overall supplier rating. Captures evaluation period, scoring methodology version, individual KPI values, weighted total score, performance tier (preferred, approved, conditional, disqualified), and corrective action flag.'
}

Table supply.corrective_action {
  corrective_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_corrective_action data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Corrective actions incur costs (containment, sorting, rework, supplier visits) that must be allocated to quality or production cost centers for defect cost tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Supplier quality costs (scrap, rework, premium freight, containment) must post to specific GL accounts for cost of poor quality (COPQ) reporting and supplier chargeback.']
  inbound_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_inspection. Business justification: A corrective action is often triggered by a specific incoming inspection failure. Linking supply_corrective_action to inbound_inspection provides the quality evidence that initiated the SCAR, enabling']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: A corrective action is raised for a specific non-conforming part. Linking supply_corrective_action to inbound_part enables part-level SCAR history, repeat defect tracking, and part-level quality risk ']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: A corrective action may be raised against a specific inbound shipment that contained defective or non-conforming parts. Linking to inbound_shipment enables shipment-level quality traceability and supp']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Corrective actions are issued by a supplier; adding supply_supplier_id FK provides the necessary parent link and resolves the silo.']
  supplier_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier_plant. Business justification: Corrective actions are directed at a specific supplier plant\'s manufacturing process. Linking to supply_supplier_plant enables plant-level SCAR tracking and ensures the corrective action is routed to ']
  supplier_scorecard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_scorecard. Business justification: A supplier corrective action request (SCAR) is typically triggered by a poor scorecard evaluation. Linking supply_corrective_action to supplier_scorecard establishes the causal chain from performance ']

  Note: 'Supplier corrective action request (SCAR) issued when a supplier fails to meet quality, delivery, or compliance requirements. Captures SCAR number, triggering event (PPM breach, OTD failure, audit finding), root cause category, 8D report reference, containment action, permanent corrective action, verification date, and closure status. Drives supplier development and continuous improvement programs.'
}

Table supply.inbound_inspection {
  inbound_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each inbound inspection record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inspection activities are performed by quality cost centers that need to track inspection labor and equipment costs for quality department budget management.']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: inbound_inspection has part_number and part_revision as denormalized strings. Adding inbound_part_id normalizes this to the supply domain\'s part master. part_number becomes redundant; part_revision ma']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: An incoming quality inspection is performed on parts received in a specific inbound shipment. Linking inbound_inspection to inbound_shipment enables shipment-level quality tracking, lot traceability, ']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Inspection results are recorded per SKU to update quality status and determine stock availability.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: An incoming inspection is for parts from a specific supplier. Adding supply_supplier_id to inbound_inspection enables supplier-level quality analytics (PPM tracking, defect rate trends) without requir']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection record was first created in the system.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of defects identified in the inspected sample.']
  defect_rate_ppm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Defects per million units calculated for the inspected lot.']
  defect_type_codes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of defect classification codes (e.g., surface, dimensional, functional).']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Action taken after inspection (accept, return to supplier, sort and use, or rework).']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'Plant or line code where the inspection was carried out.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the plant for the inspection lot.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the inspection (AQL sampling, 100% check, or skip‑lot).']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the inspection based on defect criteria.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection record.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection was performed at the plant.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units selected for inspection according to the sampling plan.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the record (e.g., SAP QM, MES).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the inspection record.']

  Note: 'Incoming quality inspection record for parts received from a supplier at an OEM plant. Captures inspection lot number, inspection date, part number, supplier, sample size, inspection method (AQL sampling, 100% check, skip-lot), number of defects found, defect type codes, inspection result (pass, fail, conditional release), and disposition (accept, return to supplier, sort and use). Integrates with SAP QM inspection lots.'
}

Table supply.price_agreement {
  price_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the price agreement record.']

Linking price_agreement to commodity_group enables commodity-level price benchmarking, annual price reduction trackin']

Business justification: Fleet and commercial contracts often include pass-through pricing or customer-negotiated supplier terms. Tracks customer-specific supplier pricing agreements, enables customer cost transparency report']

A price agreement is for a specific purchased part. Adding inbound_part_id normalizes this to the supply domain\'s part master, enabling part-l']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Price agreements in automotive are negotiated per vehicle program/model, with annual price reduction commitments tied to model lifecycle. Linking price_agreement.model_id → vehicle.model.model_id supp']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_term. Business justification: Price agreements in automotive supply contracts specify payment terms (net 30/60/90, early payment discounts, milestone payments). Critical for cash flow management, supplier negotiations, and total c']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Price agreements impact profit center margins and material cost structures. Essential for segment profitability analysis and business unit cost management.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Pricing agreements for regulated commodities must reference compliance requirements that affect cost (conflict minerals reporting, REACH substance restrictions). Critical for total cost of compliance ']
  rfq_response_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.rfq_response. Business justification: A price agreement is the formal commercial outcome of an RFQ process — it is awarded based on a specific rfq_response. Linking price_agreement to rfq_response establishes the procurement audit trail f']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Price agreements are negotiated per part/SKU; linking provides pricing data for inventory valuation and costing.']
  sourcing_nomination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_nomination. Business justification: A price agreement is the commercial outcome of a sourcing nomination process. Linking price_agreement to sourcing_nomination completes the procurement chain: nomination → RFQ → response → price agreem']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Associate price agreement with internal supplier master']
  supplier_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier_plant. Business justification: Price agreements in automotive are often plant-specific — the same supplier may have different pricing from different plants due to logistics, tooling, and labor costs. Linking to supply_supplier_plan']
  actual_otd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured on‑time delivery performance of the supplier for the current period.']
  actual_ppm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured defect rate of supplied parts for the current period.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the price agreement by the OEM.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement indicating its primary purpose (e.g., price, cost, service, tooling).']
  annual_price_reduction_commitment decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage reduction in price committed by the supplier each year.']
  compliance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal compliance review for the agreement.']
  compliance_document_ref varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the compliance documentation attached to the agreement.']
  contract_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative defining the parts, volumes, and geographic regions covered by the agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the unit price.']
  early_termination_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement may be terminated before the end date without penalty.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement expires or is terminated; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the price agreement becomes binding.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing penalties for non‑compliance such as late delivery or quality breaches.']
  price_adjustment_trigger varchar [note: 'Condition that can trigger a price change during the agreement (material index, foreign exchange, or none).']
  price_agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose, scope, and any special conditions of the agreement.']
  price_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price agreement.']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required to exercise a renewal option.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Specifies if the agreement auto‑renews, requires manual renewal, or does not renew.']
  target_otd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual target for supplier on‑time delivery performance.']
  target_ppm decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable defect rate for supplied parts under the agreement.']
  termination_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required to terminate the agreement early.']
  tooling_amortization_terms varchar [note: 'Terms describing how tooling costs are amortized over the agreement period.']
  total_annual_volume decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum total quantity of the part that may be purchased under the agreement each year.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit used for the agreed price and volume (e.g., piece, kilogram).']
  unit_price decimal(18,6) [note: 'Negotiated price per unit of the part for the agreement period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the price agreement record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the agreement reflecting amendments.']

  Note: 'Formally agreed piece price and commercial terms between the OEM and a supplier for a specific part over a defined period. Captures agreement number, part number, supplier, effective date, expiry date, agreed unit price, currency, annual price reduction commitment (APR), tooling amortization terms, price adjustment triggers (material index, FX), and agreement status (active, expired, under negotiation). Distinct from the purchase order — this is the commercial price framework.'
}

  commodity_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the internal manager responsible for the commodity group.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the commodity group record was first created in the source system.']

  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the commodity group becomes effective for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the commodity group definition is no longer valid (null if indefinite).']
  hazardous_material_description varchar [note: 'Description of the hazardous characteristics or classifications for the commodity group.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity group contains hazardous materials.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity group contains confidential pricing or strategic information.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the commodity group is applicable across all plants and regions.']
  lead_time_max_days varchar [note: 'Longest typical supplier lead time for items in this commodity group.']
  lead_time_min_days varchar [note: 'Shortest typical supplier lead time for items in this commodity group.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the commodity group.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the commodity group is subject to regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., REACH, RoHS).']
  risk_factor varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of primary supply‑chain risks (e.g., single source, geopolitical, regulatory).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating (0‑100) for the commodity group based on supplier performance and material hazards.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that supplied the commodity group data (e.g., SAP_MM, PTC_Windchill).']
  strategic_classification varchar [note: 'Kraljic matrix classification indicating the strategic importance of the commodity.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting environmental impact and sustainability of the commodity group.']

  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure used for the commodity group.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the latest update to the commodity group record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the commodity group record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for changes to the commodity group definition.']
  volume_per_unit_cbm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Typical volume of a single unit within the commodity group, expressed in cubic meters.']
  weight_per_unit_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Typical weight of a single unit within the commodity group, expressed in kilograms.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that initially created the commodity group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for commodity_group. '
}

Critical for tracking quality program progress against fiscal cal']

  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: APQP plans are plant-specific — launch quality planning is conducted per manufacturing plant. apqp_plan has no FK to manufacturing.plant. Plant-level APQP tracking is required for multi-plant program ']
  platform_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.platform. Business justification: APQP plans in automotive are scoped to vehicle platforms for new platform launches (IATF 16949). Platform-level APQP tracking drives gate reviews and quality milestones across all models sharing the p']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: APQP plans govern production line launch readiness. apqp_plan has no FK to manufacturing.production_line. Production-line-level APQP tracking is required for SOP readiness gates and IATF 16949 manufac']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project. Business justification: APQP plans are executed as formal projects in automotive OEMs with project codes, budgets, and resource allocation. Critical for tracking APQP investment and milestone achievement within project portf']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: APQP plans are created for specific parts during new product introduction. PPAP submissions require part-specific APQP documentation including design records, process FMEAs, and control plans. Removes']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: APQP planning is executed per vehicle program; linking aligns plans with program metadata and eliminates program_name duplication.']
  actual_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured parts-per-million defect rate observed after validation.']
  actual_release_date date [note: 'Actual date when the product was released to production.']
  apqp_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the APQP process for the plan.']
  classification_type varchar [note: 'Category of the APQP plan based on product scope.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current IATF 16949 compliance status of the APQP plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the APQP plan record was created in the system.']
  documentation_link varchar [note: 'URL or path to the primary APQP documentation repository.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the APQP plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the APQP plan expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  eop_date date [note: 'Date when series production ended for the vehicle/program.']
  gate_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the milestone gate was completed.']
  gate_due_date date [note: 'Planned due date for the milestone gate.']
  gate_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the milestone gate.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the APQP plan.']
  milestone_gate varchar [note: 'Specific APQP milestone gate associated with the plan.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Description of actions taken to mitigate identified risks.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year (calendar year) of the vehicle model associated with the APQP plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or observations.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal owner or manager of the APQP plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the APQP plan, often used in documentation and reporting.']
  quality_goal_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target parts-per-million defect rate defined for the APQP plan.']
  responsible_team varchar [note: 'Team or department accountable for executing the APQP plan.']
  risk_assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the risk assessment for the APQP plan was performed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification for the APQP plan.']
  sop_date date [note: 'Date when series production started for the vehicle/program.']
  target_ppm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned PPM level to achieve during the APQP process.']
  target_release_date date [note: 'Planned date for the product release associated with the APQP plan.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the APQP plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the APQP plan record.']

  Note: 'Advanced Product Quality Planning master record governing the structured quality planning process for new vehicle programs and components. Tracks APQP phases (concept, design, process, validation, launch), milestone gates, responsible teams, and completion status per program/part. Aligns with IATF 16949 requirements and links to engineering change and SOP timelines.'
}

Table quality.fmea {
  fmea_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the FMEA record.']
  apqp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: FMEA is a core APQP deliverable in IATF 16949. Every FMEA is produced within the context of an APQP plan (Design FMEA and Process FMEA are required APQP outputs). Linking fmea.apqp_plan_id → apqp_plan']
  characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.characteristic. Business justification: FMEA analyzes specific product or process characteristics (CTQ/CTC characteristics). Each FMEA record focuses on the failure modes of a specific characteristic. Linking fmea.characteristic_id → charac']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: FMEA is performed based on design specs; linking provides traceability for risk analysis reports.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: FMEAs are authored at vehicle model level per AIAG-VDA FMEA methodology and IATF 16949. Model-scoped FMEA reports are required for design reviews and regulatory submissions. No existing model_id on fm']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Process FMEAs (PFMEA) are developed for specific production lines per IATF 16949 APQP requirements. fmea has no FK to manufacturing.production_line. PFMEA-to-production-line linkage is required for pr']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project. Business justification: FMEAs (DFMEA/PFMEA) are key deliverables within product development projects. Real automotive practice: FMEA completion tied to project milestones and gate reviews for budget release and program progr']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: FMEA must be linked to applicable regulatory requirements to verify compliance of failure modes.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: FMEA analyzes failure modes for specific parts/assemblies. Risk Priority Numbers (RPN), severity ratings, and mitigation plans are part-specific and required for APQP/PPAP submissions and ongoing risk']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: PFMEAs are developed at the operation/work-center level in automotive — each operation has failure modes analyzed. fmea has no FK to manufacturing.work_center. Work-center-level PFMEA is the primary I']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date the recommended action was actually completed.']
  analysis_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the FMEA analysis, used for tracking across systems.']
  analysis_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the FMEA is a Design FMEA (DFMEA) or Process FMEA (PFMEA).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the FMEA analysis received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or manager who approved the FMEA analysis.']
  cause varchar [note: 'Underlying cause(s) that could lead to the failure mode.']
  control_effectiveness_rating varchar [note: 'Score (1‑10) assessing how well the current control detects or prevents the failure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the FMEA record was first created in the system.']
  current_control varchar [note: 'Description of existing controls that mitigate the failure mode.']
  detection_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the detection rating.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method or technique used by the current control to detect the failure.']
  detection_rating varchar [note: 'Score (1‑10) indicating the ability of current controls to detect the failure before it reaches the customer.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the FMEA analysis is considered active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the FMEA analysis is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  failure_effect varchar [note: 'Impact of the failure mode on vehicle performance, safety, or compliance.']
  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Description of the way the component could potentially fail.']
  fmea_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the FMEA record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the FMEA.']
  occurrence_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the occurrence rating.']
  occurrence_rating varchar [note: 'Likelihood score (1‑10) that the cause will occur.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Proposed corrective or preventive action to reduce severity, occurrence, or improve detection.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version of the FMEA record after each update or re‑analysis.']
  rpn varchar [note: 'Calculated risk priority number (S × O × D) used to prioritize corrective actions.']
  severity_description varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation of the severity rating.']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Severity score (1‑10) reflecting seriousness of the failure effect.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the FMEA record (e.g., Teamcenter PLM, SAP QM).']
  subsystem varchar [note: 'Higher‑level subsystem or system grouping the component belongs to (e.g., Powertrain, Chassis).']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the recommended action should be completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the FMEA record.']

  Note: 'Failure Mode and Effects Analysis master record capturing potential failure modes, their effects, causes, current controls, severity/occurrence/detection ratings, and Risk Priority Numbers (RPN) for vehicle components, subsystems, and manufacturing processes. Supports both Design FMEA (DFMEA) and Process FMEA (PFMEA) per AIAG-VDA FMEA methodology. Tracks recommended actions and revised RPN after corrective actions.'
}

Table quality.control_plan {
  control_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the quality control plan.']
  apqp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.apqp_plan. Business justification: Control plans are mandatory APQP deliverables (APQP Phase 3/4 output). Each control plan is produced under a governing APQP plan. Linking control_plan.apqp_plan_id → apqp_plan.apqp_plan_id enables ful']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: Control plans are derived from design specifications; FK enables automatic extraction of spec requirements for quality control.']
  primary_control_inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Link to the detailed inspection plan referenced by this control plan.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Control plans are line‑specific for process control; required for the Production Control Review report.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Control plans support production lines that roll up to profit centers. Essential for allocating quality control costs by profit center for P&L reporting and operational performance analysis.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Control plans document process controls that satisfy specific regulatory requirements (ISO/TS 16949, FMVSS standards). Auditors and certification bodies require traceability from control plans to regu']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Control plans specify inspection and control methods for manufactured parts. Real-world automotive control plans (per IATF 16949) reference the specific SKU/part number being controlled for process va']
  vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: Control plans for finished vehicle PDI specify the compound location where inspections are performed. Automotive PDI control plans are compound-specific for process control and traceability.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Control plans are project‑tracked in the WBS for capital budgeting and cost control.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the control plan.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Employee identifier who approved the control plan.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the change control record associated with this plan.']
  control_method varchar [note: 'Statistical or inspection method used to control the characteristic.']
  control_plan_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the purpose, scope, and key characteristics of the control plan.']
  control_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the control plan per IATF 16949.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control plan record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan is retired or superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan becomes effective for production.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control plan is mandatory for the operation.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the characteristic.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the control characteristic (e.g., mm, psi). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mm|cm|inch|mm2|g|kg|psi|kpa|percent — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the control plan.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the control plan (e.g., CP‑2024‑001).']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of manufacturing operation the plan applies to.']
  reaction_plan varchar [note: 'Defined corrective actions if a measurement falls outside specification limits.']
  responsible_function varchar [note: 'Organizational function accountable for the control plan.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the control plan.']
  sample_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which sampling is performed.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of units inspected per sampling event as defined by the plan.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Desired nominal value for the controlled characteristic.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the control plan record.']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the characteristic.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the employee who authored the control plan.']

  Note: 'Quality control plan defining the process controls, inspection methods, measurement systems, reaction plans, and control characteristics for each manufacturing operation or assembly step. Links to PFMEA and inspection plans. Specifies sample sizes, frequencies, control methods (SPC, attribute, visual), and responsible functions per IATF 16949 requirements.'
}

  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Inspection plans are defined per production line to meet line‑specific quality standards; used in the Line Inspection Planning process.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Inspection plans enforce testing protocols mandated by regulatory requirements (crash test sampling, emissions verification). The existing \'regulatory_compliance\' text field should be replaced with pr']

Manufacturing quality operations require linking inspection procedures to the SKU being inspected. Removes denormalized part_number in favor']
  vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: PDI inspection plans are executed at specific vehicle compounds. Business process: compound-based quality gate planning for finished vehicles before customer delivery.']

  gauge_type varchar [note: 'Specific gauge or instrument type used.']

  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the measurement.']

  tolerance_lower decimal(10,4) [note: 'Minimum acceptable measurement value.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(10,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable measurement value.']

  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the inspection plan.']

  Note: 'Detailed inspection plan specifying the characteristics to be measured, measurement methods, gauges/instruments, tolerances, sample sizes, and acceptance criteria for incoming material, in-process, and final vehicle inspections. Supports incoming material inspection (IQC), in-process quality gates, and PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection). Linked to SAP QM inspection lots.'
}

The control plan defines the inspection frequency, sample size, and acceptance criteria that govern each inspection lot. Linking inspecti']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inspection costs (labor, consumables, equipment usage) post to specific GL accounts for quality cost accounting. Critical for cost of quality reporting, variance analysis, and financial statement prep']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Required for the \'Inbound Part Inspection Lot\' report that ties each inspection lot to the specific inbound part received.']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inspection plan governing this lot.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Inspection lots are plant-specific. Existing \'plant_code\' is a denormalized plain attribute. Proper FK to manufacturing.plant enables plant-level quality reporting and supports multi-plant quality ben']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Traceability: inspection lot results must be linked to the specific shipment for recall, OTD and quality KPI reporting.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Inspection lots are executed per part; adding sku_master_id supports the Lot Traceability Report linking lots to the specific SKU.']
  submitting_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Incoming inspection lots require tracking the submitting party (customer returns, supplier shipments, warranty parts) for accountability and traceability in quality management systems. Essential for r']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Needed for the \'Supplier Inspection Summary\' dashboard aggregating inspection lots per supplier.']
  vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: Inspection lots for finished vehicles are created at compounds during PDI operations. Business process: batch quality inspection and acceptance at compound storage locations.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Supports the Inspection Lot Traceability report, associating each lot with its vehicle order to ensure inspection results are linked to the correct customer order.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: Inspection lots are created at specific work centers (quality gates). Existing \'work_center\' is a plain denormalized field. Proper FK enables work-center-level quality performance reporting (PPM per s']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier associated with the material or component.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action must be completed.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action must be initiated for the identified defect.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection lot record was first created in the system.']
  decision varchar [note: 'Final usage decision for the lot after inspection.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for any defect identified during inspection.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the defect.']
  inspection_lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection lot.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the inspection.']
  inspection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why the inspection was triggered (e.g., SOP, PPAP, random sampling).']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection activity was performed.']
  lot_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the inspection lot was closed after final decision.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the inspection lot, often matching the SAP lot number.']
  lot_origin varchar [note: 'Origin of the lot (e.g., goods receipt, production order).']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lot being inspected (e.g., incoming material, work‑in‑process assembly, finished vehicle, prototype).']
  measurement_result_status varchar [note: 'Result of the measurement against specification limits.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the measured value (e.g., mm, V).']
  measurement_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantitative measurement recorded during inspection (e.g., dimension, voltage).']
  quantity_accepted varchar [note: 'Number of units/items that passed inspection criteria.']
  quantity_inspected varchar [note: 'Total number of units/items examined in this inspection lot.']
  quantity_rejected varchar [note: 'Number of units/items that failed inspection criteria.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes captured by the inspector.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the inspected unit, if applicable.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the originating document such as goods receipt, production order, or delivery.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection lot record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a quality inspection event triggered for a batch of incoming materials, WIP assemblies, or finished vehicles. Captures lot origin (goods receipt, production order, delivery), inspection type, quantity inspected, inspection start/end timestamps, assigned inspector, and overall usage decision (accept, reject, conditional release). Sourced from SAP QM inspection lot management.'
}

Table quality.inspection_result {
  inspection_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection result record.']
  characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspected characteristic or measurement point.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection lot (header) to which this result belongs.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or observations recorded by the inspector.']
  cp_value decimal(5,3) [note: 'Cp value calculated for the characteristic.']
  cpk_value decimal(5,3) [note: 'Cpk value calculated for the characteristic.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection result record was created.']
  deviation_amount decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric difference between measured value and nearest specification limit.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the measurement was taken.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates if the characteristic is critical to vehicle safety or compliance.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the characteristic within the inspection lot.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value for the characteristic.']
  measurement_accuracy decimal(10,4) [note: 'Accuracy specification of the measurement instrument.']
  measurement_condition varchar [note: 'Environmental condition during measurement.']
  measurement_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage at time of measurement.']
  measurement_location varchar [note: 'Physical location on the vehicle or part where measurement was taken.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the measurement.']
  measurement_resolution decimal(10,4) [note: 'Resolution of the measurement instrument.']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the measurement data (e.g., internal sensor, external lab).']
  measurement_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature in Celsius at time of measurement.']
  measurement_tool varchar [note: 'Tool or equipment used for the measurement.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated uncertainty of the measurement.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measured value (e.g., millimeter, kilogram).']
  measurement_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric value recorded for the characteristic.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the record (e.g., active, archived).']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Pass/fail outcome of the measurement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value for the characteristic.']

  Note: 'Individual characteristic measurement result recorded during an inspection lot. Captures the measured value or attribute outcome, tolerance limits, pass/fail status, measurement instrument used, and inspector ID for each characteristic within an inspection plan. Supports SPC data collection and statistical analysis of process capability (Cp, Cpk).'
}

Linking defect_record.inspection_result_id → inspection_result.inspection_result_id provides direct traceability from th']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Defect records must reference the canonical part master for root‑cause analysis; removes redundant part_number/name.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Defect records are tied to the specific production order where the defect was detected, essential for the Defect Tracking Dashboard.']

  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the part or component associated with the defect.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location.   vehicle_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.vehicle_build. Business justification: Defects are discovered on specific vehicle builds.   vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: Defects found during compound operations (PDI, storage damage, handling issues) are compound-specific for root cause analysis, corrective action, and compound performance tracking.']
  vehicle_handover_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_handover. Business justification: Defects discovered at handover during delivery acceptance inspection must be documented for acceptance decision, warranty determination, and responsibility assignment (carrier vs. compound vs. manufac']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order.   vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: REQUIRED: Recall & warranty management needs to associate each defect record with the owning customer\'s vehicle record to trigger service actions.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Defect records are tied to a specific VIN; FK to vin_registry allows traceability from defect to exact vehicle for warranty and recall actions.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: Defects are detected at specific work centers.
