  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate action taken to contain the defect and prevent further impact.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed action to eliminate the root cause of the defect.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action must be completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the defect record was initially created in the system.']
  defect_category varchar [note: 'Stage of the product lifecycle where the defect was detected.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the defect for tracking and reporting.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the non‑conformance or quality issue.']
  defect_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the defect record.']
  defect_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the defect by its origin or nature.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the defect was first observed.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Method or channel through which the defect was discovered.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the defective item after evaluation.']
  investigation_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the investigation was concluded.']
  investigation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the root‑cause investigation began.']
  is_repeat_defect boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this defect has been observed previously.']
  ppm_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Defect rate expressed in parts per million for quality metrics.']
  quantity_affected varchar [note: 'Number of units or parts impacted by the defect.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause analysis result describing why the defect occurred.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level indicating impact on safety, performance, or compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system of the defect data (e.g., Apriso MES, SAP QM).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the defect record.']
  vin varchar [note: 'VIN of the vehicle in which the defect was found.']

  Note: 'Operational record of a quality defect or non-conformance identified at any stage — incoming material, in-process assembly, final inspection, or field. Captures defect code, defect description, location on vehicle (zone/component), severity classification, detection method, quantity affected, containment action taken, and disposition (rework, scrap, use-as-is). Sourced from Apriso/Dassault MES quality control module.'
}

Table quality.spc_chart {
  spc_chart_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the SPC chart configuration record.']
  build_stage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.build_stage. Business justification: SPC charts monitor quality at specific build stages (e.g., weld SPC at body shop, torque SPC at final assembly). Stage-level SPC monitoring is required for IATF 16949 process control. spc_chart links ']
  characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.characteristic. Business justification: spc_chart.ctq_characteristic is a free-text STRING field that should be normalized to a FK reference to the characteristic master table. Each SPC chart monitors one specific CTQ characteristic. Replac']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: SPC charts are established to monitor CTQ characteristics defined in control plans. The control plan specifies which characteristics require SPC monitoring, the control limits, and the sampling freque']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: SPC monitoring activities incur costs (software licenses, training, statistical analysis labor) that must be allocated to cost centers. Essential for activity-based costing and quality department budg']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: SPC charts in automotive production are authored for specific vehicle models to monitor process capability (Cpk) during model production runs. Model-scoped SPC reporting is required for IATF 16949 pro']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: SPC charts monitor process capability for specific parts. Control limits, capability indices (Cp/Cpk), and process stability assessments are SKU-specific measurements required for production part appr']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: SPC charts monitor key characteristics of each work center; required for the Work Center SPC Dashboard.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SPC chart configuration was formally approved.']
  center_line decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target center line (mean) for the SPC chart.']
  chart_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the SPC chart for a specific CTQ characteristic.']
  chart_type varchar [note: 'Type of SPC chart used to monitor the characteristic (e.g., X‑bar/R, p‑chart).']
  confidence_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level applied when setting control limits, expressed as a percentage.']
  control_limit_lcl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical lower control limit for the chart, calculated from process data.']
  control_limit_ucl decimal(12,4) [note: 'Statistical upper control limit for the chart, calculated from process data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SPC chart record was first created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplies the raw measurement data for the SPC chart.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the SPC chart configuration becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the SPC chart configuration is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the monitored characteristic (e.g., mm, psi). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mm|cm|mmHg|psi|rpm|kg|%|mm/s — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for any extra information or remarks.']
  ocap varchar [note: 'Defined actions to be taken when the chart signals an out‑of‑control condition.']
  process_step varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the manufacturing process step where the SPC chart is applied.']
  process_step_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the process step (e.g., ASSEMBLY_01).']
  sample_count varchar [note: 'Total number of samples used to compute the chart\'s control limits.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which samples are taken for the SPC chart.']
  spc_chart_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context for the chart.']
  spc_chart_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SPC chart configuration.']
  statistical_method varchar [note: 'Statistical technique used to calculate control limits (e.g., Shewhart, EWMA).']
  subgroup_size varchar [note: 'Number of units in each sampling subgroup used for chart calculations.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Desired target value for the characteristic (center line target).']
  tolerance_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower acceptable tolerance bound for the characteristic.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper acceptable tolerance bound for the characteristic.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the SPC chart record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the chart configuration, incremented on changes.']

  Note: 'Statistical Process Control chart master record defining the SPC monitoring configuration for a critical-to-quality (CTQ) characteristic at a specific process step. Specifies chart type (X-bar/R, X-bar/S, p-chart, c-chart), control limits (UCL, LCL), center line, subgroup size, sampling frequency, and out-of-control action plan (OCAP). Supports IATF 16949 SPC requirements.'
}

  Note: 'Production Part Approval Process submission record for a supplier part or internally manufactured component. }

  Note: 'Quality system and process audit record capturing planned and unplanned audits conducted at plants, supplier facilities, or dealer service centers. Tracks audit type (system, process, product, layered process audit — LPA), audit scope, audit date, lead auditor, findings count by severity, overall audit score, and closure status. Supports IATF 16949 internal audit requirements and customer-specific requirements (CSR).'

Table quality.standard {
  standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the quality standard record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Quality standards (ISO 9001, IATF 16949) are adopted and certified at legal entity (company code) level. Critical for multi-entity compliance tracking, certification cost allocation, and regulatory re']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or plant scope where the standard applies.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the organization against this standard.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the standard record was first created in the system.']
  document_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored standard document.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Web address or repository link to the electronic version of the standard.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the standard becomes effective.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the standard expires or is superseded (nullable if open‑ended).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with this standard is mandatory for the organization.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the standard (e.g., ISO, IATF, EPA, NHTSA).']
  language varchar [note: 'Language in which the standard document is written.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the standard record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the standard.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory authority associated with the standard (e.g., NHTSA, EPA, UNECE).']
  regulatory_section varchar [note: 'Specific section or clause of the regulation that the standard addresses.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the latest revision of the standard was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Internal revision identifier for updates to the standard document.']
  standard_code varchar [note: 'Official alphanumeric code assigned by the issuing body (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949).']
  standard_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the scope, purpose and content of the standard.']
  standard_type varchar [note: 'Category of the standard (e.g., quality management, environmental, safety, emissions, regulatory, process).']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the quality standard or specification.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the standard record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the standard (e.g., 1.0, 2.3).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Reference master for quality standards, specifications, and regulatory requirements applicable to the business. Captures standard code (IATF 16949, ISO 9001, ISO 14001, FMVSS, ECE regulations), standard title, issuing body, version, effective date, expiry date, applicability scope (global, regional, plant-specific), and compliance status. Used to link quality plans, audits, and test results to governing requirements.'
}

Table quality.characteristic {
  characteristic_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for characteristic']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: Characteristics are defined and owned within inspection plans — the inspection plan specifies which characteristics to measure, their tolerances, and measurement methods. Linking characteristic.inspec']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Quality characteristics (critical/significant) are defined per vehicle model in IATF 16949 characteristic libraries. Model-scoped characteristic definitions drive control plans, FMEAs, and inspection ']
  parent_characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on characteristic (parent_characteristic_id)']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Quality characteristics (dimensions, tolerances, material properties) are defined per SKU. Inspection plans and control plans reference SKU-specific characteristics for measurement system analysis and']
  characteristic_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the characteristic within quality domains.']
  characteristic_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what the characteristic measures or represents.']
  characteristic_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the quality characteristic.']
  characteristic_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the characteristic.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the characteristic record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the characteristic record was first created.']
  criticality_level varchar [note: 'Business impact rating of the characteristic on product quality.']
  data_type varchar [note: 'Data type of the characteristic\'s measured value.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the characteristic becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the characteristic is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the characteristic is measured or evaluated.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to capture the characteristic value.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the characteristic (e.g., mm, kg, sec).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the characteristic.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating IT system or application that defines the characteristic.']
  target_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target or nominal value that the characteristic should achieve.']
  tolerance_lower decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable deviation below the target value.']
  tolerance_upper decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum acceptable deviation above the target value.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the characteristic record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the characteristic record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for characteristic. Referenced by characteristic_id.'
}

Table logistics.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each outbound vehicle shipment.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the carrier entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for freight cost allocation per shipment in the Transportation Cost Allocation Report.']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Required for Shipment Delivery Performance Report linking each shipment to its destination dealership; enables OTD and cost tracking per dealer.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Needed to post transportation expenses to the appropriate GL account in the General Ledger.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Parts Shipment Tracking requires linking each shipment to the specific part master to satisfy regulatory traceability and cost allocation reports.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Order fulfillment shipping links each shipment to its originating production order for traceability and cost accounting.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: A shipment follows a defined logistics route (origin-destination path). shipment has origin_location and destination_location as free-text strings. Adding route_id normalizes the route reference and e']
  vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: A shipment originates from or stages at a vehicle compound. shipment has origin_location as a free-text string. Adding vehicle_compound_id links the shipment to the authoritative compound master recor']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Order fulfillment tracking report ties each physical shipment to its originating sales vehicle order, essential for delivery status and revenue recognition.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Required for Shipment Planning Report to allocate vehicles by program; logistics teams need program context for routing and dealer allocation.']
  vessel_voyage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vessel_voyage. Business justification: A shipment involving ocean transport is associated with a specific vessel voyage. Adding vessel_voyage_id to shipment normalizes the voyage reference currently captured only as free-text (voyage_numbe']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the truck, rail car, or vessel used for the shipment.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real timestamp when the shipment was received at the destination.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real timestamp when the shipment left the origin plant.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification result for the shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'Standard container identification number when applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for customs clearance documents.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for any delay beyond the planned arrival.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location (city) of the destination dealer or yard.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the freight cost.']
  export_import_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is an export, import, or domestic movement.']
  freight_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross transportation cost before discounts or adjustments.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous goods.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International commercial term governing responsibilities and costs.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipment load.']
  net_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final freight cost after discounts and adjustments.']
  number_of_units varchar [note: 'Count of individual vehicles or kits included in the shipment.']
  origin_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location (city) of the origin plant.']
  origin_plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the shipment originates.']
  otd_flag boolean [note: 'True if the shipment arrived on or before the planned arrival date.']
  planned_arrival_date date [note: 'Estimated date the shipment should arrive at the destination.']
  planned_departure_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for shipment departure from the origin plant.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible shipment number assigned by SAP SD for tracking and reference.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment.']
  temperature_control_flag boolean [note: 'True if the shipment requires temperature‑controlled transport.']
  tracking_url varchar [note: 'Web link to real‑time shipment tracking information.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the shipment record.']
  vin_list varchar [note: 'List of VINs for all vehicles in the shipment, separated by commas.']
  volume_cbm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cargo volume of the shipment in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross weight of the shipment in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Core master record for each outbound vehicle shipment from plant to dealer or distribution point. Captures shipment origin (plant/compound), destination (dealer/port/yard), transport mode (rail, truck, vessel, CKD/SKD), shipment status, planned and actual departure/arrival dates, OTD tracking, and associated VINs. Primary operational entity for finished vehicle logistics. Sourced from SAP SD outbound delivery and MES traceability.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_leg {
  shipment_leg_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each shipment leg record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Multi‑modal shipment leg tracking requires a FK to the carrier entity for each leg.']
  goods_movement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_movement. Business justification: Enables Shipment Leg Cost Allocation: reconciles each logistics leg with the corresponding inventory goods movement for cost analysis.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: Each shipment leg represents a discrete movement segment that follows a defined route. Adding route_id to shipment_leg links each leg to the route master, enabling route-level performance tracking per']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent shipment (transaction header) to which this leg belongs.']
  vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: A shipment leg may originate from or terminate at a vehicle compound (staging area, yard). Adding vehicle_compound_id to shipment_leg normalizes the compound reference for leg origin/destination, enab']
  vehicle_transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_transport_order. Business justification: A shipment leg is executed under a vehicle transport order. While shipment_leg already has shipment_id, the direct link to vehicle_transport_order enables carrier instruction tracking at the leg level']
  vessel_voyage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vessel_voyage. Business justification: For ocean transport legs, the shipment leg is directly associated with a vessel voyage. Adding vessel_voyage_id to shipment_leg links the ocean leg to the voyage master, enabling voyage-level leg trac']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Allows detailed Shipment Leg analysis tying each leg to the VIN master record for emissions, fuel consumption, and compliance reporting.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real‑time arrival date‑time recorded by carrier or IoT device.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real‑time departure date‑time recorded by carrier or IoT device.']
  container_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping container or trailer used for the leg.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for this leg (freight, duty, handling, etc.).']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the leg cost.']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Category of cost represented by cost_amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment leg record was created in the system.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the customs clearance document for cross‑border legs.']
  customs_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance for the leg.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why a leg is delayed, if applicable.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical distance covered in this leg, measured in kilometers.']
  emissions_kg_co2 decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated carbon dioxide emissions for the leg.']
  fuel_consumption_liters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total fuel used by the vehicle during the leg.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling notes for the cargo on this leg.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the leg carries hazardous materials.']
  hazardous_material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of hazardous material (e.g., flammable, corrosive).']
  leg_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the leg within the multi‑modal shipment.']
  leg_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the leg.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of cargo load for the leg.']
  odometer_end varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the end of the leg (kilometers).']
  odometer_start varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the start of the leg (kilometers).']
  planned_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled arrival date‑time for the leg.']
  planned_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled departure date‑time for the leg.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of individual vehicles or units moved in this leg.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to containers or trailers.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo requires temperature‑controlled transport.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature setting for the leg when temperature control is required.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature setting for the leg when temperature control is required.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the leg.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport used for this leg (e.g., truck, rail, ship, air, pipeline).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shipment leg record.']
  volume_cubic_meters decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total cargo volume for the leg, measured in cubic meters.']
  weight_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total weight of cargo transported on this leg, expressed in metric tons.']

  Note: 'Individual transport leg within a multi-modal shipment, representing a discrete movement segment (e.g., plant to rail yard, rail yard to port, port to dealer compound). Tracks leg sequence, transport mode, carrier assignment, origin/destination facility, planned and actual departure/arrival timestamps, distance, leg-level status, and mode-specific details (rail car number/type for rail legs, vessel name/IMO/voyage for ocean legs). After merges, this product also carries rail car assignment details and vessel voyage references for their respective transport modes. Enables end-to-end multi-modal visibility for each VIN or batch.'
}

  load_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the load planning record associated with this transport order.']
  origin_vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: vehicle_transport_order has origin_compound as a free-text STRING. Transport orders must referen']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: A vehicle transport order is executed along a defined logistics route. Adding route_id to vehicle_transport_order normalizes the route reference, enabling route-level transport order analytics, cost b']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: A transport order represents the execution of a shipment; linking it to the parent shipment enables traceability of cost and status.']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Transport planning aligns with the specific sales vehicle order to ensure correct routing, timing, and cost allocation.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Needed for Transport Order Management to group orders by vehicle program, enabling capacity planning and compliance with program launch schedules.']
  vessel_voyage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vessel_voyage. Adding vessel_voyage_id to vehicle_transport_order links the carrier instruction to the voyage master,']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment was actually received at the destination.']
  carrier_contact varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the carrier\'s dispatch desk.']
  compliance_regulation_code varchar [note: 'Code of the regulatory requirement applicable to the shipment (e.g., IATF, EPA).']
  confirmed_pickup_date date [note: 'Date the carrier confirmed for vehicle pickup.']

  emission_co2_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated CO₂ emissions for the shipment based on distance and vehicle type.']
  estimated_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System‑estimated timestamp when the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination.']
  export_import_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is an export or import movement.']

  is_expedited boolean [note: 'True if the shipment is marked for expedited handling.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by logistics planners.']
  on_time_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment was delivered within the agreed on‑time window.']
  order_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transport order was initially created in the system.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the transport order by the logistics system.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transport order.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the transport order.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first captured for audit purposes.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  requested_pickup_date date [note: 'Date the shipper requested the carrier to pick up the vehicles.']
  shipping_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling or routing instructions for the carrier.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number provided by the carrier for real‑time shipment visibility.']

  transport_cost_tax decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component of the transport cost.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the shipment.']
  vehicle_count varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles included in this transport order.']
  vin_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of VINs for the vehicles being shipped.']
  weight_tons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total weight of all vehicles in the shipment.']

  Note: 'Transport order issued to a carrier for the movement of finished vehicles. Captures order number, issuing plant, carrier reference, transport mode, vehicle count, VIN list, origin compound, destination, requested pickup date, confirmed pickup date, and order status. Represents the contractual instruction to move vehicles and is the primary document linking shipments to carrier execution. Sourced from SAP TM or SAP SD.'
}

Adding vehicle_compound_id to logistics_delivery_schedule normalizes the origin compound reference, enabling compound-l']

  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Delivery Schedule Dashboard aggregates deliveries per program; linking ensures on‑time delivery metrics are tied to the correct vehicle program.']
  vehicle_transport_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_transport_order. Business justification: A logistics delivery schedule is executed via a vehicle transport order. While logistics_delivery_schedule already has shipment_id, the direct link to vehicle_transport_order enables schedule-to-carri']
  vessel_voyage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vessel_voyage. Business justification: For ocean/export delivery schedules, the planned delivery is tied to a specific vessel voyage. Adding vessel_voyage_id to logistics_delivery_schedule links the delivery plan to the voyage master, enab']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real timestamp when the shipment arrived at the dealer.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real timestamp when the shipment actually left the plant.']
  arrival_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the vehicles to arrive at the dealer location.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates the compliance status of the shipment with relevant regulations.']
  cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final cost incurred for the delivery after execution.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected cost for the scheduled delivery, before execution.']

  customs_document_required boolean [note: 'True if customs paperwork is required for the shipment.']

  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  last_mile_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the final leg of delivery to the dealer or customer.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between departure and arrival, calculated for performance analysis.']
  logistics_delivery_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the delivery schedule.']

  planned_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles (or units) scheduled for delivery.']

  Note: 'Planned delivery schedule for finished vehicles from plant or distribution compound to dealer network. Captures scheduled delivery windows, dealer allocation, vehicle model/trim/color mix, planned quantities, transport mode, and scheduling horizon (weekly/monthly). Supports OTD planning, dealer inventory replenishment, and logistics capacity allocation. Linked to dealer orders and production scheduling.'
}

Table logistics.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the carrier record.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Carriers operate as vendors with accounts payable relationships. Carrier master data must link to billing accounts for invoice processing, payment terms management, credit limit tracking, and financia']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Carriers must be assigned to company codes for legal entity segregation in multi-entity automotive groups; intercompany freight AP/AR, tax compliance (VAT/GST), and contract management require knowing']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Carrier qualification and audit management requires tracking which regulatory requirements (DOT, IATF 16949, ISO 14001, safety standards) each carrier must meet. Essential for carrier onboarding, perf']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the carrier\'s primary business address (optional).']
  average_cost_per_mile decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average transportation cost incurred per mile for this carrier.']
  average_on_time_delivery_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average percentage of shipments delivered on or before the promised delivery date.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the transport carrier as registered with authorities.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the carrier within the logistics network.']
  carrier_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic tier assigned to the carrier based on volume, reliability, and strategic importance.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Business classification of the carrier entity.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person for the carrier.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Telephone number for the carrier\'s primary contact.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the carrier\'s master service agreement (null if open‑ended).']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier for the carrier\'s master service agreement.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the carrier\'s master service agreement.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the carrier record is considered active for reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the carrier record is no longer active (null if indefinite).']
  environmental_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental compliance certifications held by the carrier.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Main type of equipment the carrier uses for shipments.']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Number of transport units (e.g., trucks, railcars) owned or operated by the carrier.']
  iatf_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current IATF 16949 quality‑management compliance status of the carrier.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the carrier\'s liability insurance.']
  insurance_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance company covering the carrier\'s operations.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or safety audit performed on the carrier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the carrier.']
  operating_regions varchar [note: 'Geographic regions (e.g., continents, countries) where the carrier provides service.']
  performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Overall performance score (0‑5) derived from on‑time delivery, safety, and cost metrics.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety performance rating based on regulatory audits and incident history.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Four‑letter code uniquely identifying the carrier in North American logistics.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the carrier\'s primary business address.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the carrier.']
  transport_modes varchar [note: 'Transport modes the carrier is authorized to operate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the carrier record.']
  website varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the carrier.']

  Note: 'Master record for transport carriers (road haulers, rail operators, ocean shipping lines, port logistics providers) engaged for finished vehicle and CKD/SKD kit logistics. Captures carrier legal name, SCAC code, DOT number, transport modes supported, operating regions, contract reference, insurance certificate details, IATF 16949 compliance status, carrier tier classification, and performance rating. SSOT for carrier identity within the logistics domain. Referenced by transport orders, shipment legs, freight invoices, damage claims, and rate contracts.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_performance {
  carrier_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each carrier performance scorecard row.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the logistics carrier whose performance is being measured.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.lane. Business justification: carrier_performance has lane_code as a free-text STRING — a denormalized reference to the lane master. Adding lane_id as a proper FK normalizes this relationship, enabling direct join to lane for cost']
  average_transit_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean number of calendar days shipments spent in transit for the period.']
  carrier_performance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the performance record is active, inactive, or archived.']
  claim_frequency varchar [note: 'Count of loss or damage claims submitted for the carrier on this lane during the period.']
  cost_per_shipment_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average transportation cost incurred per shipment for the carrier on this lane.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the carrier performance row was initially loaded into the lakehouse.']
  damage_rate_ppm varchar [note: 'Number of damaged units per one million units shipped during the period.']
  fuel_consumption_l_per_100km decimal(6,2) [note: 'Average liters of fuel used per 100 kilometers by the carrier on this lane.']
  lane_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the lane is domestic, export, or import.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional textual comments regarding carrier performance, exceptions, or observations for the period.']
  on_time_delivery_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of shipments delivered within the agreed delivery window, calculated independently of OTD for cross‑validation.']
  otd_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of shipments delivered on or before the committed delivery date for the period.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Composite score (1‑5) summarizing carrier performance across all metrics.']
  performance_month date [note: 'Calendar month (first day) representing the reporting period for the carrier performance metrics.']
  total_claims varchar [note: 'Aggregate count of all claims (damage, loss, etc.) for the period.']
  total_damage_units varchar [note: 'Sum of individual units reported as damaged during the period.']
  total_distance_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total kilometers driven or sailed by the carrier for shipments on this lane in the period.']
  total_shipments varchar [note: 'Count of individual shipments moved by the carrier on the lane during the period.']
  transit_time_compliance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of shipments that met the agreed‑upon transit‑time windows.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for the lane (e.g., truck, rail, vessel, intermodal, air).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the carrier performance data.']

  Note: 'Periodic carrier performance scorecard capturing OTD rate, damage rate (PPM), transit time compliance, claim frequency, and overall carrier rating per lane and transport mode. Tracks performance against contracted SLAs, flags underperforming carriers, and supports carrier qualification reviews. Aggregated at carrier-lane-period level for logistics procurement decisions.'
}

Table logistics.vehicle_compound {
  vehicle_compound_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vehicle compound (yard, port, or regional staging area).']

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vehicle compounds are internal facilities with operating budgets (utilities, labor, maintenance) that must be allocated to cost centers for expense tracking and budget variance analysis in automotive ']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Compounds operate under specific jurisdictional authority determining applicable customs procedures, environmental regulations, and safety standards. Essential for regulatory compliance tracking, cust']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Outbound logistics planning: vehicle compounds serve as the immediate post-production storage and PDI facility for a specific manufacturing plant. Compound capacity planning, plant-to-compound routing']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: PDI-capable compounds and distribution centers often operate as profit centers in automotive networks, generating revenue from value-added services; P&L reporting requires profit center assignment for']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Compound facility certification requires tracking specific regulatory requirements (environmental permits, customs bonded warehouse status, safety certifications). Critical for facility audits, custom']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the compound.']

  capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicles the compound can store.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the compound is located.']

  compound_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the compound in operational systems.']
  compound_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the compound (e.g., plant yard, port yard, regional distribution center, dealer preparation site).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the compound resides.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compound record was first created in the system.']
  current_occupancy varchar [note: 'Current number of vehicles stored in the compound.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the compound became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the compound is scheduled to cease operations (null if open‑ended).']
  is_pdi_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the compound can perform Pre‑Delivery Inspection (PDI) on vehicles.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the compound record.']
  vehicle_compound_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the storage compound.']
  vehicle_compound_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the compound.']
  waste_handling_capability boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the compound is equipped to handle hazardous waste.']

  Note: 'Master record for vehicle storage compounds, yards, and staging areas used in the outbound logistics network (plant yards, rail yards, port compounds, regional distribution centers). Captures compound name, location, type (plant/port/regional/dealer prep), storage capacity (units), current occupancy, operator, PDI capability flag, and compound status. Enables compound capacity planning and in-transit inventory tracking.'
}

Provides granular yard management traceability and supports compound throughput analysis.'
}

Table logistics.in_transit_inventory {

  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: In‑transit inventory status is reported per sales order to provide customers with accurate ETA and inventory visibility.']

Business justification: For vehicles in transit via ocean, in_transit_inventory should reference the vessel voyage to enable voyage-level inventory tracking. Adding vessel_voyage_id links the in-transit snapshot to the voyag']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Needed for Real‑Time In‑Transit Inventory report to associate each inventory record with the master VIN record for compliance and warranty tracking.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle actually arrived at the destination (if arrived).']

  transport_cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred for transporting the vehicle.']

  weight_tons decimal(8,3) [note: 'Weight of the vehicle or load in metric tons.']

  Note: 'Real-time and point-in-time snapshot of finished vehicles currently in transit between plant and dealer, including vehicles on rail, truck, vessel, or staged at intermediate compounds. Captures VIN, current location (last known compound or leg), transport mode, origin, destination, estimated arrival date, days in transit, and transit status. Critical for dealer allocation visibility and OTD monitoring.'
}

Captures port facility, vessel name, voyage number, arrival/departure dates, customs clearance status, homologation inspection status, PDI completion, port charges, and release authorization. Supports global distribution and import compliance tracking per UNECE and local customs regulations.'
}

  vessel_voyage_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the vessel voyage record.']

  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Vessel voyages operate under specific maritime jurisdictional authority determining applicable maritime regulations, customs pre-clearance requirements, and port authority rules. Critical for internat']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.lane. Business justification: A vessel voyage operates on a defined logistics lane (origin port to destination port). vessel_voyage has origin_port_code and destination_port_code as STRINGs. Adding lane_id links the voyage to the ']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real‑time date and time when the vessel arrived at the destination port.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real‑time date and time when the vessel left the origin port.']
  available_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Remaining vehicle capacity after bookings (capacity minus booked units).']
  booked_units varchar [note: 'Number of vehicle units already booked for the voyage.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for the voyage (e.g., charter fee, fuel surcharge).']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the voyage record was first created in the system.']
  destination_port_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter UN/LOCODE of the port where the voyage terminates.']

  planned_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for vessel departure from the origin port.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the voyage record.']
  vessel_capacity_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicles the vessel can transport on this voyage.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the vessel used for the voyage.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the voyage by the shipping line.']
  voyage_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the voyage.']
  voyage_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current voyage status, e.g., weather delay, mechanical issue.']
  voyage_type varchar [note: 'Category of vessel operation, such as Roll‑On/Roll‑Off (RoRo) or container.']

  Note: 'Master record for ocean vessel voyages used in finished vehicle export/import logistics. Captures vessel name, IMO number, voyage number, shipping line, origin port, destination port, planned and actual departure/arrival dates, vehicle capacity (units), booked units, and voyage status. Enables RoRo vessel capacity planning and export shipment scheduling.'
}

Table logistics.transport_rate {
  transport_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the transport rate record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Transport rates are defined per carrier; adding carrier_id creates the necessary relationship.']
  logistics_lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.lane. Business justification: Rates are also specific to a logistics lane; adding logistics_lane_id links rates to lanes and resolves lane\'s silo.']
  accessorial_charges decimal(12,4) [note: 'Additional fixed charges (e.g., lift‑gate, detention) associated with the lane.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rate complies with applicable transport regulations (e.g., IATF, EPA).']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type of container used for the shipment, if applicable.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the rate record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the rate amount.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the destination dealer or distribution center.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the transport rate becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the transport rate expires (null if open‑ended).']
  fuel_surcharge_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage surcharge applied to the base rate to cover fuel price volatility.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special conditions related to the rate.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the origin plant or warehouse where the shipment starts.']
  rate_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'Base monetary amount agreed for the lane before surcharges.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the transport rate contract.']
  rate_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the rate originated.']
  rate_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for the rate record to track revisions.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation for the lane (e.g., road, rail, sea, air).']
  transport_rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the rate contract.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit that the rate amount applies to.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the rate record.']
  volume_capacity_cubic_meters decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum volume that can be transported under this rate.']
  weight_capacity_tons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum weight that can be transported under this rate.']

  Note: 'Contracted transport rate master for carrier lanes, defining the agreed cost per unit (vehicle or container) by origin-destination pair, transport mode, vehicle type/size class, and effective date range. Captures base rate, fuel surcharge structure, accessorial charges, currency, and rate validity period. Used for freight cost estimation, invoice verification, and logistics cost planning.'
}

Table logistics.lane {
  lane_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lane']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lane cost analysis requires linking each logistics lane to a cost center for profitability tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record defining a logistics lane as an origin-destination pair for finished vehicle or CKD/SKD transport. Captures origin facility (plant/compound/port), destination facility (dealer/compound/port), transport mode, distance, standard transit time, lane status, and assigned primary/backup carriers. Serves as the reference for route planning, rate assignment, and OTD benchmarking.'
}

  finished_vehicle_stock_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_vehicle_stock. Business justification: Needed for Vehicle Handover Warranty Traceability: links handover record to inventory vehicle record for warranty and compliance tracking.']
  logistics_delivery_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_delivery_schedule. Adding logistics_delivery_schedule_id to vehicle_handover links the actual handover to the planned delivery s']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Dealer handover requires linking to the production order to support warranty registration and regulatory compliance.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue from vehicle handover is recognized in the responsible profit center for margin reporting.']
  receiving_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the receiving party (dealer or customer).']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Linking handover to shipment provides traceability from transport to dealer receipt.']
  vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: vehicle_handover records the formal handover from logistics/compound to dealer or customer. handover_location is a free-text STRING. Adding vehicle_compound_id normalizes the compound from which the h']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Final handover of a vehicle to the dealer is tied to the sales order for warranty activation and final invoicing.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Vehicle handover to dealer/customer requires program identification for warranty registration, OTA update enrollment, and post-delivery program tracking. No existing FK from vehicle_handover to vehicl']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Enables Vehicle Handover audit linking handover record to VIN master for warranty start and liability tracking.']
  acceptance_signature_status varchar [note: 'Status of the acceptance signature: signed, unsigned, or pending.']
  container_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipping container (if applicable) used for the vehicle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the handover record was initially created in the system.']
  customs_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the customs clearance document.']
  delay_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for any delay affecting the handover schedule.']
  emissions_kg_co2 decimal(8,2) [note: 'Carbon dioxide emissions generated during transport, measured in kilograms.']
  export_import_flag boolean [note: 'True if the handover involves cross‑border export or import.']
  fuel_consumption_liters decimal(8,2) [note: 'Fuel consumed by the transport vehicle during the handover journey.']
  handover_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the vehicle at handover: new, used, reconditioned, or damaged.']
  handover_fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross fee charged for the handover service.']
  handover_fee_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the handover fee.']
  handover_fee_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax for the handover fee.']
  handover_fee_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component of the handover fee.']
  handover_location varchar [note: 'Physical location (plant, compound, yard, or dealer site) where the handover took place.']
  handover_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned handover reference number used for tracking and communication.']
  handover_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the handover record.']
  handover_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the handover event occurred.']
  handover_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the handover scenario: dealer stock, retail customer, export, or import.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the vehicle or its cargo includes hazardous materials.']
  hazardous_material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of hazardous material, if applicable.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International commercial term code governing the shipment (e.g., FOB, CIF).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured during the handover process.']
  odometer_reading_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the moment of handover, expressed in kilometers.']
  otd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the handover met the agreed on‑time delivery target.']
  pdi_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking to the Pre‑Delivery Inspection record.']
  receiving_party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the receiving party is a dealer or an end‑customer.']
  temperature_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature control was required for the shipment.']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature maintained during transport, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature maintained during transport, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport used for moving the vehicle to the handover point.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the handover record.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty expires.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle warranty becomes effective after handover.']

  Note: 'Record of formal vehicle handover from logistics/compound to dealer or end customer. Captures VIN, handover date, handover location, receiving party (dealer/customer), handover type (dealer stock/retail customer), odometer reading at handover, handover condition, PDI reference, and acceptance signature status. Marks the transfer of custody and triggers downstream billing and warranty start events.'
}

Table logistics.load_plan {
  load_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the load_plan data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: A load plan is assigned to a specific carrier for execution. load_plan currently has no carrier_id FK. Adding carrier_id to load_plan links the load planning record to the carrier master, enabling car']
  consolidated_load_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on load_plan (consolidated_load_plan_id)']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Load planning cost estimates are budgeted against a cost center for transportation budgeting.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Finished vehicle load planning: load planners pull confirmed production orders to build outbound loads from the plant yard. Load plans must reference the production order to validate vehicle availabil']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.route. Business justification: A load plan defines how vehicles are grouped for transport along a specific route. Adding route_id to load_plan links the load planning record to the route master, enabling route-level load planning a']
  vehicle_compound_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.vehicle_compound. Business justification: A load plan is executed at a vehicle compound (the staging/loading facility). Adding vehicle_compound_id to load_plan links the load planning record to the compound master, enabling compound-level loa']

  Note: 'Load planning record defining how finished vehicles are grouped and assigned to transport units (truck trailers, rail autoracks, RoRo vessel decks) for a given shipment or transport order. Captures load plan number, transport unit type, vehicle count, VIN assignments, weight/dimension constraints, loading sequence, special handling requirements (e.g., EV battery transport restrictions), and plan status. Supports transport utilization optimization and loading dock scheduling.'
}

  carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.carrier']

  origin_plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Outbound route management: logistics routes for finished vehicle distribution originate from manufacturing plants. Plant-specific route optimization, carrier assignment, and regulatory compliance (cab']
  return_route_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on route (return_route_id)']
  actual_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Real‑time arrival timestamp recorded when a shipment reaches the destination.']
  actual_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Real‑time departure timestamp recorded when a shipment leaves the origin.']
  average_travel_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Historical average travel time for the route in minutes.']

  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current regulatory compliance state of the route.']
  cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average cost incurred to operate the route per shipment, expressed in US dollars.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the route record was first created.']
  destination_location varchar [note: 'Standard code of the ending location or depot for the route.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical distance covered by the route in kilometers.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the route definition becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the route definition is retired or superseded; null if open‑ended.']

  is_express boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the route supports express (time‑critical) shipments.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity affecting the route (e.g., road work, carrier audit).']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days between required maintenance checks for the route.']
  max_load_capacity_tons decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum payload the route can handle, expressed in metric tons.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form remarks or special instructions for the route.']

  route_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping identifier for related routes (e.g., region, corridor).']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the transportation route.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the route.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the geographic scope of the route.']
  scheduled_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Planned arrival date and time for shipments on this route.']
  scheduled_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Planned departure date and time for shipments on this route.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the route record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for route. Referenced by route_id.'
}

Table inventory.sku_master {
  sku_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each SKU master record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Inventory valuation account assignment. Every SKU in automotive manufacturing requires a GL account for financial posting of inventory transactions (receipts, issues, revaluations). Standard SAP/ERP p']
  material_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.material_specification. Business justification: Each automotive SKU must conform to an approved engineering material specification for REACH/RoHS compliance and supplier qualification. Direct FK enables automated compliance verification during rece']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Required for traceability report linking engineering part definitions to inventory SKUs for production planning and cost tracking.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory mapping: each part must satisfy specific regulatory requirements for reporting and certification.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the country where the SKU was manufactured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the SKU master record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for price fields.']
  customs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'HS or tariff code used for import/export compliance.']
  ean13 varchar [note: '13‑digit European Article Number for the SKU.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the SKU definition is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the SKU definition remains valid; null if indefinite.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the SKU is considered expired.']
  hazardous_class varchar [note: 'Regulatory hazard class (e.g., Class 1, Class 2) for the SKU.']
  hazardous_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is classified as hazardous under regulatory standards.']
  height_cm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the SKU in centimeters.']
  last_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Most recent purchase price recorded for the SKU.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from order to receipt of the SKU.']
  length_cm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the SKU in centimeters.']
  lot_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'True if inventory of the SKU is managed by lot numbers.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Grouping used for reporting and analysis of similar SKUs.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Technical classification of the SKU (e.g., RAW, HALB, FERT).']
  max_order_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered in a single transaction.']
  min_order_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered for the SKU.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Responsible planner or group for the SKU\'s MRP.']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'Material Requirements Planning type that controls how the SKU is planned.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is externally procured (E), internally produced (I), or mixed (M).']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the SKU (e.g., Engine, Body, Electrical).']
  product_subcategory varchar [note: 'Secondary classification within the product category.']
  reorder_point_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order.']
  safety_stock_qty decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum inventory level to protect against demand variability.']
  serial_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'True if each unit of the SKU is tracked by a unique serial number.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the SKU can be stored before it expires.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used across the enterprise to reference the SKU.']
  sku_master_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the SKU, including functional characteristics.']
  sku_master_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SKU.']
  sku_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the SKU.']
  standard_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Default price used for cost calculations and standard costing.']
  tax_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is subject to tax.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for inventory transactions of the SKU.']
  upc varchar [note: '12‑digit Universal Product Code for the SKU.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the SKU master record.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Key used for accounting valuation of the SKU.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical volume occupied by one unit of the SKU.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of a single unit of the SKU in kilograms.']
  width_cm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the SKU in centimeters.']

  Note: 'SSOT for all Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) definitions across the enterprise. Owns the material master record for raw materials, production components, WIP sub-assemblies, finished vehicles, and service parts. Aligned with SAP MM material master (MARA/MARC). Captures SKU identity, classification, unit of measure, weight/dimensions, hazardous material flags, shelf-life, and MRP planning parameters. Referenced by all inventory movement and stock transactions.'
}

Storage locations (warehouses, yards) in automotive plants are assigned to cost centers for overhead allocation, labor cost tracking, and operational expense reporting. Esse']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant to which the location belongs.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the location.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building).']
  agv_routing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority used by automated guided vehicles when selecting this location.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle designation within the zone.']
  bin varchar [note: 'Specific bin or shelf code for inventory placement.']
  capacity_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount of material the location can hold.']
  capacity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for capacity (e.g., kg, m3, units).']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the location is situated.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the location became active for inventory.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the location is scheduled to be retired (null if indefinite).']

  hazardous_material_allowed boolean [note: 'True if hazardous parts may be stored in this location.']
  inventory_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured accuracy of inventory records for this location.']

  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the location.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'External code used in ERP/WMS to identify the location.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the storage location.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the physical storage configuration.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the location.']

  temperature_range_celsius varchar [note: 'Allowed temperature range for the location (e.g., "0-5").']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  used_capacity_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current utilization of the location expressed as a percentage of total capacity.']
  zone varchar [note: 'Logical zone within the warehouse (e.g., receiving, bulk, cold).']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical and logical storage locations within plants, warehouses, distribution centers, and dealer parts depots. Captures location hierarchy (plant → warehouse → storage type → storage bin), location type (bulk, rack, floor, cold-chain), capacity constraints, and WM (Warehouse Management) configuration. Aligned with SAP WM storage location and bin master. Enables precise bin-level inventory tracking and AGV routing.'
}

Table inventory.stock_balance {
  stock_balance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each stock balance record.']

Stock balances must be assigned to company code for consolidated financial reporting, intercompany stock transfers, and legal entity-level inventory valuation. Requir']
