  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: stock_balance also references a storage location; adding storage_location_id FK normalizes location data.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch when batch management is active.']
  blocked_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity that is blocked due to quality or administrative reasons.']
  consignment_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity owned by a supplier but stored at the plant.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the valuation price.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the material is considered expired or unusable.']
  goods_movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the transaction (e.g., receipt, issue, transfer).']
  in_transit_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity that is currently being transferred between locations.']
  is_serialized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is managed at the serial number level.']
  last_movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent goods movement affecting this stock record.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the stock quantity (e.g., unrestricted, blocked).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of the material.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'Date the material was produced or assembled.']
  physical_location_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Concatenated path representing plant, warehouse, aisle, and bin (e.g., PL01/WH02/AIS03/BIN04).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site where the stock is held.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Most recent purchase order that affected the stock balance.']
  quality_inspection_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity currently under quality inspection.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest quality inspection for the stock batch.']
  quantity_on_hand varchar [note: 'Total physical quantity of the material available at the location.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock balance record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the stock balance record.']
  safety_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Reserved quantity to protect against demand variability.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the individual unit when serialization is enabled.']
  stock_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the stock for reporting (e.g., raw material, finished good).']
  supplier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the primary supplier for the material.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the stock quantity is measured (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  unrestricted_stock_qty varchar [note: 'Quantity that is free for use or sale.']
  valuation_area_code varchar [note: 'Accounting valuation area for inventory valuation purposes.']
  valuation_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value per unit for the material based on the valuation type.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory valuation.']

  Note: 'Current on-hand stock balance snapshot for each SKU at each storage location, plant, and valuation area. Captures unrestricted stock, quality inspection stock, blocked stock, consignment stock, in-transit stock, and safety stock levels. Aligned with SAP MM stock overview (MMBE / MARD). Supports MRP execution, inventory turnover analysis, and obsolescence monitoring. Updated by every goods movement transaction.'
}

Table inventory.goods_movement {
  goods_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each goods movement transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Goods movement postings require a cost‑center for internal cost allocation; finance cost_center is the authoritative source for reporting.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Period assignment for goods movements. Every inventory transaction must be assigned to fiscal period for period-end closing, cutoff control, and financial reporting. Essential for month-end/year-end i']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Direct posting account for goods movement. Each movement type (goods receipt, issue, scrap, transfer) posts to specific GL accounts for inventory accounting. Required for real-time financial integrati']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: Goods movements for supplier parts need inbound_part reference for supplier-specific cost, lead time, and PPAP status. Enables supplier part number traceability and purchase price variance analysis at']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Goods receipt movements originate from inbound shipments. Links enable supplier quality traceability, customs documentation reconciliation, receiving variance analysis, and ASN-to-GR matching for supp']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Goods receipts trigger quality inspection (QI stock in SAP 101 movement type). GR documents reference inspection lot numbers for quality-gated movements. Enables traceability from material receipt to ']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or component being moved.']
  primary_goods_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for goods issue movements.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order linked to the movement.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.recall_campaign. Business justification: Recall-driven goods movements require campaign traceability for NHTSA/Transport Canada reporting, segregation compliance, and audit trails. Enables tracking which movements were executed to quarantine']
  replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: Goods movements can be the physical inventory transaction that fulfills a replenishment order (e.g., a goods receipt posting that satisfies the replenishment demand). Adding replenishment_order_id FK ']
  reversal_of_movement_goods_movement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original goods movement that this record reverses.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Every goods movement event (GR, GI, transfer) is for a specific SKU. goods_movement currently carries material_number (STRING) as a denormalized SKU reference. Adding sku_master_id FK normalizes this ']
  source_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Normalize source storage location reference; replace string with FK to storage_location']
  stock_transfer_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_transfer_order. Business justification: Goods movements are the physical execution records generated when a stock transfer order is carried out. goods_movement has reference_document_number and reference_document_type (STRING) as denormaliz']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.work_center. Business justification: Goods movements (material issues to production) are posted against specific work centers for activity-based cost allocation and work-center-level consumption reporting. This is a standard SAP MM-PP in']
  amount_local decimal(15,2) [note: 'Value of the movement in the plant\'s local currency.']
  amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Value of the movement converted to US dollars for consolidated reporting.']
  base_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure associated with the quantity field.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods movement record was initially created.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Delivery document number for goods issue movements.']
  destination_plant varchar [note: 'Plant receiving the material.']
  destination_storage_location varchar [note: 'Storage location in the destination plant where the material is placed.']
  goods_movement_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the goods movement record.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'True if the movement was performed automatically by a system (e.g., AGV).']
  is_lot_tracked boolean [note: 'True if the material is tracked by lot.']
  is_serial_tracked boolean [note: 'True if the material is tracked by serial number.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordering number of this line within the goods movement document.']
  location_zone varchar [note: 'Specific zone or area within the warehouse for the storage location.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number used for traceability of batch‑controlled items.']
  movement_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason prompting the inventory movement.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the goods movement was posted.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the movement was posted.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material moved, expressed in the base unit of measure.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Number of the source document that triggered the movement, such as purchase order or production order.']
  reference_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of the reference document (Purchase Order, Production Order, Work Order, Delivery, Service Order).']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this record represents a reversal of a previous movement.']
  source_plant varchar [note: 'Plant where the material originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Inventory valuation method used for this movement.']
  warehouse_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse where the movement occurs.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every inventory movement event including goods receipts (GR), goods issues (GI), stock transfers, returns, and scrapping. Aligned with SAP MM material document (MSEG/MKPF). Captures movement type, quantity, source and destination storage locations, reference document (purchase order, production order, delivery), posting date, and batch/serial number. Provides full audit trail for lot traceability and IATF 16949 compliance.'
}

Table inventory.stock_transfer_order {
  stock_transfer_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock transfer order.']
  destination_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant receiving the stock.']
  replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.replenishment_order. Business justification: Stock transfer orders are frequently created to physically execute replenishment orders — the replenishment_order is the demand signal and the stock_transfer_order is the warehouse execution instructi']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: A stock transfer order governs the physical movement of a specific SKU between storage locations. stock_transfer_order currently carries material_number (STRING) and material_description (STRING) as d']
  source_plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant where stock is sourced.']
  source_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: A stock transfer order moves stock FROM a source storage location TO a destination storage location. While stock_transfer_order already has source_plant_id (cross-domain to manufacturing.plant), it la']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Transfer order creation is driven by a vehicle order; linking them supports the Transfer Order Planning report used in production logistics.']
  agv_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the AGV assigned to execute the transfer.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the material batch being moved.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the confirmation step before execution.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Internal cost associated with moving the stock.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center responsible for the transfer expense.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost amount.']
  execution_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the stock transfer was completed.']
  execution_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when physical movement of stock began.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free‑text instructions for special handling during transfer.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'True if the material is classified as hazardous.']
  is_jis boolean [note: 'Indicates if the transfer follows Just‑In‑Sequence sequencing.']
  is_jit boolean [note: 'Indicates if the transfer supports Just‑In‑Time replenishment.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch identifier for traceability.']
  movement_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the stock is being transferred.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the transfer order.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Project identifier if the transfer is linked to a specific project.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Amount of material to be moved.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  serial_number_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is tracked by serial number.']
  special_handling_flag boolean [note: 'True if any non‑standard handling procedures apply.']
  stock_transfer_order_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the transfer order.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature‑controlled handling is required.']
  transfer_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transfer order was created in the system.']
  transfer_order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the transfer order.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Category of stock movement represented by the order.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the transfer quantity.']

  Note: 'Warehouse Management transfer order governing the physical movement of stock between storage bins, storage types, or plants. Aligned with SAP WM transfer order (LT0A). Captures source and destination bin, transfer quantity, movement reason, AGV assignment, picker assignment, confirmation status, and execution timestamps. Supports JIT/JIS sequencing for line-side replenishment and inter-plant stock balancing.'
}

Table inventory.mrp_requirement {
  mrp_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the MRP requirement record.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: MRP requirements in automotive are generated by BOM explosion. Linking mrp_requirement to the specific BOM version used enables change impact analysis — when a BOM revision occurs, planners identify w']
  model_year_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_model_year_program. Business justification: MRP requirements in automotive manufacturing are planned against specific model year programs. Production planners run parts requirements by model year program to align supply with production ramp-up/']
  powertrain_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: MRP requirements for powertrain-specific components (EV battery packs, engine assemblies) are driven by planned production of specific powertrain variants. Supply planners must trace which powertrain ']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: MRP requirements are pegged to production orders in order-based automotive manufacturing (make-to-order). This SAP PP standard link enables order-level material availability checks, shortage analysis,']
  production_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_schedule. Business justification: MRP requirements in automotive are pegged to production schedules — the schedule drives demand that generates requirements. This standard SAP PP-MRP link enables requirement-to-schedule traceability, ']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Planning under regulation: MRP calculations consider mandatory regulatory constraints for material planning.']
  safety_stock_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.safety_stock_policy. Business justification: MRP requirements incorporate safety stock and reorder point values that are governed by safety_stock_policy. mrp_requirement carries safety_stock (DECIMAL) and reorder_point (DECIMAL) as denormalized ']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: MRP requirements are generated per material/SKU during the MRP planning run (MD04/MD05). mrp_requirement carries material_number (STRING) as a denormalized SKU reference. Adding sku_master_id FK links']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Link MRP requirement to storage_location for proper location tracking']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Automotive MRP requirements are program-specific; production plans are organized by vehicle program. Direct link enables program-level demand planning reports, launch readiness dashboards, and capacit']
  batch_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is managed in batches.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the demand that generated the requirement.']
  exception_message varchar [note: 'System-generated message describing any planning exception for the requirement.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned procurement or production lead time expressed in days.']
  lot_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum production or procurement lot size applicable to the material.']
  mrp_requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the requirement.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days covered by the planning run that produced this requirement.']
  planning_scenario varchar [note: 'Planning run scenario used to generate the requirement (e.g., MPS, MRP, DRP).']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the requirement applies.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the requirement for planning urgency.']
  quantity_required decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of the material required for the planning horizon.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requirement record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the requirement record.']
  requirement_date date [note: 'Date by which the material is needed to meet production schedules.']
  requirement_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the requirement by the MRP run.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the requirement is independent (external demand) or dependent (internal component need).']
  source_of_supply varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the material will be sourced internally or from external suppliers.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the required quantity.']

  Note: 'MRP (Material Requirements Planning) planned requirement record generated by SAP MRP run (MD04/MD05). Captures dependent and independent demand requirements for each SKU, planned order proposals, reorder points, lot sizes, lead times, and exception messages. Drives procurement requisitions and production orders. Supports safety stock calculation, demand smoothing, and supply gap analysis across the manufacturing network.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count {
  cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the physical inventory cycle count event.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant where the inventory is stored.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Cycle counts in automotive plants are scoped to specific production lines for lineside inventory accuracy audits required by IATF 16949. Linking cycle_count to production_line enables line-level inven']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: A cycle count is a physical inventory count event for a specific SKU at a specific storage location. cycle_count already has storage_location_id but is missing sku_master_id — a critical gap since a c']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical storage location (warehouse, plant, or depot) where the count took place.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC inventory classification driving count frequency.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the count was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Employee who approved the count after review.']
  book_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity recorded in the system (book) prior to the count.']
  compliance_iatf16949_flag boolean [note: 'True if the count complies with IATF 16949 inventory control requirements.']
  compliance_nhtsa_flag boolean [note: 'True if the count satisfies NHTSA traceability obligations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center associated with the inventory location.']
  count_date date [note: 'Date on which the physical count was performed.']
  count_document_number varchar [note: 'External document number assigned by SAP MM for the inventory count (e.g., MI01/MI07).']
  count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled counts for this SKU/location.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the count event.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the count is a full inventory, partial, or scheduled cycle count.']
  counted_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity physically counted during the inventory event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the count record was first created.']
  cycle_count_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to perform the count.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the count took place.']
  inventory_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the inventory item.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the count record is locked from further edits.']
  is_obsolete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is marked as obsolete.']
  last_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent prior count for this SKU/location.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of the counted material.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle or component associated with the SKU.']
  next_scheduled_count_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next cycle count based on ABC classification.']
  physical_inventory_doc_type varchar [note: 'SAP document type for the inventory count.']
  posted_by varchar [note: 'Employee who posted the count results.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the count was posted to inventory.']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recount was required after the initial count.']
  recount_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the recount attempt, if applicable.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or observations.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity at which a replenishment order should be triggered.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Predefined safety stock level for the SKU at the location.']
  serial_number_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the counted items are serialized (true) or not (false).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record, e.g., SAP WM.']
  storage_bin varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code of the specific bin or shelf within the location.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for quantity fields (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the count record.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance calculated as (variance_quantity / book_quantity) * 100.']
  variance_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Difference between counted and book quantities (counted - book).']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for any observed variance.']

  Note: 'Physical inventory cycle count event record for a specific SKU at a specific storage location. Aligned with SAP MM physical inventory document (MI01/MI07). Captures count date, counted quantity, book quantity, variance quantity, variance percentage, count status (planned, counted, posted, approved), counter identity, and recount flag. Supports inventory accuracy KPIs, ABC classification-driven count frequency, and IATF 16949 inventory control requirements.'
}

Table inventory.hold {
  hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory hold record.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.change. Business justification: Engineering change orders (ECOs) are the primary trigger for inventory holds on affected parts pending disposition. Linking hold to the triggering change enables ECO impact tracking — quality teams qu']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Holds placed due to pending homologation approval or certification issues require linkage to specific certification records. Supports EU type-approval delays, EPA certification blocks, and market-spec']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: Quality holds frequently originate from defective inbound shipments. Link enables shipment-level quarantine, supplier corrective action requests, and root cause analysis tied to specific ASN/delivery ']
  notified_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Quality or recall holds on finished vehicles or parts require immediate dealer notification when the hold affects dealer allocations or inventory in transit. Tracks primary dealer responsible for coor']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Inventory holds in automotive are frequently placed at the engineering part level (affecting all SKUs of that part) due to design defects or ECO dispositions. Direct link enables quality engineers to ']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Inventory holds triggered by production quality failures reference the producing production order for root cause analysis, cost assignment, and IATF 16949 non-conformance reporting. Traceability from ']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_recall_campaign. Business justification: Recall management: holds placed due to a recall must reference the specific recall campaign.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: inventory_hold blocks stock for a SKU; linking to sku_master eliminates duplicate SKU identifier.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or plant location where the hold applies.']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: Holds placed on specific customer-owned vehicles for warranty disputes, lemon law cases, or owner-specific quality issues. Complements recall_campaign_id for non-campaign holds. Supports customer-spec']
  actual_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold was actually released.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for the inventory affected.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments describing the hold context.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was created in the system.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Action to be taken after hold is released.']
  expected_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for hold release.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'System-generated hold reference number.']
  hold_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the hold was generated automatically by the system or entered manually.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hold.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of hold indicating reason source.']
  initiating_department varchar [note: 'Department that initiated the hold.']
  is_critical_hold boolean [note: 'Indicates if the hold is critical to production safety.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability.']
  quantity_held decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of items placed on hold.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code representing specific reason for the hold as defined by quality or engineering.']
  serial_number_end varchar [note: 'Ending serial number in the range held.']
  serial_number_start varchar [note: 'Starting serial number in the range held (if serialized).']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hold became effective.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the held quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the hold record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the hold record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the hold record.']

  Note: 'Inventory hold and block record restricting the use or movement of specific stock due to quality holds, engineering change notices (ECN), regulatory non-conformance, supplier PPAP failures, or recall investigations. Captures hold type, affected SKU/batch/serial range, quantity blocked, hold reason code, initiating department, hold start date, expected release date, and disposition decision (use-as-is, rework, scrap, return-to-supplier). Supports APQP/PPAP compliance and quality containment actions.'
}

Table inventory.replenishment_order {
  replenishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the replenishment order.']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.case. Business justification: Customer cases trigger emergency parts replenishment (warranty claims, service escalations requiring expedited parts to dealers). Links customer service needs to supply chain response, supporting case']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Replenishment orders are budgeted and expensed against a cost center for internal cost control.']
  destination_location_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consuming location (line‑side, assembly station, dealer parts room).']
  mrp_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.mrp_requirement. Business justification: Replenishment orders are frequently generated as a direct response to MRP planned requirements (demand signals from the MRP run). Adding mrp_requirement_id FK to replenishment_order creates a traceabl']
  primary_replenishment_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplying storage location (warehouse, supermarket, PDC).']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: Replenishment orders for lineside materials are triggered by specific production lines running low on components in JIT/JIS environments. Linking to production_line enables line-specific replenishment']
  safety_stock_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.safety_stock_policy. Business justification: Replenishment orders are triggered when stock falls below safety stock thresholds defined in safety_stock_policy. replenishment_order has trigger_source (STRING) which is a denormalized reference to w']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: replenishment_order requests stock for a SKU; linking to sku_master centralizes SKU attributes.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the replenishment was physically received at the destination.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the replenished material.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost of a single unit of the SKU at the time of order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the replenishment order record was first created in the system.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The moment the replenishment request was generated (e.g., pull signal from the shop floor).']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment state of the order.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates if the SKU is a critical component requiring priority handling.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the replenishment order record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days from order release to delivery.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for batch‑controlled items, if applicable.']
  max_stock_level varchar [note: 'Maximum allowable inventory level for the SKU at the source location.']
  min_stock_level varchar [note: 'Minimum desired inventory level for the SKU at the source location.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the replenishment.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the replenishment order.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the replenishment order.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Methodology used to trigger the replenishment (e.g., kanban, min‑max, JIT pull, JIS sequence).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the order to influence fulfillment sequencing.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Date promised by the supplying location for delivery.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Whether the order was generated automatically by the system or entered manually.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the consuming location expects the replenishment.']
  requested_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the SKU requested for replenishment.']
  safety_stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the SKU kept as safety stock at the source location.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number for individually tracked high‑value components (e.g., ECUs, batteries).']
  trigger_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the trigger that generated the replenishment request.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the requested quantity (e.g., each, kilogram, liter, box).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the replenishment order record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the replenishment order record.']

  Note: 'Internal replenishment order triggering stock movement from a supplying storage location (warehouse, supermarket, PDC) to a consuming location (line-side, assembly station, dealer parts room). Captures replenishment type (kanban, min-max, JIT pull, JIS sequence), trigger source, requested SKU and quantity, source and destination locations, priority, requested delivery time, and fulfillment status. Supports lean manufacturing pull systems and dealer parts replenishment cycles.'
}

Table inventory.safety_stock_policy {
  safety_stock_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the safety stock policy record.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: safety_stock_policy defines policies per SKU; linking to sku_master eliminates redundant sku_code.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Safety stock policies are defined per SKU per location (the policy description explicitly states \'minimum stock buffer for each SKU at each [location]\'). safety_stock_policy already has sku_master_id ']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Safety stock policies in automotive are program-specific — launch programs require higher safety stock buffers than steady-state production. Linking safety_stock_policy to vehicle_program enables prog']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the safety stock policy record was first created.']
  demand_variability_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Coefficient representing demand volatility used in safety stock calculation (percentage).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the policy becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the policy expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  last_recalculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the safety stock values were last computed.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from order placement to receipt for the SKU.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Stage of the policy within its approval and implementation lifecycle.']
  maximum_stock_level decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper bound of inventory allowed for the SKU to avoid overstock.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale associated with the policy.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant or production site where the policy is enforced.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier or code assigned to the safety stock policy.']
  policy_type varchar [note: 'Category of inventory control the policy addresses.']
  reorder_point decimal(15,2) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a replenishment order for the SKU.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Frequency, in days, at which the safety stock parameters are reviewed and potentially recalculated.']
  safety_stock_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine safety stock (statistical, fixed amount, or dynamic algorithm).']
  safety_stock_policy_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the safety stock policy.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(15,2) [note: 'Calculated buffer quantity to be kept on hand for the SKU at the specified location.']
  safety_stock_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the safety stock values – generated by the system, entered manually, or supplied by an external tool.']
  service_level_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired fill‑rate or service level expressed as a percentage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the policy record.']

  Note: 'Safety stock and reorder point policy record defining the minimum stock buffer for each SKU at each plant/location to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Captures safety stock quantity, reorder point, maximum stock level, replenishment lead time, demand variability factor, service level target, review cycle, and last recalculation date. Drives MRP safety stock parameters and supports supply chain resilience planning.'
}

Table customer.party {
  party_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the party record.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the party\'s primary mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the party\'s primary mailing address (optional).']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the primary address (e.g., billing, shipping).']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the primary mailing address.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for outbound communications.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the primary mailing address.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External credit rating assigned to the party for risk assessment.']
  customer_lifetime_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total net revenue expected from the party over the relationship.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification for analytics and targeting.']
  data_residency_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the party\'s personal data is stored to satisfy data‑sovereignty requirements.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of an individual party, used for age verification and KYC.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for electronic communication.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Additional external reference used for cross‑system reconciliation.']
  gdpr_consent_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has consented to receive email communications under GDPR.']
  gdpr_consent_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has consented to receive SMS communications under GDPR.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date the organization was legally formed.']
  industry_classification varchar [note: 'Standard industry code (NAICS or SIC) describing the party\'s primary business.']
  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party is exempt from sales tax.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of KYC verification for the party.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the party\'s information was last validated against source data.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual or organization as recorded for contracts and compliance.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the party within the business relationship.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level within the loyalty program.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the party has consented to receive marketing communications.']
  net_promoter_score varchar [note: 'Latest NPS rating captured for the party.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by business users about the party.']
  onboarding_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the party was onboarded.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the party was first created in the system.']
  party_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the party is an individual customer or an organization (e.g., fleet operator, dealer).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for voice or SMS contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the primary mailing address.']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code the party prefers for billing and pricing.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code for the party\'s preferred communication language.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Method most frequently used to reach the party.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the party record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the party record.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number for an organization (e.g., corporate registration, dealer license).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the party record (e.g., Salesforce, CDK).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the party in the source system.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the primary mailing address.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or code for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier (e.g., EIN, VAT) for the party.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Doing‑business‑as name or brand name used by the organization.']

  Note: 'SSOT master entity for all customer identities served by Automotive — retail buyers (B2C), fleet operators, corporate accounts, government entities, and dealer principals. Stores the unified identity record including party type (individual/organization), legal name, tax identification, registration number, preferred language, preferred currency, date of birth (for individuals), incorporation date (for organizations), industry classification (NAICS/SIC), credit rating, KYC status, GDPR consent flags, data residency region, source system (Salesforce Automotive Cloud party ID), onboarding channel, onboarding date, lifecycle status (prospect/active/inactive/churned), and last verified date. This is the anchor entity for all customer-domain relationships.'
}

  payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_plan. Business justification: Retail customers enroll in payment plans for vehicle financing, extended warranties, and service contracts. Individual customer records must track active payment plan enrollment for credit decisioning']

  country_of_residence varchar [note: 'Country where the individual currently resides.']
  customer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the individual as B2C, B2B, government, or fleet.']
  driver_license_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the driver license.']
  driver_license_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued driver license identifier.']

  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the individual.']

  marketing_opt_in_email boolean [note: 'True if the individual has consented to receive marketing emails.']
  marketing_opt_in_phone boolean [note: 'True if the individual has consented to receive marketing phone calls.']
  marketing_opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'True if the individual has consented to receive marketing SMS messages.']

  nationality varchar [note: 'Country of citizenship of the individual.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Latest NPS rating provided by the individual (‑100 to 100).']

  preferred_contact_method varchar [note: 'Individual\'s preferred channel for communications.']

  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the individual record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the individual record.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment used for marketing and analytics (e.g., luxury, economy).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the record (e.g., Salesforce, SAP).']
  suffix varchar [note: 'Suffix such as Jr., Sr., III, if applicable.']
  vehicle_ownership_count varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles currently owned by the individual.']

  Note: 'B2C retail customer profile extending the party master for natural persons. Captures personal identity attributes: first name, middle name, last name, suffix, gender, marital status, employment status, occupation, annual income band, education level, driver license number, driver license state/country, driver license expiry, primary spoken language, nationality, country of residence, household_id (FK to household), loyalty tier, NPS score (latest), CLTV estimate, preferred contact method, marketing opt-in flags, and Salesforce Contact ID. Supports personalized marketing, loyalty management, and customer journey analytics for retail vehicle buyers.'
}

Enables B2B fleet sales, government procurement, and corporate account management.'

Table customer.contact_point {
  contact_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each contact point record.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Contact points belong to a party (customer or organization). Adding contact_point.party_id creates the required inbound link and eliminates isolation.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the mailing address (optional).']
  channel varchar [note: 'Preferred channel associated with the contact point for outbound messaging.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the mailing address.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Business classification indicating the nature of the party owning the contact point.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Type of communications the party consents to receive via this contact point.']
  contact_point_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact point.']
  contact_point_type varchar [note: 'Category of the contact point indicating the medium (e.g., email, phone, mailing address, social media handle).']
  contact_point_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The raw value of the contact point (e.g., email address, phone number, full mailing address, social handle).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the mailing address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contact point record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the contact point becomes valid for use.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Email address used for electronic communication.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the contact point is no longer valid.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact point is the primary method for reaching the party.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'True when the contact point has been successfully verified.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communications sent to this contact point.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent timestamp when this contact point was used for communication.']
  opt_in_status boolean [note: 'True when the party has consented to receive communications via this contact point.']
  opt_out_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the party withdrew consent for this contact point.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Telephone number including country code, used for voice or SMS contact.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the mailing address.']
  preferred_contact_hours varchar [note: 'Typical daily time window (e.g., 09:00-17:00) when the party prefers to be contacted.']
  preferred_time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier representing the party\'s preferred time zone for contact.']
  social_handle varchar [note: 'Identifier for the party on a social media platform (e.g., @user).']
  social_platform varchar [note: 'Social media platform associated with the social handle.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the contact point record (e.g., Salesforce, CDK).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the mailing address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contact point record.']
  verification_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contact point was verified.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the contact point (e.g., email link, SMS code).']

  Note: 'Stores all contact information for a party (individual or organization) including email addresses, phone numbers, mailing addresses, and social media handles. Each record captures: contact point type (email/phone/address/social), contact point value, is_primary flag, is_verified flag, verification date, verification method, opt-in status, opt-out date, channel (voice/SMS/email/mail/push), preferred time zone, preferred contact hours, source system, and effective/expiry dates. Supports omnichannel communication, regulatory compliance (TCPA, CAN-SPAM, GDPR), and Salesforce Automotive Cloud contact synchronization.'
}

Table customer.vehicle_ownership {
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key uniquely identifying each vehicle ownership record.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Vehicle sales and deliveries generate AR invoices for retail/fleet customers. Linking ownership to invoice enables revenue recognition validation, warranty reserve calculation, and sales commission re']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.configuration. Business justification: When a customer purchases a vehicle, the exact commercial configuration (trim, color, powertrain, options) must be recorded on the ownership record for warranty claim processing, recall targeting by c']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Required for warranty/service eligibility reports linking each ownership record to the dealer that sold the vehicle; dealer_code is denormalized and replaced.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Fleet and commercial customers capitalize owned vehicles as fixed assets. Linking ownership to fixed_asset record supports depreciation tracking, asset disposal accounting, and lease-vs-buy analysis—e']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: Vehicle ownership records must reference the homologation approval for the specific vehicle configuration owned. Required for warranty validation, recall eligibility determination, and emissions compl']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (party) that owns or leases the vehicle.']
  production_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_variant. Business justification: Recall management, OTA software update targeting, and service campaign execution require knowing the exact production variant a customer owns. Regulatory recall notices (NHTSA/UNECE) must identify aff']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Links ownership record to the original sales order, enabling warranty, service, and revenue attribution reports.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Required for warranty, recall and production tracking reports that map each owned vehicle to its engineering program.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Required for ownership verification and warranty claim processing; links each ownership record to the VIN registry entry to retrieve vehicle specs.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'Source through which the vehicle was obtained.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the customer acquired the vehicle (purchase, lease start, etc.).']
  acquisition_dealer_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer that facilitated the acquisition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ownership record was first created.']
  current_odometer varchar [note: 'Most recent mileage reported for the vehicle.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle was disposed of (sold, traded, etc.).']
  disposition_odometer varchar [note: 'Mileage recorded at the time of disposition.']
  disposition_type varchar [note: 'How the ownership relationship ended.']
  insurance_carrier varchar [note: 'Company providing the vehicle\'s insurance coverage.']
  insurance_expiry date [note: 'Date the insurance coverage ends.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the insurance policy covering the vehicle.']
  is_primary_vehicle boolean [note: 'True if this vehicle is the primary vehicle for the customer household.']
  lien_holder_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution or entity holding a lien on the vehicle.']
  odometer_at_acquisition varchar [note: 'Vehicle mileage recorded at the time of acquisition.']
  ownership_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the ownership agreement between the customer and the manufacturer/dealer.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the vehicle is held by the customer.']
  purchase_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount paid by the customer for the vehicle at acquisition.']
  registration_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the registration jurisdiction.']
  registration_expiry date [note: 'Date the vehicle registration expires.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official license plate number assigned to the vehicle.']
  registration_state varchar [note: 'State or province where the vehicle is registered.']
  title_number varchar [note: 'Legal document number proving ownership.']
  title_state varchar [note: 'State that issued the title.']
  trade_in_vin varchar [note: 'VIN of a vehicle traded in as part of the acquisition, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ownership record.']
  vehicle_ownership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the ownership record.']

  Note: 'Tracks the ownership history of vehicles by customers — the authoritative record linking a customer (party) to a VIN. Captures: VIN, party_id (owner), ownership type (retail purchase/lease/fleet/government), acquisition date, acquisition channel (dealer/direct/auction/fleet), acquisition dealer code, purchase price, trade-in VIN, registration state/country, registration number, registration expiry, title number, title state, lien holder name, insurance carrier, insurance policy number, insurance expiry, odometer at acquisition, current odometer (last reported), is_primary_vehicle flag, disposition type (sold/traded/totaled/repossessed), disposition date, and disposition odometer. SSOT for customer-vehicle relationship; cross-references vehicle domain VIN master.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_membership {
  loyalty_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the loyalty membership record.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: Loyalty programs require billing account linkage for consolidated statements, points-based payment offsets, and redemption processing. OEMs integrate loyalty benefits into customer billing cycles for ']
  primary_loyalty_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (party) that holds the membership.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty membership record was first created.']
  current_tier varchar [note: 'Current loyalty tier of the member based on points qualification.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer enrolled (e.g., online, in‑store).']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date the customer enrolled in the loyalty program.']
  is_primary_member boolean [note: 'Indicates if this member is the primary account holder in a household linkage.']
  last_activity_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent activity (e.g., point accrual, redemption) by the member.']
  last_redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent points redemption.']
  lifetime_points_earned decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total points the member has earned over the lifetime of the membership.']
  membership_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the membership itself expires if not renewed.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the member within the loyalty program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes about the membership.']
  points_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current balance of loyalty points available for redemption.']
  points_balance_last_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Points balance at the end of the previous fiscal year.']
  points_earned_this_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Points accrued by the member during the current calendar year.']
  points_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current points balance will expire if not used.']
  points_redeemed_this_year decimal(12,2) [note: 'Points redeemed by the member during the current calendar year.']
  preferred_redemption_category varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred category for redeeming points.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the membership within the loyalty program.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the record for data‑governance purposes.']
  redemption_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is currently eligible to redeem points.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code used by the member to refer new customers.']
  salesforce_loyalty_member_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the member record in Salesforce Automotive Cloud Loyalty Management.']
  tier_benefits_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of benefits associated with the current tier.']
  tier_expiry_date date [note: 'Date the current tier expires if not renewed.']
  tier_points_required decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of points required to reach the next tier level.']
  tier_qualification_date date [note: 'Date the member qualified for the current tier.']
  total_points_redeemed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate number of points the member has redeemed.']
  total_redemptions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of redemption transactions performed by the member.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the loyalty membership record.']

  Note: 'Manages customer enrollment and status in Automotive loyalty programs (e.g., owner rewards, EV early adopter program, fleet loyalty). Captures: program_id, party_id, membership number, enrollment date, enrollment channel, current tier (bronze/silver/gold/platinum), tier qualification date, tier expiry date, points balance, lifetime points earned, points expiry date, redemption eligibility flag, referral code, referred_by party_id, preferred redemption category, program status (active/suspended/expired/cancelled), and Salesforce Loyalty Management program member ID. Enables tier-based benefits, points accrual/redemption, and retention marketing.'
}

Table customer.preference {
  preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each preference entry.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (person or organization) to whom the preference applies.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for delivering communications when the preference_category is \'communication\'.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence level (0.0000‑1.0000) that the stored preference accurately reflects the customer\'s intent.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the preference record was first created in the system.']
  data_origin_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the source system that supplied the preference record.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the preference becomes active.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the preference is no longer valid; null if indefinite.']
  is_opt_out boolean [note: 'True if the customer has explicitly opted out of the indicated preference; otherwise false.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the preference record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form comments or remarks about the preference.']
  preference_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the preference (e.g., communication, vehicle feature, service scheduling, digital experience, privacy).']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the preference (e.g., active, inactive, expired, pending review).']
  preference_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value assigned to the preference key; stored as text and interpreted according to value_data_type.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the preference record: self‑declared by the customer, inferred by analytics, or imported from an external system.']
  value_data_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the native data type of preference_value (e.g., string, boolean, integer, list).']

  Note: 'Stores customer-declared and inferred preferences for communications, vehicle features, service scheduling, and digital experiences. Each record captures: party_id, preference category (communication/vehicle/service/digital/privacy), preference key (e.g., preferred_fuel_type, preferred_body_style, preferred_service_day, email_frequency), preference value, value data type (string/boolean/integer/list), source (self-declared/inferred/imported), confidence score, effective date, expiry date, and last updated timestamp. Supports personalized marketing, product recommendation engines, and connected vehicle feature configuration.'
}

Table customer.nps_response {
  nps_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each NPS survey response record.']
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_repair_order. Business justification: NPS surveys in automotive aftersales are triggered by specific service visits. Linking NPS responses to repair orders enables satisfaction tracking by service event, technician performance analysis, a']
  case_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service case created if the response was escalated.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (person or organization) who provided the NPS response.']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: NPS surveys in automotive are frequently triggered by vehicle purchase events, service visits, or ownership milestones — all of which are tied to a specific vehicle ownership record. nps_response curr']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Automotive NPS surveys are tied to vehicle programs (model launches, model years) so program teams can track customer satisfaction by program and prioritize engineering improvements. Program managers ']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the NPS response record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Code of the dealer or service location linked to the survey touchpoint.']
  follow_up_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the response requires follow‑up action (true) or not (false).']
  follow_up_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any follow‑up activity triggered by the response.']
  nps_response_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the NPS response record.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating provided by the respondent on a 0‑10 scale.']
  promoter_category varchar [note: 'Categorization of the NPS score: promoter (9‑10), passive (7‑8), or detractor (0‑6).']
  response_date date [note: 'Date on which the respondent submitted the completed survey.']
  salesforce_survey_response_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the response record in Salesforce Survey system.']
  survey_channel varchar [note: 'Medium through which the survey was delivered to the respondent.']
  survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the survey was presented to the respondent.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the NPS survey is transactional (post‑purchase) or relational (brand experience).']
  touchpoint varchar [note: 'Business interaction point at which the NPS survey was triggered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the NPS response record.']
  verbatim_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑text comment supplied by the respondent providing qualitative feedback.']

  Note: 'Captures Net Promoter Score survey responses from customers across all touchpoints in the automotive ownership journey. Records: party_id, VIN (if vehicle-specific), survey_id, survey type (transactional/relational), touchpoint (vehicle_purchase/service_visit/delivery/recall/roadside/connected_app), dealer code, nps_score (0-10), promoter category (promoter/passive/detractor), verbatim comment, survey channel (email/SMS/app/IVR), survey date, response date, follow-up required flag, follow-up status, case_id (if escalated), and Salesforce Survey Response ID. Feeds NPS KPI reporting and closed-loop customer recovery workflows.'
}

Table customer.case {
  case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the customer service case.']
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_repair_order. Business justification: Customer complaints and escalations frequently reference specific repair orders. This link supports case management workflows, enables root cause analysis of service failures, tracks repeat repairs, a']
  aftersales_warranty_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_warranty_claim. Business justification: Warranty claim disputes are a major source of customer cases in automotive. Linking cases to warranty claims enables escalation tracking, adjudication appeals, goodwill decision support, and regulator']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Service cases (repairs, warranty claims) often generate customer-pay invoices for non-covered work or deductibles. Linking case to invoice enables dispute resolution, goodwill adjustment tracking, and']
  assigned_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.contact. Business justification: Cases are routinely assigned to specific dealer personnel (service advisors, sales managers, customer relations specialists) for resolution and follow-up. Case ownership tracking, workload management,']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Customer service cases in automotive frequently involve dealer-specific issues (service quality complaints, sales disputes, warranty administration). Case routing, dealer performance tracking, escalat']
  disputed_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Customer service cases frequently involve disputed invoices (incorrect charges, warranty claim billing errors). Case management systems must reference specific invoices to track dispute resolution and']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Warranty and recall case management requires identifying the specific defective part. Quality engineers use case-to-part linkage for root-cause analysis, recall scope determination, and warranty cost ']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer (individual, fleet, corporate) who raised the case.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.recall_campaign. Business justification: Customer cases frequently originate from recall campaigns—owners report symptoms, request recall service, or escalate incomplete repairs. Links case management to recall tracking for regulatory report']
  vehicle_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.vehicle_build. Business justification: Warranty root-cause analysis and IATF 16949 traceability require linking customer quality/warranty cases directly to the specific vehicle build record (shift, line, BOM version, quality gate results, ']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: A customer service case — particularly warranty claims, recall notifications, and service complaints — is directly associated with a specific vehicle ownership record. case already has party_id (who f']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Needed for service case management to pull vehicle specifications, warranty status, and recall info from VIN registry.']
  case_category varchar [note: 'Primary business category for the case (e.g., Service, Sales, Technical).']
  case_description varchar [note: 'Full free‑text description of the issue, request or complaint.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible case number assigned at creation.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the case.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the case purpose.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was finally closed.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Post‑resolution satisfaction rating provided by the customer (e.g., 1‑10).']
  dynamics_case_reference varchar [note: 'Native Microsoft Dynamics 365 identifier for the case.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Level of escalation applied to the case, if any.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the case was escalated.']
  opened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was initially created.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level indicating urgency (P1 highest).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the case record was first persisted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to the case record.']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing how the case was resolved.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the resolution actions taken.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the case was marked as resolved.']
  salesforce_case_reference varchar [note: 'Native Salesforce identifier for the case record.']
  sla_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target date/time by which the case should be resolved per Service Level Agreement.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the case was originally submitted.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'More detailed sub‑category within the main category.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief title or subject line summarising the case.']
  total_handle_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Cumulative time spent handling the case, measured in minutes.']

  Note: 'Customer service case record tracking complaints, inquiries, warranty claims, recall notifications, and roadside assistance requests raised by customers through any channel. Captures: party_id, VIN (if vehicle-related), case number, case type (complaint/inquiry/warranty_claim/recall/roadside/goodwill/lemon_law/regulatory), case category, case sub-category, subject, description, priority (P1-P4), status (new/in_progress/pending_customer/resolved/closed/escalated), opened date, SLA due date, resolved date, closed date, resolution code, resolution description, dealer code (if dealer-handled), assigned agent ID, escalation level, escalation reason, total handle time (minutes), customer satisfaction score (post-resolution), Salesforce Case ID, and Microsoft Dynamics 365 Case ID. SSOT for customer service operations.'
}

Table sales.opportunity {
  opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales opportunity record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that generated this opportunity, if applicable.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Opportunities must track which legal entity will book revenue for multi-entity OEMs. Critical for revenue planning, territory-to-entity mapping, and cross-border sales compliance in global automotive ']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealership managing this opportunity.']
  opportunity_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prospect or customer associated with this opportunity.']
  opportunity_model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Opportunity tracking by model enables sales forecasting, market analysis, and model performance dashboards.']
  opportunity_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prospect or customer associated with this opportunity.']
  opportunity_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or account owner assigned to this opportunity.']
  opportunity_sales_representative_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or account owner assigned to this opportunity.']
  opportunity_vehicle_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle configuration or model of interest for this opportunity.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Opportunity evaluation checks applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., emissions, safety) for the target market.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Opportunities are associated with sales territories (opportunity has territory STRING). Normalizing this to a FK to sales_territory enables proper territory-opportunity association, territory-level pi']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Required for Opportunity Management report to align sales opportunities with engineering program schedules, ensuring capacity planning.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle configuration or model of interest for this opportunity.']
  actual_close_date date [note: 'Actual date when the opportunity was closed (won or lost).']
  competitor_brand varchar [note: 'Competitor brand or OEM that the customer is also considering, if known.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Preferred delivery location or dealership for vehicle handover.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the opportunity, including dealer discounts and manufacturer incentives.']
  estimated_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total deal value including vehicle price, options, accessories, and services.']
  expected_close_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the opportunity is expected to close (either won or lost).']
  exterior_color varchar [note: 'Preferred exterior color for the vehicle.']
  financing_type varchar [note: 'Type of financing or payment method the customer intends to use.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the opportunity is expected to close, used for sales forecasting and quota tracking.']
  fleet_size varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles in the fleet opportunity, applicable for fleet and commercial sales.']
  incentive_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total manufacturer incentive amount applicable to this opportunity.']
  interior_color varchar [note: 'Preferred interior color or trim for the vehicle.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the opportunity is currently active (open) or closed.']
  is_won boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the opportunity was closed as won (successful sale).']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent sales activity or interaction related to this opportunity.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was last modified.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Original channel or source through which the opportunity was generated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|phone|email|walk_in|referral|event|advertising|social_media|partner — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  loss_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason why the opportunity was lost, if applicable. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: price|product_fit|competitor|timing|financing|no_response|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
