  aged_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator flagging vehicles that have exceeded the aging threshold defined in the floor plan agreement (typically 90, 120, or 180 days). True indicates aged inventory requiring attention.']
  aging_threshold_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after which inventory is considered aged per the floor plan agreement terms. Triggers curtailment requirements or OEM assistance programs.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Result status of the most recent floor plan audit. Passed indicates compliance; failed indicates discrepancies; pending indicates audit in progress; not_required indicates no audit needed; exception indicates special circumstances.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this floor plan financing record was first created in the system.']
  credit_line_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit line amount approved by the financing institution for the dealer\'s floor plan facility. Represents the maximum borrowing capacity.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this floor plan record (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).']
  curtailment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Required curtailment payment amount for aged inventory, typically a percentage of the original financed amount after a specified aging period (e.g., 90 or 120 days).']
  curtailment_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date by which the dealer must make a curtailment payment (partial paydown) on aged inventory per the floor plan agreement terms.']
  curtailment_paid_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the required curtailment payment has been made by the dealer. True indicates paid; False indicates outstanding.']
  daily_interest_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Daily interest accrual amount calculated based on the outstanding balance and interest rate. Used for dealer financial health monitoring.']
  days_in_inventory varchar [note: 'Number of days the vehicle has been in dealer inventory under floor plan financing, calculated from financing start date to current date or sale date.']
  dealer_invoice_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original dealer invoice amount for the vehicle, typically the basis for the floor plan financed amount.']
  default_date date [note: 'Date when the dealer was declared in default on floor plan obligations for this vehicle. Null if no default has occurred.']
  default_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the dealer has defaulted on floor plan payments for this vehicle. True indicates default; False indicates current status.']
  dms_floor_plan_reference varchar [note: 'External identifier for this floor plan record in the dealer\'s DMS (e.g., CDK Global). Used for system integration and reconciliation.']
  financing_end_date date [note: 'Date when floor plan financing for this vehicle unit was terminated, either through retail sale and payoff or other resolution.']
  financing_institution_code varchar [note: 'Internal or industry-standard code identifying the financing institution for system integration and reporting.']
  financing_institution_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution or captive finance company providing the floor plan credit line (e.g., OEM captive finance, third-party lender).']
  financing_start_date date [note: 'Date when floor plan financing for this vehicle unit commenced. Typically the date the vehicle was received into dealer inventory and financed.']
  floor_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the floor plan financing record. Active indicates ongoing financing; paid_off indicates vehicle sold and financing settled; defaulted indicates payment failure; suspended indicates temporary hold; pending_audit indicates under review; curtailed indicates partial paydown required.']
  interest_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual interest rate percentage charged by the financing institution on the outstanding floor plan balance for this vehicle.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent floor plan audit conducted by the financing institution or OEM to verify physical inventory and compliance with financing terms.']
  msrp_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price for the financed vehicle. Used for margin analysis and dealer financial health assessment.']
  next_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next floor plan audit. Used for audit planning and dealer notification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special circumstances related to this floor plan financing record (e.g., audit exceptions, payment arrangements, OEM approvals).']
  oem_assistance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Dollar amount of OEM assistance provided for this floor plan record, such as interest rate buy-down or direct payment subsidy.']
  oem_assistance_program_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any OEM floor plan assistance program applied to this vehicle (e.g., interest subsidy, extended aging allowance, special financing terms for slow-moving models).']
  outstanding_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current outstanding principal balance owed to the financing institution for this specific vehicle unit. Updated upon curtailment payments and final payoff.']
  payoff_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount paid to the financing institution to settle the floor plan obligation for this vehicle, including principal and accrued interest.']
  payoff_date date [note: 'Date when the floor plan financing for this vehicle was fully paid off, typically upon retail sale or dealer buyout.']
  per_unit_floor_plan_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The financed amount for this individual vehicle unit, typically equal to dealer invoice or a percentage thereof. Basis for interest calculation.']
  sap_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP financial document number associated with the floor plan financing transaction in the OEM\'s ERP system (SAP S/4HANA FI module).']
  total_interest_paid decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative interest amount paid to the financing institution over the life of the floor plan financing for this vehicle unit.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this floor plan financing record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']

  Note: 'Dealer vehicle floor plan financing records tracking the financing of new vehicle inventory. Captures financing institution, credit line amount, outstanding balance, per-unit floor plan cost, curtailment schedule, aged inventory flags, and floor plan audit dates. Critical for dealer financial health monitoring and OEM floor plan assistance program administration.'
}

Table dealer.dealer_warranty_claim {
  dealer_warranty_claim_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dealer_warranty_claim data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_warranty_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.warranty_claim. Business justification: Report: Dealer‑submitted warranty claim reports need to point to the authoritative aftersales warranty claim record for audit and reimbursement.']
  dealer_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealer_repair_order. Business justification: A warranty claim is generated from a specific dealer repair order — the RO documents the labor, parts, and diagnosis that form the basis of the warranty reimbursement claim. Linking dealer_warranty_cl']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealership. Business justification: Warranty claims are submitted by a specific dealership to the OEM for reimbursement. dealer_warranty_claim currently has no FK to dealership (only to aftersales.aftersales_warranty_claim, customer.par']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Warranty claim handling and compliance reporting require the claimant party to be identified.']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment. Business justification: Approved warranty claims result in reimbursement payments to dealers. Standard aftersales financial settlement - enables payment tracking and warranty cost reconciliation per claim.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Warranty claim processing needs the VIN registry reference to verify coverage and claim eligibility.']

  Note: 'Warranty claims submitted by the dealership to the OEM for reimbursement of warranty repair labor and parts. Captures VIN, repair order reference, failure date, mileage at failure, DTC (Diagnostic Trouble Code), causal part number, labor operation codes, claimed labor hours, claimed parts cost, submission date, OEM adjudication status (approved, rejected, partially approved), and reimbursement amount.'
}

Table dealer.recall_completion {
  recall_completion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the recall completion record. Primary key for the recall completion transaction.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.change. Business justification: Engineering change orders (ECOs) that identify safety defects initiate recall campaigns. Linking recall_completion to the originating engineering change enables closed-loop traceability from field rem']
  dealer_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealer_repair_order. Business justification: A recall completion event is executed through a dealer repair order — the RO captures the actual labor, parts, and technician details for the recall remedy. recall_completion already has repair_order_']
  payment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment. Business justification: Recall completions trigger reimbursement payments to dealers for recall remedy work. Regulatory and operational necessity - tracks payment for safety recall campaign execution.']
  primary_recall_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the vehicle at the time of recall completion.']
  primary_recall_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer location that performed the recall remediation work.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.recall_campaign. Business justification: Recall completions must reference the specific OEM recall campaign being remedied for warranty reimbursement claims, NHTSA reporting, campaign effectiveness tracking, and regulatory compliance verific']
  service_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_campaign. Business justification: Recall completions at dealers must reference the authoritative service campaign record for regulatory reporting (NHTSA/UNECE), remedy validation, and campaign performance tracking. Campaign defines re']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: NHTSA recall completion reporting and OEM recall tracking require scoping completions by vehicle_program. nameplate, model, model_year, and powertrain_type on recall_completion are denormalized vehicl']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Recall completion tracking must reference the VIN registry to ensure the correct vehicle is marked as completed.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when the recall remediation work was completed and the vehicle was returned to the customer. Used for NHTSA compliance reporting and OEM recall closure tracking.']
  completion_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the recall completion transaction, typically generated by the DMS or OEM recall management system.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the recall completion work. Completed indicates full remediation; incomplete or parts_pending indicate work not yet finished.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the recall work was marked complete in the DMS system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall completion record was first created in the data system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, CAD).']
  customer_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the customer was notified that the recall work was completed. Used for customer satisfaction tracking and communication audit.']
  customer_notification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to notify the customer of recall completion: email, SMS, phone call, postal mail, or in-person at dealer.']
  dms_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier from the CDK Global DMS system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation between DMS and OEM systems.']
  labor_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost for the recall work, calculated based on labor hours and dealer labor rate. Used for OEM reimbursement to dealer.']
  labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total labor hours spent on the recall remediation work. Used for dealer reimbursement calculation and efficiency analysis.']
  nhtsa_report_date date [note: 'Date when the recall completion was reported to NHTSA. Required for compliance tracking and audit trails.']
  nhtsa_report_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this recall completion has been reported to NHTSA as required for safety recalls. True means reported; false means not yet reported or not required.']
  odometer_reading varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at the time of recall completion, typically in kilometers or miles. Used for warranty validation and vehicle history tracking.']
  odometer_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the odometer reading: km (kilometers) or mi (miles).']
  parts_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of parts used in the recall remediation. OEM reimburses dealer for parts at wholesale cost.']
  parts_quantity_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or pipe-separated list of quantities corresponding to parts_used_list. Used for parts consumption analysis.']
  parts_used_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or pipe-separated list of part numbers used in the recall remediation. Used for parts consumption tracking and inventory replenishment.']
  post_repair_inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Technician notes from the post-repair inspection confirming that the recall remediation was successful and the vehicle is safe to operate.']
  pre_repair_inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Technician notes from the pre-repair inspection documenting the condition of the vehicle and the recall-related issue before remediation.']
  quality_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the quality verification or audit of the recall work was performed.']
  quality_verification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recall work was subject to quality verification or audit by the dealer service manager or OEM field representative. True means verified; false means not verified.']
  quality_verifier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the quality verification or audit of the recall work (typically service manager or OEM field representative).']
  reimbursement_claim_number varchar [note: 'Claim number submitted by the dealer to the OEM for reimbursement of recall work. Links to the OEM financial system for payment processing.']
  reimbursement_paid_date date [note: 'Date when the OEM paid the dealer for the recall work. Used for dealer cash flow analysis and OEM financial reporting.']
  reimbursement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the dealer reimbursement claim. Pending indicates claim submitted but not yet reviewed; approved means claim validated; paid means funds transferred to dealer.']
  remedy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the remediation work performed to address the recall. Includes parts replaced, adjustments made, software updates applied, etc.']
  sublet_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost of any sublet or outsourced work required to complete the recall (e.g., specialized welding, paint work). Reimbursable by OEM if pre-approved.']
  technician_certification_number varchar [note: 'Certification or license number of the technician. Some recalls require certified technicians to perform the work.']
  technician_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the technician who completed the recall work. Used for performance tracking and quality audits.']
  total_reimbursement_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount claimed by the dealer for reimbursement from the OEM, including labor, parts, and sublet costs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this recall completion record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']

  Note: 'Tracks the completion of NHTSA safety recalls and OEM service campaigns at the dealer level. Records recall/campaign number, VIN remediated, completion date, technician, parts used, labor time, reimbursement claimed, and completion status. Supports NHTSA compliance reporting and OEM recall closure rate monitoring.'
}

Table aftersales.aftersales_repair_order {
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the repair_order data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_service_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_service_appointment. Business justification: A repair order is typically created from a service appointment when the customer arrives. aftersales_repair_order currently stores appointment_scheduled_timestamp, appointment_arrival_timestamp, and a']

Build spec determines repair eligibility and procedure. No existi']

Business justification: Repair orders need to track which service center warehouse location parts are pulled from for accurate job costing, inventory depletion, and parts availability management. Enables service advisors to ']

  primary_aftersales_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer/service center handling the repair.']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Traceability report linking each repair order to the original production order enables warranty and recall investigations per VIN.']

  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: Service order processing uses the ownership record to confirm warranty coverage and apply owner‑specific service rules.']

Business justification: Repair orders must be attributed to vehicle programs for program-level warranty cost reporting, service frequency analysis by program, and engineering quality KPI dashboards. OEM service operations an']
  vehicle_warranty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.vehicle_warranty. Business justification: A repair order covered under warranty should reference the specific VIN-level vehicle_warranty entitlement record. aftersales_repair_order currently stores warranty_flag and warranty_type as denormali']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Required for warranty verification and recall tracking in the Repair Order processing workflow, linking each order to the vehicle\'s VIN registry record.']
  actual_completion_time timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the repair was completed.']
  aftersales_repair_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the repair order.']
  close_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the repair order was closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the repair order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  customer_feedback_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1-5) provided by the customer after service.']
  customer_signature_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer signed off on the repair order.']
  diagnostic_code varchar [note: 'Standard OBD diagnostic code recorded during service.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the repair order.']
  is_estimate boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record is an estimate prior to work being performed.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard labor rate applied per hour.']
  labor_total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of labor before taxes and discounts.']
  labor_total_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total labor hours recorded for the repair order.']

  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after tax and discount.']
  open_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the repair order was opened.']
  parts_total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of parts consumed before taxes and discounts.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the customer to settle the repair order.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment for the repair order.']
  promised_completion_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time promised to the customer for repair completion.']
  ro_number varchar [note: 'External repair order number assigned by the service center.']
  service_center_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the service center is located.']
  service_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by service advisor describing work performed.']
  service_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the repair order.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of service performed.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the repair order.']
  technician_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the technician during repair.']
  total_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the repair order before tax and discount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the repair order record.']
  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the warranty claim associated with this repair.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair is covered under warranty.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record capturing a vehicle service or repair event at an authorized service center or dealership. Tracks RO number, vehicle VIN, customer, service advisor, open/close dates, labor operations performed, parts consumed, total labor cost, total parts cost, total RO value, payment method, warranty vs. customer pay vs. internal pay type, DMS source (CDK Global), mileage at write-up, promised completion time, actual completion time, technician assignments, and RO status lifecycle (open, in-progress, quality-check, closed, invoiced).'
}

Table aftersales.repair_order_line {
  repair_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the repair order line.']
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent repair order header to which this line belongs.']
  aftersales_service_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service appointment linked to this line.']
  aftersales_warranty_claim_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warranty claim associated with this line, if applicable.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_change. Business justification: Specific line items may be affected by an engineering change; linking supports detailed root‑cause analysis and cost allocation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each line\'s labor/parts expense is posted to a specific GL account for detailed expense reporting.']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: REQUIRED: Parts Traceability Report for warranty investigations needs to link each repair line to the inbound part record it originated from.']
  inbound_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_shipment. Business justification: REQUIRED: Repair Order Shipment Tracking report ties repaired parts to the specific inbound shipment they arrived on for logistics audit.']
  labor_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.labor_time_standard. Business justification: Repair order line should reference the standard labor time definition for the operation code.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authorized service center where work was performed.']
  service_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_part. Business justification: A repair order line that involves parts consumption should reference the aftersales service_part master record. repair_order_line already has sku_master_id (cross-domain inventory) and inbound_part_id']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: REQUIRED: Parts used in a repair order line must be tracked against the inventory SKU master for cost, stock deduction, and compliance reporting.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: REQUIRED: Service parts are issued from a specific warehouse bin; linking to storage_location enables inventory location traceability for service.']
  technician_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who performed the labor.']
  tsb_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.tsb. Business justification: A repair order line may be performed following a specific Technical Service Bulletin (TSB). Linking repair_order_line to tsb enables tracking of TSB compliance, labor time adherence, and repair proced']
  actual_technician_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual labor hours logged by the technician for this line.']
  cause_complaint varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the customer complaint or issue that initiated the service.']
  correction varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the corrective action taken to resolve the complaint.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the repair order line record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values on the line.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to this line, if any.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the labor type performed.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly labor rate applied to the technician for this operation.']
  labor_skill_level varchar [note: 'Skill level required for the technician to perform the operation.']
  labor_time_standard decimal(5,2) [note: 'Flat‑rate standard labor time in hours for the operation code.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the repair order for ordering.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the repair order line.']
  line_total decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount for the line (labor_rate * actual_technician_hours) before discounts and taxes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes entered by the technician or service advisor.']
  operation_code varchar [note: 'Standard code representing the specific labor operation performed (e.g., oil change, brake inspection).']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'True if overtime rates were applied to this labor line.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(3,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the labor_rate when overtime_flag is true (e.g., 1.5).']
  part_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard price per unit of the part at the time of service.']
  part_quantity decimal(6,2) [note: 'Quantity of the part used for this line.']
  parts_used_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any parts were consumed on this line.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date on which the service work was performed.']
  sublet_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost charged by the sublet vendor for this line.']
  sublet_flag boolean [note: 'True if the work was performed by an external subcontractor.']
  tax_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Tax applied to the line total.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the repair order line record.']
  vehicle_vin varchar [note: 'Unique 17‑character identifier of the vehicle serviced.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the labor line is covered under warranty (true) or not (false).']

  Note: 'Individual labor operation or job line within a repair order. Captures operation code, labor time standard (flat-rate hours), actual technician hours, labor rate, line total, technician ID, cause/complaint/correction (3C) narrative, warranty flag, sublet flag, and line status. Supports granular cost analysis and technician productivity tracking per CDK Global DMS job line structure.'
}

Linking claims directly to production_variant enables warranty-to']

OEMs routinely charge back warranty costs to suppliers when parts ']
  vehicle_build_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.vehicle_build. Business justification: Warranty adjudication requires build-time evidence: build stage, quality gate status, rework count, and build timestamps to determine manufacturing-origin liability. OEM warranty teams routinely pull ']
  vehicle_ownership_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.vehicle_ownership. Business justification: Claims processing must reference the specific ownership period to determine coverage limits and calculate accurate claim payouts.']

  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Needed for claim eligibility validation against the vehicle\'s VIN registry during Warranty Claim adjudication.']
  warranty_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.warranty_policy. Business justification: A warranty claim is filed under a specific warranty policy; linking directly to warranty_policy provides direct access to policy terms without needing to traverse vehicle_warranty.']

  adjusted_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the adjustment applied to the claim.']
  approved_labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount approved for labor services.']
  approved_labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of labor hours approved for reimbursement.']
  approved_parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount approved for parts used in the repair.']

  claim_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the claim type.']
  claim_created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who entered the claim.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'External claim identifier assigned by the OEM or dealer for tracking.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warranty claim.']
  claim_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the warranty claim was submitted to the OEM.']

  failure_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle failure that triggered the warranty claim occurred.']
  goodwill_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim is processed as goodwill (no reimbursement).']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard labor rate used for calculating labor cost.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes entered by the service advisor or adjudicator.']
  parts_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax amount applied to the approved parts cost.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the claim record.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a claim was rejected, if applicable.']
  repair_date date [note: 'Date the repair work was performed on the vehicle.']
  total_claim_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the claim (labor + parts).']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle\'s warranty coverage expires.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle\'s warranty coverage began.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Category of warranty coverage applicable to the claim.']

  Note: 'Warranty claim submitted by a dealer or authorized service center to the OEM for reimbursement of warranty-covered repairs. Tracks claim number, VIN, repair order reference, failure date, repair date, claim submission date, claim status (submitted, approved, rejected, adjusted, paid), approved labor hours, approved parts cost, total claim amount, rejection reason code, goodwill flag, campaign/recall linkage, and OEM adjudication outcome. Integrates with SAP SD warranty module and CDK Global DMS.'
}

Table aftersales.warranty_policy {
  warranty_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the warranty policy record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Warranty policies are legal contracts issued by specific legal entities (company codes). Required for warranty reserve accounting by legal entity, intercompany warranty claim settlement, regulatory co']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Warranty policies are scoped to specific vehicle models (e.g., 2024 F-150 base warranty). Warranty administrators and customer-facing warranty lookup tools require model-level policy resolution. No ex']
  powertrain_spec_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.powertrain_spec. Business justification: Powertrain-specific warranty policies (EV battery, hybrid, ICE) are tied to specific powertrain specifications for coverage term determination and regulatory compliance (e.g., CARB EV battery warranty']
  powertrain_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: Warranty policies are powertrain-specific (EV battery warranties are regulated separately from ICE under federal law). The plain text powertrain_type column on warranty_policy is a denormalization of ']
  production_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_variant. Business justification: Warranty policies differ by production variant — EV powertrain variants carry different coverage terms than ICE variants; ADAS-equipped variants may have distinct component warranties. OEM warranty po']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Warranty policies must comply with consumer protection regulations (Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act in US, EU Consumer Rights Directive, regional warranty disclosure laws). Tracking the regulatory basis en']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.standard. Business justification: Warranty policies must comply with regulatory quality standards (Magnuson-Moss, EU directives, regional consumer protection laws). Links warranty terms to governing quality/regulatory standards - requ']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Warranty policies are defined per vehicle program; the link supports cost forecasting and compliance tracking per program.']
  authorized_dealer_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether warranty service must be performed at an authorized dealer (true) or can be performed at any qualified service center (false).']
  claim_limit_per_year varchar [note: 'Maximum number of warranty claims allowed per vehicle per calendar year.']
  claim_limit_total varchar [note: 'Overall maximum number of warranty claims permitted during the policy term.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code indicating the primary country of warranty applicability.']
  coverage_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of what components or systems are covered under the policy.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Category of coverage provided by the warranty (e.g., basic, powertrain, corrosion, emissions, EV battery, ADAS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the warranty policy record was first created in the system.']
  deductible_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount the customer must pay for each covered repair under the warranty.']
  duration_months varchar [note: 'Length of the warranty coverage expressed in calendar months.']
  effective_end_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the warranty coverage expires (typically EOP or calculated from duration).']
  effective_start_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the warranty coverage becomes effective (typically SOP).']
  eop_date date [note: 'The production end date of the vehicle model to which the warranty applies.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'List of components, conditions, or events excluded from coverage.']
  extension_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the warranty may be extended beyond the original term under defined conditions.']
  extension_terms varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the conditions, cost, and duration for extending the warranty.']
  inclusions varchar [note: 'List of components, systems, or services explicitly covered.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the warranty policy record.']
  market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region where the warranty is offered (e.g., North America, EU).']
  mileage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum distance the vehicle may travel while the warranty remains valid, expressed in miles.']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle for which the warranty is defined.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or remarks about the warranty policy.']
  policy_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the warranty policy, used in contracts and customer communications.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warranty policy.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Governing authority that mandates or oversees the warranty (e.g., NHTSA, EPA).']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory filing or certification linked to the warranty.']
  renewal_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty can be renewed after expiration.']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Details of renewal options, pricing, and eligibility.']
  service_center_network varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service‑center network eligible for warranty work (e.g., OEM network, third‑party network).']
  sop_date date [note: 'The production start date of the vehicle model to which the warranty applies.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty can be transferred to a subsequent owner (true) or is non‑transferable (false).']

  Note: 'Master definition of warranty coverage terms applicable to a vehicle nameplate, model year, powertrain type, or market. Captures warranty type (basic/bumper-to-bumper, powertrain, corrosion, emissions, EV battery, ADAS), coverage duration in months, coverage distance in miles/km, deductible amount, transferability flag, market/region applicability, SOP and EOP dates, and governing regulatory body (NHTSA, EPA). Serves as the authoritative reference for warranty eligibility determination.'
}

Table aftersales.vehicle_warranty {
  vehicle_warranty_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the vehicle warranty record.']
  aftersales_warranty_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.warranty_policy. Business justification: Vehicle warranty must reference the applicable warranty policy; adds inbound to warranty_policy and outbound from vehicle_warranty.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.party. Business justification: Warranty administration requires linking each warranty to the owning party for eligibility verification and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  primary_vehicle_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer that sold the vehicle or issued the warranty.']
  recall_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a recall record that may affect the warranty.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authorized service center handling warranty work.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service contract linked to the warranty.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Vehicle warranties must be directly associated with the vehicle program for program-level warranty cost tracking, reserve accrual reporting, and engineering quality dashboards. The indirect path via w']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Connects warranty contracts to the VIN registry to validate coverage periods and claim eligibility.']
  claims_last_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the most recent warranty claim.']
  claims_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent warranty claim.']
  coverage_area varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the warranty coverage.']
  coverage_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of what components and services are covered.']
  coverage_end_mileage varchar [note: 'Maximum mileage at which warranty coverage ends.']
  coverage_start_mileage varchar [note: 'Mileage reading at which warranty coverage begins.']
  cpo_warranty_flag boolean [note: 'True if the warranty applies to a Certified Pre‑Owned vehicle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warranty record was first created in the system.']
  duration_months varchar [note: 'Total length of the warranty in months.']
  eligible_for_recall boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle is eligible for a safety recall under this warranty.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the warranty coverage ends.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Text describing items or conditions excluded from coverage.']
  extended_warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty has been extended beyond the original terms.']
  last_transfer_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent warranty transfer.']
  mileage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum mileage allowed under the warranty.']
  original_purchase_date date [note: 'Date the vehicle was originally purchased by the first owner.']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the warranty policy.']
  program_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the warranty program.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the warranty program (e.g., New Vehicle, CPO, Extended).']
  remaining_mileage varchar [note: 'Mileage remaining before the warranty limit is reached.']
  remaining_months varchar [note: 'Number of months remaining before warranty expiration.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date on which the warranty was renewed or is scheduled to renew.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty is eligible for renewal.']
  service_plan varchar [note: 'Name of the service plan associated with the warranty.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the warranty coverage became effective (in‑service date).']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the warranty is in its current status.']
  transfer_allowed boolean [note: 'True if the warranty may be transferred to a subsequent owner.']
  transfer_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the warranty has been transferred to a new owner.']
  transfer_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged for transferring the warranty to a new owner.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the warranty record.']
  vehicle_warranty_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the warranty.']
  warranty_claims_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all warranty claims.']
  warranty_claims_count varchar [note: 'Total number of warranty claims filed to date.']
  warranty_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic copy of the warranty contract.']
  warranty_number varchar [note: 'External warranty contract number assigned by the manufacturer or dealer.']
  warranty_terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the warranty terms document.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Category of coverage provided by the warranty.']

  Note: 'VIN-level warranty entitlement record linking a specific vehicle to its applicable warranty policies. Tracks warranty start date (in-service date), expiration date, remaining months, remaining mileage, warranty status (active, expired, voided), extended warranty flag, CPO (Certified Pre-Owned) warranty flag, and any warranty transfer history. This is the SSOT for whether a specific vehicle is under warranty at any point in time.'
}

Table aftersales.service_campaign {
  service_campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the service campaign record.']
  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.change. Business justification: Service campaigns are initiated when an engineering change order identifies a field-safety or quality issue requiring customer notification. The ECO-to-campaign traceability is required for regulatory']
  fmea_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.fmea_record. Business justification: Safety recalls and service campaigns must be traceable to the FMEA failure mode that identified or predicted the defect. NHTSA/UNECE regulatory submissions require documented root cause; linking servi']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: NHTSA and UNECE regulatory reporting requires campaign-to-model traceability (e.g., how many campaigns affect Model X). The existing vehicle_program FK is program-level; model-level campaign scoping i']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Service campaigns triggered by plant-specific manufacturing defects must reference the originating plant for regulatory reporting (NHTSA/UNECE) and corrective action tracking. OEM recall teams scope a']
  primary_part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Service campaigns (recalls and non-safety campaigns) are triggered by specific part failures. Linking to the primary engineering part enables supplier recovery chargebacks, FMEA corrective action trac']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: When a production line process defect causes a field issue, the service campaign is scoped to vehicles produced on that specific line. Manufacturing quality and aftersales teams use this link to drive']
  production_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_variant. Business justification: Service campaigns and recalls target specific production variants. Campaign managers must identify the affected VIN population by production variant to scope notifications, parts provisioning, and rem']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Service campaigns often implement regulatory compliance fixes (emissions software updates, safety modifications per EPA/CARB/UNECE requirements). Tracking the regulatory basis is essential for audit t']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Service campaigns are planned per vehicle program; linking enables program‑level impact analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  affected_model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year in the range of vehicles covered.']
  affected_model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year in the range of vehicles covered.']
  affected_vin_population varchar [note: 'Estimated count of VINs that fall within the campaign scope.']
  campaign_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected total cost to execute the campaign (currency assumed USD).']
  campaign_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments from campaign managers.']
  campaign_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for resource allocation and scheduling.']
  campaign_region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region(s) affected; uses ISO‑3 country codes.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign: safety recall, emissions recall, customer satisfaction program, or technical service bulletin action.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the campaign with applicable regulations.']
  customer_satisfaction_flag boolean [note: 'True if the campaign is a voluntary customer‑satisfaction program.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign is closed or no longer applicable (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the campaign becomes effective for affected vehicles.']
  emissions_recall_flag boolean [note: 'True if the campaign addresses emissions compliance.']
  estimated_repair_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time, in minutes, required to complete the repair per vehicle.']
  nhtsa_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign meets NHTSA requirements.']
  parts_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of parts required for the campaign.']
  parts_required varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers needed to perform the remedy.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']
  regulatory_reporting_date date [note: 'Date on which the campaign was reported to the regulator.']
  regulatory_reporting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the campaign\'s regulatory filing with NHTSA/UNECE.']
  remedy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the repair or corrective action required.']
  safety_recall_flag boolean [note: 'True if the campaign is a safety‑related recall.']
  technical_service_bulletin_flag boolean [note: 'True if the campaign originates from a TSB.']
  unece_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign meets UNECE regulations.']
  warranty_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign affects warranty coverage.']

  Note: 'Master record for a service campaign (recall or non-safety field action) issued by the OEM. Captures NHTSA recall number, campaign code, campaign type (safety recall, emissions recall, customer satisfaction program, technical service bulletin action), affected nameplate/model year range, affected VIN population count, remedy description, estimated repair time, parts required, campaign open date, campaign close date, regulatory reporting status, and NHTSA/UNECE compliance flags. Integrates with NHTSA recall database and SAP QM.'
}

  aftersales_service_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_service_appointment. Business justification: A campaign VIN record tracks the scheduled service appointment for the recall/campaign remedy. campaign_vin has scheduled_service_date (DATE) as a denormalized value — the authoritative appointment re']
  service_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_campaign. Business justification: Campaign VIN records need to be linked to the parent service campaign for proper campaign tracking.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Associates vehicles with recall or service campaigns, enabling campaign effectiveness reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory compliance deadline for completing the remedy.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status for the vehicle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign VIN record was created.']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer where remedy was performed.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the campaign is classified as critical safety.']
  labor_rate_usd_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Labor rate applied for the remedy in USD per hour.']
  labor_time_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Labor time recorded for the remedy in hours.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent service performed on the vehicle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes entered by the service technician.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date when the vehicle was notified about the campaign.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current notification status of the vehicle for the campaign.']
  odometer_reading_km varchar [note: 'Vehicle odometer reading at time of remedy in kilometers.']
  owner_response varchar [note: 'Owner\'s response to the campaign notification.']
  parts_consumed varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers consumed for the remedy.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Official recall number assigned by regulatory authority.']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Category of the recall or campaign.']
  remedy_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the remedy was completed.']
  remedy_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the remedy execution.']
  service_center_location varchar [note: 'Location identifier of the service center where remedy was performed.']

  total_parts_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of parts consumed for the remedy in USD.']
  total_remedy_cost_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost (parts + labor) for the remedy in USD.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  warranty_covered boolean [note: 'Indicates if the remedy is covered under warranty.']

  Note: 'Association record linking a specific VIN to an open service campaign or recall. Tracks VIN, campaign code, notification status (not notified, notified, scheduled, completed, parts-on-order, owner-refused), remedy completion date, dealer code where remedy was performed, parts consumed for remedy, and compliance deadline. Enables OEM and NHTSA to track recall completion rates at the individual vehicle level.'
}

  transportation_option varchar [note: 'Customer transportation provision during service.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the appointment record.']

  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'True if the appointment is covered under a warranty agreement.']

  Note: 'Scheduled service appointment record for a vehicle at a dealership or authorized service center. Captures appointment date/time, customer contact, VIN, service type (maintenance, warranty repair, recall, PDI, customer pay), service advisor assigned, estimated duration, transportation option (loaner, shuttle, wait), appointment source (online, phone, DMS, mobile app), confirmation status, check-in time, and no-show flag. Sourced from CDK Global DMS scheduling module.'
}

  change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.change. Business justification: TSBs are frequently triggered by or co-issued with engineering change orders (ECOs). OEMs require ECO-to-TSB traceability for regulatory submissions and warranty cost recovery. This link enables the e']

Quality teams analyze defect records to identify systemic issues requiring technical service bulletins - core process linking quality defect track']
  design_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_specification. Business justification: Technical Service Bulletins are issued based on design specifications; the link provides traceability for compliance audits.']
  ecu_catalog_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.ecu_catalog. Business justification: OTA-capable TSBs (is_ota_capable flag on tsb) reference specific ECU catalog entries to identify affected software baselines.   fmea_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.fmea. Business justification: TSBs are created based on FMEA failure mode predictions validated by field data. Quality engineering uses FMEA analysis to inform TSB root cause and remedy procedures - standard automotive quality-to-']
  fmea_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.fmea_record. Business justification: TSBs are directly traceable to FMEA failure modes per APQP/PPAP requirements. When a field failure matches a predicted failure mode, the FMEA record is referenced in the TSB for corrective action trac']
  labor_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.labor_time_standard. Linking tsb to labor_time_standard normalizes the labor time reference — the TSB should reference the official fl']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: TSBs are issued for specific vehicle models; service technicians look up applicable TSBs by model during diagnosis. The plain text affected_vehicle_model column is a denormalization of vehicle.model. ']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: TSBs reference the engineering part (not just service part) for design traceability, supplier recovery, and FMEA feedback. tsb.part_number is a denormalized plain-text field; replacing with part_maste']
  production_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_variant. Business justification: TSBs are issued for specific production variants (e.g., a specific engine or powertrain variant with a known field issue).   regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement.   service_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_campaign. Business justification: Technical Service Bulletin (TSB) is issued as part of a service campaign; linking provides campaign context.']
  validation_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.validation_test. Business justification: TSB root-cause analysis requires traceability to the validation test that failed to detect the field issue. OEM DVP gap reporting and APQP corrective action processes depend on this link to identify w']
  affected_model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year in the range affected by the bulletin.']
  affected_model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year in the range affected by the bulletin.']
  affected_vin_end varchar [note: 'Ending VIN in the range covered by the bulletin.']
  affected_vin_start varchar [note: 'Starting VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) in the range covered by the bulletin.']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'Link to the full PDF or digital document of the bulletin.']
