  author_engineer varchar [note: 'Name of the OEM engineer who authored the bulletin.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bulletin record was first created in the data lake.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of bulletin distribution to the dealer network.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the bulletin recommendations become applicable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the bulletin is no longer applicable (nullable for open‑ended bulletins).']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated technician labor time required to complete the repair.']
  is_ota_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fix can be delivered via Over‑The‑Air update.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the bulletin was officially issued by the OEM.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard labor rate applied for cost estimation (currency assumed USD).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the bulletin was last reviewed for relevance or accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier of the required part.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the bulletin satisfies a regulatory requirement (e.g., NHTSA, EPA).']
  repair_procedure varchar [note: 'Step‑by‑step instructions for service technicians to resolve the issue.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent technical review.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Technical explanation of why the symptom occurs.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Impact severity of the issue addressed by the bulletin.']
  superseded_by_tsb_number varchar [note: 'TSB number that supersedes this bulletin, if any.']
  symptom_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the customer‑reported or observed symptom that triggers the bulletin.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the bulletin.']
  total_estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Combined estimated cost of parts and labor for the bulletin repair.']
  tsb_number varchar [note: 'OEM-assigned alphanumeric identifier for the bulletin (e.g., TSB-2023-001).']
  tsb_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bulletin.']
  tsb_type varchar [note: 'Category of the bulletin indicating its purpose.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the bulletin record.']
  vehicle_system_affected varchar [note: 'Specific vehicle subsystem (e.g., ECU, transmission) impacted by the issue.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the bulletin content.']
  warranty_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the repair is covered under vehicle warranty.']

  Note: 'Technical Service Bulletin master record issued by OEM engineering to communicate repair procedures, diagnostic guidance, or part supersessions to the dealer service network. Captures TSB number, title, affected nameplate/model year/VIN range, symptom description, root cause, recommended repair procedure, required parts list, estimated labor time, issue date, supersession reference, and distribution status to dealer network. Integrates with PTC Windchill and CDK Global DMS.'
}

Table aftersales.labor_time_standard {
  labor_time_standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each labor time standard record.']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Labor time standards are defined per vehicle model for repair order costing and technician scheduling. Service advisors look up standard hours by model daily. The vehicle_segment, body_style, and mode']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.routing. Business justification: Aftersales flat-rate labor time standards are derived from and validated against manufacturing routing operation times. Service engineering teams reference manufacturing routings when establishing or ']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Flat rate labor time standards are published per vehicle program and model year. OEM service engineering teams maintain program-specific labor time manuals; linking labor_time_standard to vehicle_prog']
  body_style varchar [note: 'Body style classification of the vehicle (e.g., hatchback, pickup). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: coupe|sedan|hatchback|wagon|pickup|van|SUV — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_nhtsa_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory code from NHTSA that the labor operation must satisfy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the labor time standard becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the labor time standard is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the operation is mandatory for the vehicle model/year, false otherwise.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of labor (e.g., maintenance, repair).']
  labor_time_standard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the labor time standard.']
  labor_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the labor is covered under warranty, billed to the customer, or part of a recall.']
  last_revision_date date [note: 'Date when the labor time standard was last revised.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special instructions for the labor operation.']
  oem_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number of the OEM component associated with the operation, if any.']
  operation_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the labor operation (e.g., "A123").']
  operation_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the labor operation performed.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the standard applies.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the revision of the standard.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Technician skill level required to perform the operation.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the labor time data (OEM guide, MOTOR, Alldata, etc.).']
  special_tool_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a special tool is needed (true) or not (false).']
  standard_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Flat‑rate labor time in hours for the operation.']
  tool_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the special tool required for the operation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  vehicle_model_year_end varchar [note: 'Last model year for which this labor time standard applies.']
  vehicle_model_year_start varchar [note: 'First model year for which this labor time standard applies.']
  vehicle_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of vehicle (e.g., sedan, SUV) to which the standard is applicable.']

  Note: 'Reference master for flat-rate labor time standards (operation times) used to price warranty claims and customer-pay repair operations. Captures operation code, operation description, applicable nameplate/model year range, standard flat-rate hours, skill level required, special tool requirement flag, last revision date, and source (OEM labor time guide or MOTOR/Alldata industry standard). Used by CDK Global DMS to auto-populate labor time on repair order lines.'
}

Table aftersales.service_part {
  service_part_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the service part record.']
  inbound_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.inbound_part. Business justification: REQUIRED: Service Part Origin Traceability needed for regulatory compliance (NHTSA/UNECE) links service parts to their inbound part records.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Required for parts traceability report linking service inventory to engineering part master specs, essential for compliance and quality analysis.']
  compliance_certification varchar [note: 'Regulatory certification(s) applicable to the part (e.g., EPA, DOT).']
  core_charge decimal(12,2) [note: 'Refundable deposit required for parts that are returned for reuse.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating where the part was manufactured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the service part record was first created in the system.']
  dealer_net_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price offered to authorized dealers after standard discounts.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the part is retired from the service catalog (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the part becomes valid for use in service operations.']
  epa_hazardous_material_code varchar [note: 'EPA classification code for hazardous parts, if applicable.']
  hazardous_classification varchar [note: 'Regulatory classification code for hazardous parts (e.g., UN number).']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is classified as hazardous material.']
  height_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical height of the part in millimetres.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current inventory availability status of the part.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the part was last consumed in a service transaction.']
  length_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical length of the part in millimetres.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the part within the after‑sales catalog.']
  list_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Manufacturer suggested retail price before any discounts.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer that produces the part.']
  part_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the part type.']
  part_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the part.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned part number used to uniquely identify the part across the enterprise.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier for engineering changes to the part.']
  service_part_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the part, including fitment notes.']
  superseded_by_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number that replaces this part when it is superseded.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for inventory and transaction quantities (e.g., EA, SET).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the service part record.']
  warranty_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part is covered under the standard warranty program.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the part is covered by warranty from the date of installation.']
  weight_kg decimal(8,2) [note: 'Net weight of the part in kilograms.']
  width_mm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical width of the part in millimetres.']

  Note: 'Aftersales service parts master record for parts stocked and consumed in dealer service and repair operations. Captures OEM part number, supersession chain (current and all prior part numbers), part description, part category (mechanical, electrical, body, consumable, fluid, accessory), unit of measure, standard list price, dealer net price, core charge amount, weight, hazmat flag, country of origin, minimum order quantity, and lifecycle status (active, superseded, discontinued, obsolete). Serves as the aftersales-specific view of the parts catalog optimized for service ordering and warranty claims — distinct from the manufacturing BOM parts master in the engineering domain.'
}

Table aftersales.parts_order {
  parts_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the parts_order data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  aftersales_repair_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_repair_order. Business justification: A parts order may be placed specifically to fulfill parts needed for an active repair order (emergency or special order). This FK links the parts procurement directly to the repair event. Nullable — m']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Parts procurement cost is charged to a cost center for expense reporting in the Parts Spend Report.']
  fulfillment_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Parts orders must specify the warehouse location for picking and shipping operations. Fulfillment location code is denormalized; proper FK enables inventory allocation, pick list generation, and shipp']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: GL account reference is required for posting parts purchase amounts in the Procurement GL Posting process.']
  primary_aftersales_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the dealer or service center that placed the parts order.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center allocation allows analysis of parts cost contribution to overall profitability.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Dealer‑to‑Supplier PO Reconciliation report matches dealership parts orders to supplier purchase orders for cost allocation and audit.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_center. Business justification: A parts order is placed by or on behalf of a specific service center. aftersales_parts_order has dealership_id FKs but lacks a direct FK to the service_center that needs the parts. This FK enables ser']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Service parts distribution from central warehouses to dealership service centers requires inbound logistics tracking. Service centers need shipment visibility for inventory planning, appointment sched']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the parts were actually received at the dealer.']
  backorder_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any line items are on backorder.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the parts order record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the order.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount granted on the order.']
  freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost of shipping the parts from the fulfillment location to the dealer.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms governing freight cost responsibility.']
  net_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after applying discounts, taxes, and freight.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the parts order by the dealer or OEM system.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the parts order (e.g., submitted, confirmed, picked, shipped, received, invoiced, cancelled). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: submitted|confirmed|picked|shipped|received|invoiced|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  order_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the parts order was placed by the dealer.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order based on business need.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment condition for the order.']
  priority_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the order is marked as high priority.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date requested by the dealer for parts to be delivered.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used to deliver the parts.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for any additional handling or delivery instructions.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the order.']
  total_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the order before discounts, taxes, and freight.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the parts order record.']

  Note: 'Parts order placed by a dealer or service center to the OEM parts distribution center (PDC) or regional warehouse. Captures order number, ordering dealer code, PDC fulfillment location, order date, requested delivery date, order type (stock, emergency, campaign/recall, special), order status (submitted, confirmed, picked, shipped, received, invoiced), total order value, freight terms, and backorder flags. Integrates with SAP MM and dealer DMS parts ordering module.'
}

Table aftersales.parts_order_line {
  parts_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each line item within a dealer parts order.']
  parts_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parts order (header) to which this line belongs.']
  service_part_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the part master record referenced by this line.']
  stock_balance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_balance. Business justification: Parts order lines should reference the specific stock balance record allocated for fulfillment to enable lot/batch traceability, FIFO/FEFO picking, expiration date management, and quality status verif']
  backorder_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Units still pending fulfillment after initial shipment.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of units the supplier confirmed it can supply.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which the monetary amounts are expressed.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary discount granted for this line item.']
  estimated_availability_date date [note: 'Projected calendar date when the part can be shipped.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the line item in the parts order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the line item.']
  line_total decimal(14,2) [note: 'Gross amount for the line before discounts, taxes, and adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks entered by the dealer or supplier regarding the line item.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of units the dealer ordered.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of units dispatched from the warehouse.']
  substitution_part_number varchar [note: 'Part number of a substitute component offered in place of the original.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax calculated for the line based on applicable rates.']
  unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Price per single unit of the part before discounts and taxes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the line record.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'True if the part is supplied under warranty terms.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a dealer parts order. Captures part number, ordered quantity, confirmed quantity, shipped quantity, unit price, line total, backorder quantity, estimated availability date, and substitution part number if original is unavailable. Enables granular parts fulfillment tracking and backorder management at the line level.'
}

Table aftersales.service_contract {
  service_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the service contract record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Contract revenue allocation uses a cost center to capture service contract income in the Contract Revenue Report.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: GL account reference is required for posting contract revenue and expense in the Contract Accounting process.']
  primary_service_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who purchased the service contract.']
  primary_service_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer that sold the service contract to the customer.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit center links enable profitability analysis of service contracts across business units.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Service contracts (extended warranties, maintenance plans) must comply with insurance regulations, consumer protection laws, and warranty disclosure requirements that vary by jurisdiction. Tracking re']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary service center authorized to fulfill the contract.']
  tertiary_service_transfer_to_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer that received the transferred contract.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Service contracts are sold per vehicle program; linking enables revenue forecasting and program‑level service planning.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Binds service contracts to the VIN registry to enforce service plan applicability and mileage tracking.']
  warranty_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.warranty_policy. Business justification: A service contract defines coverage based on a warranty policy; linking to warranty_policy enables consistent policy lookup and eliminates redundant policy attributes in service_contract.']
  administrator_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity (OEM or third‑party) that administers the contract.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract was formally cancelled, if applicable.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason provided for contract cancellation.']
  claim_count varchar [note: 'Total number of service claims submitted under this contract.']
  contract_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the contract terms and conditions.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of coverage provided by the service contract.']
  coverage_mileage_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum mileage (kilometres) covered under the contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the contract value.']
  deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed amount the customer must pay per claim before the contract pays.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Discount granted on the contract price before tax.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the contract coverage becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the contract coverage expires or terminates (nullable for open‑ended contracts).']
  is_refundable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract value is refundable upon early termination.']
  is_transferable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract may be transferred to another dealer or owner.']
  last_claim_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent claim filed against the contract.']
  mileage_used varchar [note: 'Total mileage recorded on the vehicle while the contract was active.']
  net_contract_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final contract value after applying discounts and taxes.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the customer to pay for the contract.']
  payment_term_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment term applied to the contract invoice.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the contract complies with all applicable automotive regulatory requirements.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for contract renewal if renewal_option is not \'none\'.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Policy for contract renewal: automatic, manual, or none.']
  service_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service contract.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the contract price.']
  total_contract_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the contract at the time of sale.']
  transfer_date date [note: 'Date when the contract ownership was transferred to another dealer or party.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the contract record.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle is still under the original OEM warranty (true) or not (false).']

  Note: 'Extended service contract or vehicle service agreement (VSA) sold to a customer beyond the standard OEM warranty. Captures contract number, VIN, customer reference, contract type (powertrain, comprehensive, maintenance plan, tire and wheel), coverage start date, coverage end date, coverage mileage limit, deductible amount, selling dealer, administrator (OEM or third-party), contract status (active, expired, cancelled, transferred), and total contract value. Distinct from the OEM warranty_policy which is factory-issued.'
}

  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the extended service contract under which the claim is filed.']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Claims are aggregated by vehicle program for reliability metrics and warranty cost analysis.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Service contract claims are filed against specific vehicles. The plain text vin column on service_contract_claim is a denormalization of vehicle.vin_registry. A structured FK enables vehicle-level cla']

  claim_adjusted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the claim amount has been adjusted after initial approval.']
  claim_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for any monetary adjustment to the claim.']
  claim_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the claim type.']
  claim_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the claim was closed or paid.']
  claim_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim record was first created in the system.']
  claim_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the claim becomes effective for processing.']
  claim_line_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual line items (parts, labor, etc.) associated with the claim.']
  claim_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the claim by the service contract system.']
  claim_original_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Original total amount requested before any deductions or adjustments.']
  claim_payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to remit payment for the claim.']
  claim_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the claim payment.']
  claim_payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the approved claim amount was paid to the service provider.']
  claim_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the claim for processing.']
  claim_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the claim was reviewed.']
  claim_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who reviewed the claim.']
  claim_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the claim.']
  claim_submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the claim was submitted to the service contract system.']
  claim_submitted_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who submitted the claim.']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the claim is under an extended service contract or a voluntary service agreement.']
  claim_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the claim record.']
  claimed_labor_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Labor cost claimed for the repair, before any adjustments.']
  claimed_parts_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Parts cost claimed for the repair, before any adjustments.']
  covered_repair_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the repair work covered by the claim.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values in the claim.']
  deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Deductible applied to the claim as per the service contract terms.']
  denial_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating why a claim was denied, if applicable.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard labor rate applied to calculate labor charges.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text notes entered by claim processors.']
  parts_tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax amount applied to the parts portion of the claim.']

  Note: 'Claim submitted against an extended service contract or VSA for a covered repair. Tracks claim number, service contract reference, repair order reference, VIN, claim submission date, covered repair description, claimed labor amount, claimed parts amount, deductible applied, approved amount, claim status (pending, approved, denied, paid), and denial reason code. Distinct from OEM warranty_claim which is submitted to the manufacturer.'
}

The plain text vin column on goodwill_adjustment is a denormalization of vehicle.vin_registry. A structured FK enables vehicle-']
  adjustment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the goodwill adjustment for tracking and audit.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the goodwill adjustment record.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goodwill adjustment is associated with a warranty claim.']

  Note: 'Goodwill or customer assistance transaction where the OEM or dealer provides financial relief to a customer for a repair outside standard warranty coverage. Tracks OEM goodwill spend against budget allocations and enables cost management reporting by region, model, and failure type.'
}

Business justification: Service centers operate under specific legal entities for tax compliance, employment law, liability management, and financial reporting. Essential for multi-entity automotive groups with regional serv']
  dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer group to which the service center belongs.']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: Service centers must comply with local regulations (environmental disposal, labor laws, safety standards, emissions testing authorization). Linking to jurisdiction enables tracking authorization statu']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.plant. Business justification: Service Center performance dashboard aggregates service metrics by originating plant for warranty and recall analysis.']
  adas_calibration_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the center can calibrate Advanced Driver Assistance Systems.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the service center.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  authorization_level varchar [note: 'Combined classification of services the center is authorized to perform.']
  average_service_time_minutes decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean duration from service order start to completion, expressed in minutes.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the service center is located.']
  collision_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the center is certified to perform collision repairs.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service center record was first created.']
  dsk_instance_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CDK Global DMS instance that manages this service center.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the service center ceased operations (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the service center became active in the network.']
  ev_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the center is qualified to service electric vehicles.']
  iatf_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with IATF 16949 quality management standards.']
  is_primary_center boolean [note: 'Indicates if this center is the primary hub for its dealer group.']
  iso9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with ISO 9001 quality management standards.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent compliance audit was performed.']
  latitude double [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the service center location.']
  loaner_fleet_size varchar [note: 'Number of loaner vehicles maintained for customer use.']
  longitude double [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the service center location.']
  market varchar [note: 'Market segment served (e.g., premium, mass‑market, commercial).']
  network_status varchar [note: 'Current participation status of the center in the OEM service network.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the service center.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard weekly operating hours (e.g., Mon‑Fri 08:00‑18:00).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code.']
  recall_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the center can execute manufacturer recall campaigns.']
  region varchar [note: 'Regional grouping (e.g., North America, EMEA) used for reporting.']
  regulatory_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the center\'s compliance with automotive regulations.']
  service_bay_count varchar [note: 'Total count of service bays available for vehicle work.']
  service_center_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the service center in dealer and OEM systems.']
  service_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the authorized service center.']
  service_center_type varchar [note: 'Category of the service center based on ownership and relationship to OEM.']
  service_orders_processed varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of service orders completed at the center.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province abbreviation.']
  technician_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of technicians employed at the service center.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the service center record.']
  warranty_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the center can perform warranty repairs.']
  warranty_claims_processed varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of warranty claims handled by the center.']

  Note: 'Master record for authorized service centers and dealership service departments in the OEM aftersales network. Captures service center code, name, address, dealer group affiliation, authorization level (warranty, recall, certified collision, EV-certified, ADAS-calibration), service bay count, technician headcount, CDK Global DMS instance ID, operating hours, loaner fleet size, and network status (active, suspended, terminated). Distinct from the dealer domain\'s dealer profile — this is the service-operations-specific view.'
}

Table aftersales.technician {
  technician_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the service technician.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service center to which the technician is assigned.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current availability of the technician for new assignments.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the technician\'s current certification expires.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'Level of certification achieved by the technician.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of certification held by the technician (e.g., ASE, OEM, EV, ADAS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the technician record was first created.']
  current_active_ro_count varchar [note: 'Number of repair orders currently assigned to the technician.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for communication with the technician.']
  flat_rate_efficiency_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Efficiency rating expressed as a percentage of flat‑rate labor productivity.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the technician.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the technician was hired by the organization.']
  last_training_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent training session attended by the technician.']
  max_concurrent_ro varchar [note: 'Maximum number of repair orders the technician can handle simultaneously.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes about the technician (e.g., performance comments, special accommodations).']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the technician is eligible for overtime pay.']
  overtime_rate_multiplier decimal(3,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to the base labor rate for overtime work.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the technician.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Work shift classification for the technician.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Skill tier of the technician based on experience and performance.']
  specialization varchar [note: 'Technical area of expertise for the technician.']
  technician_status varchar [note: 'Current employment status of the technician.']
  training_hours_completed varchar [note: 'Cumulative hours of formal training completed by the technician.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the technician record.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total number of years the technician has worked in automotive service.']

  Note: 'Master record for service technicians employed at authorized service centers. Captures technician ID, name, service center assignment, certification level (ASE, OEM-certified, EV-certified, ADAS-certified), specialization (powertrain, electrical, body, diagnostics), flat-rate efficiency rating, current active RO count, hire date, and certification expiry dates. This is the aftersales-specific technician profile focused on service capacity and certification — distinct from the workforce domain\'s employee record.'
}

Table aftersales.parts_return {
  parts_return_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the parts return transaction.']
  aftersales_warranty_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_warranty_claim. Business justification: Warranty parts returns are required by OEMs as part of the warranty claim process — defective parts must be returned for inspection. parts_return has warranty_flag indicating warranty-related returns ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Returned parts credit is allocated to a cost center for accurate reverse logistics cost accounting.']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Returned parts are analyzed against the engineering part master to identify systemic defects and drive quality improvements.']
  parts_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_parts_order. Business justification: A parts return is typically associated with the original parts order from which the part was received. parts_return currently stores original_order_number as a denormalized STRING — this should be a p']
  primary_parts_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the dealer originating the return.']
  receiving_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Returned parts must be received into a specific warehouse location for inspection, disposition, and restocking. Return location string is denormalized; FK enables proper goods receipt posting, quality']
  repair_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.repair_order_line. Business justification: A parts return may be traced back to the specific repair order line where the part was consumed (warranty core returns, defective parts used in repair). parts_return currently stores original_order_li']
  service_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service or recall campaign linked to the return.']
  service_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_part. Business justification: A parts return is for a specific service part. parts_return has part_master_id (cross-domain engineering FK) but lacks a FK to the aftersales-domain service_part master. This FK enables direct lookup ']
  supplier_quality_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.supplier_quality_event. Business justification: Parts returns from dealer inventory due to supplier quality issues must be tracked back to originating supplier quality events for cost recovery and supplier scorecarding - standard supplier quality m']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch identifier of the returned part, if applicable.']
  core_return_flag boolean [note: 'True when the part is a core that must be returned for reuse.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the return record was created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross credit amount owed to the dealer before taxes or adjustments.']
  credit_memo_date date [note: 'Date the credit memo was generated.']
  credit_memo_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credit memo generated for the return.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the credit amount.']
  inspected_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the inspection.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date the returned parts were inspected.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from the inspection process.']
  inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of OEM inspection of the returned part.']
  is_credit_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a credit memo has been issued.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for traceability of the returned part.']
  net_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and any adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Any supplemental information related to the return.']
  overstock_flag boolean [note: 'True when the return is due to dealer overstock.']
  part_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the part at the time of return.']
  quantity_returned varchar [note: 'Number of units returned.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the OEM received the returned parts.']
  return_authorization_number varchar [note: 'External reference number issued to authorize the parts return.']
  return_date date [note: 'Date the dealer initiated the return.']
  return_method varchar [note: 'Means by which the parts were returned to the OEM.']
  return_reason_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the return reason.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the part is being returned.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the return reason.']
  return_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or MES system that created the return record.']
  return_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the return.']
  return_type varchar [note: 'Category of the return based on business rules.']
  shipment_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking identifier for the return shipment.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the credit.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity returned.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the return record.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the return is covered under warranty.']

  Note: 'Parts return transaction record for defective, warranty-core, or over-ordered parts returned from a dealer to the OEM PDC. Captures return authorization number, originating dealer, return reason code (warranty defect, core return, overstock, campaign supersession), part number, quantity returned, credit amount, return shipment tracking, OEM inspection outcome (accepted, rejected, partial credit), and credit memo reference. Integrates with SAP MM returns management.'
}

Table aftersales.tsb_part_requirement {
  tsb_part_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for this TSB-to-part requirement record.']
  service_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the service part required for this TSB repair procedure.']
  tsb_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the Technical Service Bulletin that specifies this part requirement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this TSB-part requirement record was first created in the system.']
  installation_sequence varchar [note: 'Order in which this part should be installed during the repair procedure (1 = first, 2 = second, etc.). Relevant for complex multi-part TSB repairs.']
  is_primary_part boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this part is the primary component being replaced (true) or a supplemental part (gasket, fastener, fluid). Identified in detection reasoning as relationship-specific data.']
  part_revision varchar [note: 'Specific revision identifier of the part required for this TSB repair. Moved from tsb.part_revision because the revision requirement is specific to each TSB-part combination.']
  part_role_in_procedure varchar [note: 'Categorizes the part\'s function within the TSB repair procedure (e.g., primary replacement component, supplemental gasket, consumable fluid, diagnostic tool, optional upgrade). Identified in detection reasoning as relationship-specific data.']
  quantity_required decimal(10,3) [note: 'Number of units of this part required to complete the TSB repair procedure. Identified in detection reasoning as relationship-specific data.']
  required_parts varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers and revisions needed for the repair. [Moved from tsb: The tsb.required_parts STRING attribute is a denormalized representation of the M:N relationship. This comma-separated list should be normalized into individual tsb_part_requirement records. This is the classic indicator of a collapsed M:N relationship.]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this TSB-part requirement record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the parts bill-of-materials specified in a Technical Service Bulletin. Each record links one TSB to one service part with attributes that define the part\'s role, quantity, and revision requirements for executing the repair procedure described in the bulletin. This is actively managed by OEM parts engineering and used by dealer parts departments for inventory pre-positioning.. Existence Justification: In automotive aftersales operations, Technical Service Bulletins specify a bill-of-materials (parts list) required to execute the repair procedure, and service parts are referenced across multiple TSBs as they apply to different symptoms, model years, or VIN ranges. OEM parts engineering actively manages this relationship as \'TSB Part Requirements\' or \'Repair Kit Definitions\' to enable dealer parts departments to pre-position inventory. The current model\'s denormalized STRING fields (tsb.required_parts, tsb.part_revision) are clear evidence that this M:N relationship has been collapsed into a single record and should be normalized.'
}

Table billing.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the invoice record. Primary key.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Invoicing is performed per billing account; needed for account‑level revenue reporting and statement generation.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Invoice includes carrier charges; linking to carrier enables carrier performance reporting and compliance with freight cost audits.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Invoices must post to a legal entity (company code) for financial consolidation, statutory reporting, and intercompany reconciliation. Required for SAP FI integration and multi-entity automotive opera']
  dealer_statement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.dealer_statement. Business justification: A dealer statement is a periodic summary of invoices issued to a dealer. Individual invoices belong to a dealer statement period. Adding dealer_statement_id to invoice enables grouping invoices by sta']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Revenue recognition and period-close processes require invoices assigned to fiscal periods for accrual accounting, financial statement preparation, and revenue cutoff controls in automotive manufactur']
  invoice_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (dealer, retail buyer, fleet account, or corporate buyer) to whom this invoice is issued.']
  invoice_dealer_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer entity when the invoice is issued to a dealer for wholesale vehicle or parts transactions. Null for retail and fleet invoices.']
  invoice_dealership_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the dealer entity when the invoice is issued to a dealer for wholesale vehicle or parts transactions. Null for retail and fleet invoices.']
  invoice_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (dealer, retail buyer, fleet account, or corporate buyer) to whom this invoice is issued.']
  msrp_pricing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.msrp_pricing. Business justification: Automotive vehicle sale invoices are generated from the OEM MSRP pricing record — the invoice price basis, gas guzzler tax, EV tax credit eligibility, and destination charge all derive from the MSRP p']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_term. Business justification: Invoice references a payment term; using a foreign key to payment_term provides full term details and removes the redundant code column.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_purchase_order. Business justification: AP process matches supplier purchase orders to invoices for payment reconciliation; experts expect a PO FK on invoice.']
  rebate_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rebate_agreement. Business justification: Rebate agreements generate credit invoices or settlement invoices when rebates are paid out. Linking invoice.rebate_agreement_id to rebate_agreement enables tracking which invoices (particularly credi']
  rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the salesperson or account manager responsible for this sale, used for commission and performance tracking.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_contract. Business justification: REQUIRED: Service‑contract billing generates periodic invoices; linking enables contract‑level revenue and compliance tracking.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Freight invoices must reference specific shipments for cost reconciliation, accrual accounting, and dispute resolution. OEMs receive carrier invoices itemizing shipment-level charges; finance requires']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Needed for program-level financial reporting and cost allocation: linking each invoice to its vehicle program enables revenue tracking per engineering program.']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Invoices for vehicle sales need to reference the exact vehicle VIN; adding vin_id creates a direct link between billing and the vehicle master.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address to which the invoice is sent.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (suite, building, department).']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the billing address.']
  billing_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the legal entity or business unit issuing the invoice (e.g., regional sales company, parts distribution center, service center).']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the billing address.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for invoice cancellation (order cancelled, billing error, duplicate invoice).']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice was cancelled, if applicable. Null for active invoices.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  credit_memo_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to a credit memo document number if this invoice has been partially or fully credited.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code in which the invoice is denominated.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the invoice (volume discounts, promotional discounts, early payment discounts).']
  dispute_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice was flagged as disputed by the customer or internal review.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute if the invoice status is disputed (pricing error, quantity mismatch, quality issue, delivery issue).']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment is expected per the agreed payment terms.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year when the invoice was issued.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the invoice was issued, used for financial reporting and period closing.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before taxes, discounts, and adjustments.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was formally issued to the customer. Principal business event timestamp for the billing transaction.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique invoice document number issued to the customer. Business identifier for the invoice.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the billing and collection workflow.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice based on the customer segment and transaction nature (dealer wholesale, retail customer, fleet account, parts sale, service work, warranty claim).']
  model_year varchar [note: 'Model year of the vehicle or product being invoiced, providing context for pricing and product configuration.']
  msrp_based_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice pricing is based on MSRP (true) or negotiated/contracted pricing (false).']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final invoice amount payable after applying taxes, discounts, and all adjustments. This is the amount due from the customer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the invoice (special instructions, payment arrangements, delivery notes).']
  outstanding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice (net_amount minus paid_amount).']
  paid_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount paid against this invoice to date. Used to track partial payments.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or method expected or used for settling this invoice.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date when the full or final payment was received for this invoice. Null if not yet fully paid.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order or service order that this invoice bills for.']
  sales_region_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sales region or territory to which this invoice is attributed for revenue reporting.']
  sap_billing_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP SD billing document number from which this invoice was generated. System of record identifier.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the invoice (sales tax, VAT, GST, etc.).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified. Audit trail field.']
  vin varchar [note: '17-character Vehicle Identification Number of the vehicle being invoiced, if applicable (for vehicle sales invoices).']

  Note: 'Core billing document representing a formal demand for payment issued to dealers, retail customers, fleet accounts, or corporate buyers for vehicle sales, parts, or services. Captures invoice number, invoice date, due date, invoice type (dealer/retail/fleet/parts/service), billing entity, billing address, currency, total amount, tax amount, discount amount, net amount, payment terms, invoice status (draft/issued/partially_paid/paid/cancelled/disputed), SAP SD billing document reference, MSRP-based or negotiated pricing flag, and model year context. SSOT for all outbound billing documents in the enterprise.'
}

Business justification: Warranty claims generate invoice lines for OEM reimbursement to dealers. Critical for warranty accounting, dealer compensation, and financial reconciliation. Business justification: Goodwill adjustments create credit memo invoice lines for customer satisfaction resolutions. Required for financial adjustment tracking, customer account reconciliation, and goodwill cost reporting in']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent invoice header. Links this line item to the billing invoice document.']
  order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_line. Business justification: Invoice lines itemize order lines (vehicle, accessories, options, delivery charges). Core billing detail: each invoiced item traces to ordered item for revenue recognition, warranty tracking, and orde']
  part_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.part_master. Business justification: Required for warranty claim and recall tracking: each invoice line must reference the engineering part master to associate sold parts with design, compliance, and cost data.']
  parts_inventory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.parts_inventory. Business justification: Parts sales invoice lines reference dealer parts inventory for parts sold to dealers. Standard parts distribution invoicing - tracks which inventory items were invoiced.']
  parts_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.aftersales_parts_order. Business justification: Parts orders generate invoice lines when parts are sold to dealers or service centers. Essential for wholesale parts billing, dealer statement generation, and parts revenue recognition.']
  parts_return_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.parts_return. Business justification: Parts returns generate credit memo invoice lines for returned inventory. Essential for returns processing, inventory adjustment, dealer credit management, and parts return financial reconciliation.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_po_line. Business justification: Invoice line validation against PO line items is required for accurate cost allocation and audit; standard in automotive AP.']
  price_condition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.price_condition. Business justification: Invoice line items can be priced by a pricing condition; linking to price_condition normalizes pricing logic.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports profit‑center profitability analysis by linking each sales line to its profit center.']
  recall_completion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.recall_completion. Business justification: Recall reimbursement invoice lines reference specific recall completions. Enables recall-level payment tracking - critical for campaign cost management and regulatory reporting.']
  rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the salesperson who completed this transaction. Used for sales commission calculation and performance tracking.']
  repair_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.repair_order_line. Business justification: REQUIRED: Each invoice line originates from a repair‑order line; the link supports detailed cost‑to‑service analysis.']
  service_contract_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_contract_claim. Business justification: REQUIRED: Claims under service contracts are billed; linking claim to invoice line enables claim settlement and audit.']
  service_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to aftersales.service_part. Business justification: Service parts sold generate invoice line items with specific part pricing, cost, and margin tracking. Essential for parts revenue recognition, inventory valuation, and profitability analysis in afters']
  shipment_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment_leg. Business justification: Intermodal freight invoicing itemizes costs per leg (ocean, rail, trucking, port handling). Invoice lines must reference specific legs for cost allocation to business units, dispute resolution on leg-']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.sku_master. Business justification: Invoice lines for parts/materials sales must reference the specific SKU being billed. Essential for parts revenue tracking, warranty claims processing, and service parts invoicing in automotive afters']
  tax_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.tax_code. Business justification: Invoice line tax details should reference the tax_code master; the tax rate can be derived, so the string code and rate columns are redundant.']
  trim_option_availability_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vehicle_option_package. Business justification: Option packages (sunroof, towing, tech packages) are billed as discrete invoice line items in automotive dealer invoicing. Linking billing_invoice_line to vehicle_option_package enables option package']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Invoice line items are allocated to a specific vehicle for warranty and service charge tracking; linking ensures accurate vehicle‑level financial reporting.']
  billing_date date [note: 'Date when this line item was billed. Represents the accounting date for revenue recognition and the invoice document date.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description if this line was cancelled. Provides audit trail for cancelled billing lines and supports dispute analysis.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for this revenue line. Used for internal cost accounting and profitability analysis by organizational unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was first created in the billing system. Provides audit trail for record creation.']
  credit_memo_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the credit memo document if this line was credited. Links to the reversing document for returns, adjustments, or billing corrections.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Indicates the currency in which the line is billed (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, GBP, CAD).']
  dealer_code varchar [note: 'Dealer or distribution partner code responsible for this sale. Identifies the selling dealer location for dealer network reporting and commission calculation.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery document that fulfilled this line. Links the billing to the physical shipment or service delivery event.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total discount applied to this line item. Represents the monetary reduction from the base unit price, including promotional discounts, volume discounts, and negotiated reductions.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount expressed as a percentage of the unit price. Provides the rate of discount applied to calculate the discount_amount.']
  item_code varchar [note: 'Universal item identifier for the billed product or service. May represent a vehicle model code, part number, labor operation code, accessory SKU (Stock Keeping Unit), or fee code depending on line_item_type.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the billed item. Provides detailed text explaining the product, service, or charge represented by this line.']
  labor_operation_code varchar [note: 'Standardized labor operation code for service work. Identifies the specific repair or maintenance task performed. Populated only when line_item_type is labor.']
  line_item_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice line item. Distinguishes between vehicle units, parts, labor operations, accessories, service fees, freight charges, warranty products, and insurance products. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: vehicle|part|labor|accessory|fee|freight|warranty|insurance — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the invoice. Determines the ordering and display sequence of line items on the invoice document.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the invoice line. Tracks the lifecycle state from initial billing through payment or dispute resolution.']
  line_total_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount for this line item including all charges and taxes. Calculated as subtotal_amount + tax_amount. Represents the final billable amount for this line.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system that last modified this invoice line record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this invoice line record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to the record for audit and data quality purposes.']
  msrp decimal(18,4) [note: 'Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price for the item. The list price recommended by the OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer). Primarily applicable to vehicle and accessory line items.']
  negotiated_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final negotiated price per unit after dealer or customer negotiations. May differ from MSRP (Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price) based on market conditions, promotions, or customer agreements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this invoice line. Captures special instructions, explanations, or additional context for the billing line item.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center for internal P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting. Enables profitability analysis by business segment, product line, or geographic region.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Marketing promotion or incentive program code applied to this line. Tracks the specific promotional campaign that influenced the pricing or discount.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of items or units billed on this line. For vehicles typically 1, for parts the number of units, for labor the number of hours.']
  revenue_category varchar [note: 'Revenue classification for financial reporting and analytics. Categorizes the line item into major revenue streams for P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting and business intelligence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_vehicle|used_vehicle|parts|service|warranty|finance|insurance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sales_order_line_number varchar [note: 'Line number on the originating sales order. Provides traceability from the invoice line back to the specific sales order line item.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order document. Links the invoice line back to the sales order that authorized this billing.']
  service_date date [note: 'Date when the service or delivery occurred. For labor lines, the date the work was performed; for parts, the date the parts were delivered; for vehicles, the delivery date.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Line subtotal before taxes. Calculated as (quantity × unit_price) - discount_amount. Represents the net selling amount before tax.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax charged on this line item. Calculated by applying the tax_rate to the subtotal_amount. Includes sales tax, VAT (Value Added Tax), or GST (Goods and Services Tax) as applicable.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity. EA=Each (for countable items), HR=Hour (for labor), KG=Kilogram, LB=Pound, L=Liter, GAL=Gallon, M=Meter, FT=Foot. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|HR|KG|LB|L|GAL|M|FT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit before discounts and taxes. Represents the base selling price for one unit of the item.']
  warranty_code varchar [note: 'Warranty program code if this line represents warranty coverage. Identifies the specific warranty plan or extended service contract being billed.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system that created this invoice line record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Individual line item on a billing invoice representing a specific charge for a vehicle unit, parts SKU, labor operation, accessory, or service fee. Captures line number, item type (vehicle/part/labor/accessory/fee), item code, item description, VIN reference for vehicle lines, part number for parts lines, quantity, unit price, MSRP, negotiated price, discount amount, tax code, tax amount, line total, and revenue category. Supports multi-line invoices for mixed vehicle and parts/service billing.'
}

Table billing.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment record. Primary key for the payment entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: A payment is made against a billing account. While payment already links to invoice (which links to account), a direct account_id FK on payment enables efficient account-level payment queries, reconci']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Payments must be recorded against the receiving legal entity for cash management, bank reconciliation, and intercompany settlement in multi-entity automotive operations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Payments are charged to a cost center for cash‑flow and expense tracking.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Cash accounting and treasury reporting require payments assigned to fiscal periods for liquidity analysis, DSO calculation, and cash flow statement preparation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Cash receipt posting requires a GL account to record the payment in the ledger.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the invoice against which this payment is applied. Links payment to the billing document.']
  payment_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity making the payment (dealer, retail customer, fleet operator, or corporate account).']
  payment_payer_entity_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the entity making the payment (dealer, retail customer, fleet operator, or corporate account).']
  payment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_plan. Business justification: When a payment is made as an installment under a structured payment plan, the payment must reference the payment_plan it belongs to. This enables tracking of installment fulfillment, outstanding balan']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profit‑center reporting of cash receipts requires linking payments to the responsible profit center.']
  rebate_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.rebate_agreement. Business justification: Rebate agreements generate periodic settlement payments to dealers or fleet accounts. Linking payment.rebate_agreement_id to rebate_agreement enables tracking of which payments represent rebate settle']
  receivable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.receivable. Business justification: A payment directly reduces an open receivable. Linking payment.receivable_id to receivable enables direct AR management — tracking which receivable a payment closes or partially reduces, supporting du']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the payment received before any adjustments or fees.']
  applied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount that has been successfully applied to outstanding invoices or account balances.']
  authorization_code varchar [note: 'Authorization code provided by the payment processor or bank confirming approval of the payment transaction.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number from which the payment originated. Masked or tokenized for security. Used for reconciliation and fraud prevention.']
  bank_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the bank or financial institution for the payment transaction. Used for reconciliation and dispute resolution.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier for the batch or group of payments processed together. Used for bulk payment processing and reconciliation.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Interface or channel through which the payment was submitted. Distinguishes between online portal, mobile app, bank transfer, mail, and in-person payments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: online_portal|mobile_app|bank_transfer|mail|in_person|automated_system|dealer_system — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was cleared and funds were confirmed as received in the company bank account.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system that created the payment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discount amount granted for early payment according to payment terms (e.g., 2% discount for payment within 10 days).']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this payment is currently under dispute or has been disputed by the payer or payee.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for payment dispute. Populated only when dispute_flag is true.']
  lockbox_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the bank lockbox service used to receive and process the payment. Applicable for check and mail payments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to the payment. May include remittance advice, special instructions, or reconciliation notes.']
  payer_entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the entity making the payment. Distinguishes between dealer payments, retail customer payments, fleet operator payments, and corporate account payments.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was initiated or received by the payer. Represents the business event date of the payment transaction.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument or mechanism used to make the payment. Includes wire transfer, ACH (Automated Clearing House), check, credit card, floorplan financing, and dealer draft. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wire_transfer|ach|check|credit_card|debit_card|floorplan_financing|dealer_draft|electronic_funds_transfer|cash|promissory_note — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the payment transaction. Used for customer communication and reconciliation.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment. Tracks whether the payment is pending, cleared, returned, reversed, or cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|cleared|returned|reversed|cancelled|on_hold|partially_applied — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  processor varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party payment processor or gateway used to process the payment (e.g., Stripe, PayPal, bank name).']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was reconciled with bank statements and confirmed in the accounting system.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the payment has been successfully reconciled with bank statements and accounting records.']
  remittance_advice varchar [note: 'Detailed remittance information provided by the payer indicating which invoices or charges this payment is intended to cover.']
  return_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was returned or reversed by the bank or payment processor. Populated only when payment status is returned or reversed.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for payment return or reversal (e.g., insufficient funds, account closed, stop payment). Populated only when payment status is returned or reversed.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Bank routing number or SWIFT code for the payer financial institution. Used for wire transfers and ACH (Automated Clearing House) transactions.']
  sap_clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP FI clearing document that records the application of this payment to open invoices. Links payment to financial accounting records.']
  sap_payment_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP FI payment document that records the receipt of this payment. Primary link to ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) financial records.']
  terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms under which this payment was made (e.g., net 30, net 60, immediate). Links to master payment terms reference.']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment processor or gateway. Used for tracking and reconciliation with external systems.']
  unapplied_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the payment amount that remains unapplied and is held as credit on the payer account.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system that last modified the payment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Record of a payment received against an invoice or account balance from a dealer, retail customer, fleet operator, or corporate account. Captures payment date, payment method (wire/ACH/check/credit_card/floorplan/dealer_draft), payment amount, currency, applied invoice references, bank reference number, clearing date, payment status (pending/cleared/returned/reversed), SAP FI clearing document reference, and payer entity type. SSOT for all inbound payment receipts in the billing domain.'
}

Table billing.payment_allocation {
  payment_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment allocation record. Primary key for the payment allocation entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.account. Business justification: payment_allocation has a customer_account_number (STRING) denormalized field. Replacing this with a proper account_id FK to the account master normalizes the allocation record and enables direct accou']
  dealer_incentive_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dealer.dealer_incentive_claim.
