  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Category of the cost center indicating its primary function within the organization.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the cost center\'s primary location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code used for budgeting and reporting.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes valid for posting costs.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the cost center is retired or no longer valid (nullable).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the budget is defined (e.g., FY2025).']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy (1 = top level).']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the cost center\'s budget and performance were last reviewed.']
  region varchar [note: 'Business region (e.g., North America, Europe) where the cost center operates.']
  reporting_level varchar [note: 'Level at which the cost center is aggregated for financial reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost center record.']
  variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual spend (budget – actual).']

  Note: 'Cost center master record representing an organizational unit to which manufacturing costs, labor, overhead, and indirect expenses are assigned. Aligned with SAP CO cost center accounting (CCA). Captures cost center hierarchy, responsible manager, plant assignment, profit center linkage, valid-from/to dates, currency, and cost center category (production, administration, R&D, sales). Supports EBITDA reporting and profitability analysis by plant, vehicle line, and region.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the profit center record.']
  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent profit center in the hierarchy.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant associated with the profit center.']

  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code to which the profit center belongs.']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profit center record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which the profit center records its transactions.']

  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the profit center became operational.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the profit center is scheduled to be retired (nullable for open‑ended).']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used in external ERP or reporting systems to reference this profit center.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Slash‑delimited path showing the profit center\'s position in the hierarchy (e.g., /1000/2000/3000).']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the profit center is included in corporate consolidation.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'True if the profit center participates in intercompany transactions.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent financial or operational review.']
  margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Profit margin expressed as a percentage of revenue.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments about the profit center.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name of the business owner or sponsor of the profit center.']
  plan_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned financial amount for the upcoming period.']
  profit_center_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category such as Vehicle Line, Service, Parts, or Finance.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in SAP and external reports.']
  profit_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the profit center\'s purpose and scope.']
  profit_center_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping used for internal reporting (e.g., Group A, Group B).']
  profit_center_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the profit center within the organizational hierarchy.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the profit center.']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Category of profit center indicating its accounting purpose.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region of the profit center.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for consolidated financial reporting of the profit center.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of scheduled reviews for the profit center.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the profit center based on financial and operational exposure.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Segment classification of the profit center (e.g., electric vehicle, internal combustion, hybrid, commercial).']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status of the profit center.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the profit center record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Profit center master record representing a management accounting unit used to analyze profitability by vehicle line, plant, region, or business segment. Aligned with SAP CO-PCA (Profit Center Accounting). Captures profit center hierarchy, responsible manager, company code assignment, segment classification (EV, ICE, HEV, commercial), and reporting currency. Enables contribution margin and EBITDA analysis at granular business unit level.'
}

  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the entity\'s address.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the legal entity.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction applicable to the entity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the company code record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website URL of the legal entity.']

  Note: 'Legal entity and company code master record representing an independent accounting unit within the Automotive enterprise. Aligned with SAP FI company code configuration. Captures legal entity name, country of incorporation, fiscal year variant, chart of accounts assignment, local currency, functional currency, IFRS/GAAP reporting standard, tax jurisdiction, and intercompany settlement group. Supports multi-entity consolidation and intercompany eliminations.'
}

Table finance.fiscal_period {
  fiscal_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the fiscal period record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: fiscal_period.company_code is a denormalized STRING attribute. In SAP FI, fiscal periods are defined per company code (fiscal year variant is company-code-specific). Adding company_code_id FK normaliz']
  accrual_cutoff_date date [note: 'Date after which accruals are no longer allowed for this period.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the fiscal period record was initially created in the system.']
  fiscal_period_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text notes describing special characteristics of the period.']
  fiscal_period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the period used for posting: open, closed, or locked.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year to which the period belongs, e.g., 2024.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for the fiscal year variant configuration (e.g., 12‑month, 4‑quarter, 13‑period).']
  is_current_period boolean [note: 'True if this period is the active period for ongoing postings.']
  is_interim boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period is an interim reporting period (true) or a full fiscal period (false).']
  lock_date date [note: 'Date on which the period was locked for posting; null if not locked.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the fiscal period.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable label for the period, e.g., "January", "Q1", "Special Adjustment".']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the period within the fiscal year (1‑12 for monthly, 1‑4 for quarterly).']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the fiscal period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period: regular reporting, adjustment, or special period.']
  posting_deadline_date date [note: 'Final date by which all transactions must be posted to this period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the fiscal period record.']

  Note: 'Fiscal period and fiscal year (FY) calendar reference master defining the financial reporting periods used across the enterprise. Captures fiscal year, period number, period name, start date, end date, period status (open, closed, locked), period type (regular, special/adjustment), and company code applicability. Governs FY close processes, period-end accruals, and IFRS/GAAP reporting windows. Aligned with SAP FI fiscal year variant configuration.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Replace string company_code with FK to company_code for proper relational integrity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Reference cost_center master instead of free‑text code.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Link journal entry to fiscal period master for consistent period handling.']
  party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business partner (vendor, customer, or other) associated with the entry.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Reference profit_center master for reporting consistency.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Automotive OEMs track compliance-related costs (certification fees, testing expenses, regulatory fines, remediation costs) in journal entries. Finance teams need to report compliance spending by regul']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of the journal entry in the document currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the amounts.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the line is a debit or credit.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date recorded on the accounting document (may differ from posting date).']
  document_language varchar [note: 'Language key of the document (e.g., EN, DE).']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External document number assigned by SAP FI for the journal entry.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Type of accounting document (e.g., SA – General Ledger, KR – Vendor Invoice, AB – Customer Invoice).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert foreign currency to local currency.']
  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Type of exchange rate (e.g., M – average, G – spot).']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the entry is part of an intercompany transaction.']
  is_adjustment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entry is an adjusting entry (e.g., accrual).']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'True if the entry is part of a consolidated financial statement.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'True if the entry was entered manually rather than by automated process.']
  is_test_entry boolean [note: 'True if the entry is a test or simulation record.']
  journal_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the journal entry.']
  ledger_group varchar [note: 'Ledger group indicating IFRS or local GAAP ledger.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of line items associated with this journal entry.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Plant code where the transaction originated.']
  posting_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the posting (e.g., GL, AP, AR).']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP posting key defining the transaction type (e.g., 40 for debit).']
  posting_period varchar [note: 'Posting period identifier (e.g., 202401).']
  posting_reference varchar [note: 'External reference identifier (e.g., external system ID).']
  posting_text varchar [note: 'User‑defined text describing the posting.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the entry was posted to the ledger.']
  posting_user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the user who posted the entry (e.g., accountant, system).']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number linking to related documents (e.g., invoice).']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original entry being reversed.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the entry is a reversal of a previous entry.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Segment identifier for internal reporting (e.g., automotive, powertrain).']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module within the source system (e.g., FI‑GL).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that generated the entry (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for the entry.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applied to the entry for tax determination.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code applicable to the entry.']
  transaction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the business transaction (e.g., AP, AR).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the journal entry record.']

  Note: 'General ledger journal entry header record capturing all financial postings in the SAP FI ledger. Represents the primary transactional record for every accounting document including goods issue postings, vendor invoice postings, customer payments, accruals, depreciation runs, intercompany settlements, and manual adjustments. Captures document type, posting date, fiscal year/period, company code, reference document, posting user, reversal indicator, and ledger group (IFRS vs local GAAP). SSOT for all GL postings.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the journal entry line record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Link line to cost_center master for cost allocation.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: journal_entry_line has fiscal_period (STRING) and fiscal_year (STRING) as denormalized attributes. The journal_entry header already has fiscal_period_id FK. Adding fiscal_period_id to the line item no']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Replace string GL account code with FK to gl_account master.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent journal entry (header) to which this line belongs.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Link line to profit_center master for profitability reporting.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Reference WBS element master for project cost tracking.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the GL account (e.g., balance sheet, profit & loss).']
  amount_cc decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount posted on the line in the company code (local) currency.']
  amount_tc decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount posted on the line in the transaction currency.']
  assignment varchar [note: 'User‑defined assignment field for additional categorisation (e.g., cost object).']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Business area classification for reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry line record was created in the system.']
  currency_cc varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the company code (local) currency.']
  currency_tc varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the transaction currency.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Flag indicating whether the line is a debit (D) or credit (C).']
  document_date date [note: 'Date printed on the accounting document.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert amount_tc to amount_cc.']
  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Identifier of the exchange rate type (e.g., M for market, A for average).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the journal entry, used for ordering.']
  line_text varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the line item.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or location code associated with the posting.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the line is posted to the ledger.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP posting key that determines the type of posting (e.g., debit/credit).']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity associated with the line (e.g., number of units, hours).']
  reference_document_item varchar [note: 'Item number of the referenced external document.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External document number referenced by this line (e.g., invoice).']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this line reverses a previous posting.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Segment code for profitability analysis.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code used for tax calculation on the line.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the journal entry line record.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a GL journal entry, representing a single debit or credit posting to a GL account. Captures GL account, debit/credit indicator, posting amount in transaction currency and company code currency, cost center, profit center, WBS element, plant, tax code, assignment field, and line item text. Supports detailed cost allocation, profitability analysis, and SOX audit trail requirements. Aligned with SAP FI line item table (BSEG).'
}

Table finance.ar_invoice {
  ar_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the AR invoice record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: ar_invoice.cost_center_code is a denormalized STRING reference to the cost center. Adding cost_center_id FK normalizes this relationship, enabling cost center-based AR reporting and profitability anal']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: ar_invoice has fiscal_period (STRING) and fiscal_year (STRING) as denormalized attributes. Adding fiscal_period_id FK normalizes the fiscal period reference on AR invoices, enabling proper period-base']
  homologation_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.homologation_record. Business justification: OEMs invoice customers for homologation and certification services (type approval testing, emissions certification). Invoices reference specific homologation records for billing accuracy and audit tra']
  primary_ar_company_code_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related legal entity in an intercompany invoice.']
  primary_customer_party_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer or dealer billed on the invoice.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: ar_invoice.profit_center_code is a denormalized STRING reference to the profit center. Adding profit_center_id FK normalizes this relationship, enabling profit center-based revenue and AR reporting. T']
  vehicle_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.vehicle_order. Business justification: Invoices must reference originating vehicle order for reconciliation, warranty start date tracking, and dispute resolution. Essential for automotive order-to-cash process and customer service operatio']
  vin_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.vin_registry. Business justification: Accounts receivable invoices for vehicle sales must reference the VIN registry to validate warranty eligibility and tax reporting.']
  accounting_date date [note: 'Date used for accounting period posting.']
  aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Age category of the invoice based on days past due.']
  ar_invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the invoice.']
  billing_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the SAP billing document linked to this invoice.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Status of the invoice in the collections process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the invoice.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Delivery document associated with the shipped goods.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any discount applied to the invoice.']
  discount_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or code for the discount granted.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the product was sold (e.g., dealer, fleet, direct).']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected according to payment terms.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, and adjustments.']
  intercompany_flag boolean [note: 'True if the invoice is part of an intercompany transaction.']
  invoice_category varchar [note: 'Business segment or market category for the invoice.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was issued to the customer.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice identifier assigned by the billing system.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the invoice content.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes and discounts.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount actually received from the customer.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the customer to settle the invoice.']
  payment_received_date date [note: 'Date on which payment was recorded.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment transaction.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition applied to the invoice.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant where the vehicle or part was produced.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was posted to the general ledger.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order from the customer or dealer, if applicable.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for reporting and tax purposes.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date on which revenue from this invoice is recognized per accounting policy.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Sales order that triggered the invoice.']
  sales_org_code varchar [note: 'Code of the sales organization responsible for the transaction.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the invoice.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code used for tax calculation.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the invoice.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the invoice record.']
  warranty_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount set aside for future warranty claims related to this invoice.']
  warranty_reserve_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a warranty reserve has been booked for this invoice.']

  Note: 'Accounts Receivable (AR) customer invoice record capturing outgoing invoices issued to dealers, fleet customers, and intercompany entities for vehicle sales, parts, and services. Aligned with SAP FI-AR and SD billing integration. Captures customer/dealer ID, invoice date, due date, payment terms, gross amount, tax amount, net amount, currency, billing document reference, vehicle line, MSRP vs net selling price, and collection status. Supports dealer billing reconciliation and revenue recognition.'
}

Table finance.ar_payment {
  ar_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the AR payment record.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: ar_payment.invoice_number is a denormalized STRING reference to the AR invoice. Payments are applied to invoices in the AR process. Adding ar_invoice_id FK establishes the proper referential relations']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: ar_payment has no company_code reference despite being a financial transaction that must be posted to a specific legal entity. Adding company_code_id FK enables legal entity-level AR payment reporting']
  primary_party_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (dealer, fleet account, or intercompany entity) that made the payment.']
  ar_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number where the payment was received.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank that received the payment.']
  cash_application_status varchar [note: 'Status of cash application against the invoice(s).']
  clearance_date date [note: 'Date the payment cleared the bank and was posted to the ledger.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the payment.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discounts applied to the payment, if any.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the payment was expected according to terms.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency conversion rate applied when payment currency differs from functional currency.']
  exchange_rate_date date [note: 'Date on which the exchange rate was sourced.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount received before any deductions.']
  is_partial_payment boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the payment covers the full invoice amount.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount applied to the invoice after tax and discounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional information about the payment.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment amount in the original foreign currency before conversion.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was submitted.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date the payment was received or processed.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to make the payment.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Unique payment reference assigned by the finance system.']
  payment_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the payment within the organization.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms associated with the invoice.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was posted to the general ledger.']
  remittance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference provided by the payer to reconcile the payment.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component deducted from the gross amount, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment record.']

  Note: 'Accounts Receivable incoming payment record capturing payments received from dealers, fleet accounts, and intercompany entities against AR invoices. Captures payment date, customer/dealer ID, payment method, amount received, currency, cleared invoice references, bank account, remittance advice reference, partial payment indicator, and cash application status. Supports dealer receivables management, DSO (Days Sales Outstanding) tracking, and cash flow forecasting.'
}

Table finance.capex_request {
  capex_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the CapEx request record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Code of the manufacturing plant where the investment will be implemented.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: CapEx requests in SAP are assigned to profit centers for management accounting and EBITDA impact analysis. capex_request currently has cost_center_id but no profit_center_id. Adding profit_center_id e']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.project. Business justification: CapEx requests in automotive manufacturing are typically associated with capital projects (e.g., new production line, EV platform investment). capex_request already links to wbs_element which links to']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Capex requests must be tied to the specific regulatory requirement they satisfy for compliance tracking and audit.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.wbs_element']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date when the project was actually completed.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative amount actually spent to date on the project.']
  approval_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request received final approval.']
  approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget amount approved by the governance board.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost of the proposed investment.']
  capex_request_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the proposed capital investment, including objectives and scope.']
  capex_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the request.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the budget.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the capital asset.']
  depreciation_years varchar [note: 'Number of years over which the asset will be depreciated.']
  external_funding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of external funding contributions.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the request belongs (e.g., FY2024).']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Name of the external entity providing funding (e.g., government grant, joint venture).']
  has_external_funding boolean [note: 'Indicates whether part of the investment is funded by external sources.']
  investment_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the investment type.']
  irr decimal(5,2) [note: 'Internal rate of return percentage for the proposed investment.']
  is_capitalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the expense is capitalized (true) or expensed (false).']
  is_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the request complies with all applicable regulations.']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business case narrative explaining the need and expected benefits.']
  npv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net present value of the investment calculated in the request currency.']
  payback_period_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated number of years to recover the investment.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the request.']
  procurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to acquire the goods or services.']
  project_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date of the capital project.']
  project_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the capital project.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory approvals for the investment.']
  request_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the request was initially submitted.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the request (e.g., CR‑2024‑001).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment rating for the proposed investment.']
  supporting_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to attached supporting documentation stored in the document management system.']
  tax_implication varchar [note: 'Notes on tax treatment or incentives related to the investment.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief title describing the investment proposal.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Capital Expenditure (CapEx) appropriation request record capturing investment proposals for manufacturing equipment, tooling, plant infrastructure, EV battery production lines, and R&D facilities. Captures project title, requesting plant/department, investment category (tooling, machinery, IT, facility, EV/autonomous R&D), estimated total cost, approved budget, actual spend to date, project start/end dates, approval workflow status, NPV/IRR justification, and WBS element linkage. Supports CapEx governance and FY budget planning.'
}

Table finance.budget_plan {
  budget_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the budget plan record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Budget plans are created per legal entity (company code) in SAP. budget_plan currently has cost_center_id, profit_center_id, and gl_account_id but no company_code_id. Adding this FK enables legal enti']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Budget plans are tied to specific fiscal periods for planning and variance analysis. budget_plan has fiscal_year (INT) and planning_period (STRING) as denormalized attributes but no fiscal_period_id F']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.gl_account']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Finance teams budget for compliance obligations (testing programs, certification costs, recall reserves). Budget plans reference specific obligations to allocate resources, track compliance spending, ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.profit_center']
  project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project linked to this budget plan, if applicable.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_territory. Business justification: Sales territory budgets (marketing spend, headcount, incentives) require explicit territory assignment for regional P&L planning and territory performance analysis. Standard practice for automotive re']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Budget plans in automotive manufacturing are often created at the WBS element level for project-based cost tracking (e.g., EV platform development, plant expansion). budget_plan already has project_id']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate the budget across cost objects.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the budget plan received formal approval.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business area the budget pertains to.']
  budget_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the budget plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget plan record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the budget amounts.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the budget plan expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the budget plan becomes effective.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the budget applies.']
  is_forecast boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is a forecast rather than a firm budget.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'True if the budget plan is locked from further edits.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the budget plan.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the budget plan in financial systems.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the budget plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the budget plan indicating its purpose.']
  planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original budgeted monetary amount.']
  planning_period varchar [note: 'Period covered by the budget (e.g., FY2025, Q1).']
  revised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Updated budget amount after revisions.']
  scenario varchar [note: 'Analytical scenario applied to the budget (e.g., base, optimistic, pessimistic).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system for the budget data (e.g., SAP CO, BPC).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the budget plan.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the budget plan for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Annual and multi-year financial budget plan record capturing planned financial targets by cost center, profit center, GL account, and fiscal year. Supports FY budget planning, rolling forecast cycles, and variance analysis. Captures budget version (original, revised, forecast), budget type (opex, capex, headcount), planning period, planned amount, currency, approval status, and responsible controller. Aligned with SAP CO planning and SAP BPC (Business Planning and Consolidation) processes.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the fixed asset record.']
  capex_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.capex_request. Business justification: Fixed assets are created from approved CapEx requests in the automotive manufacturing capital investment process. Linking fixed_asset to capex_request enables tracking of the full CapEx lifecycle: req']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Fixed assets in SAP AA are always assigned to a company code as the primary organizational unit. fixed_asset currently only has plant_id (cross-domain) but no company_code_id. Adding this FK enables l']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: fixed_asset.cost_center_code is a denormalized STRING reference to the cost center. Fixed assets are assigned to cost centers for depreciation cost allocation in SAP AA. Adding cost_center_id FK norma']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the asset is located.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: In SAP AA, fixed assets can be linked to WBS elements for project-based asset tracking (e.g., assets created during a plant expansion project). Adding wbs_element_id FK enables project-level asset rep']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recorded to date for the asset.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original cost incurred to acquire the asset, recorded in the functional currency.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was acquired or placed into service.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset according to the company\'s asset class hierarchy (e.g., machinery, building, IT equipment).']
  asset_condition varchar [note: 'Current physical condition of the asset.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the asset, including purpose and key characteristics.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the asset.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the asset.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset tag or number used for tracking the asset within the organization.']
  asset_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of asset within its class (e.g., stamping press, paint shop robot, battery assembly line).']
  capitalized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost has been capitalized (true) or expensed (false).']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Numeric rating (1‑5) of the asset’s condition, where 5 is best.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the asset record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the acquisition cost.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate depreciation (e.g., straight‑line, declining balance, units of production).']
  depreciation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation calculations begin for the asset.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of the asset (e.g., sale, scrap, donation).']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount covered by the insurance policy.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the insurance coverage expires.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the insurance covering the asset.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection or audit of the asset.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific physical location (e.g., warehouse, line, bay) of the asset.']
  maintenance_schedule varchar [note: 'Reference to the maintenance plan applicable to the asset.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the asset.']
  model varchar [note: 'Model designation or number assigned by the manufacturer.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current carrying amount of the asset (acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation).']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled inspection.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department accountable for the asset’s operation and maintenance.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date the asset was officially retired from service.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number uniquely identifying the physical unit.']
  tax_depreciation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Depreciation amount recognized for tax reporting purposes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the asset record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated economic life of the asset expressed in years for depreciation purposes.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the asset warranty period expires.']
  warranty_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the party providing the warranty for the asset.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the asset warranty period begins.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset master record for all capitalized assets including manufacturing machinery, production tooling, dies, stamping presses, paint shop equipment, EV battery assembly lines, plant buildings, and IT infrastructure. Aligned with SAP FI-AA (Asset Accounting). Captures asset class, asset description, acquisition date, acquisition cost, useful life, depreciation method (straight-line, declining balance), accumulated depreciation, net book value, plant assignment, cost center, and retirement date. Supports CapEx tracking and IFRS/GAAP asset disclosure.'
}

Table finance.currency_rate {
  currency_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each currency rate record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Currency rates are maintained per legal entity; linking to company_code provides context and eliminates the isolated lookup table.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: currency_rate has fiscal_year (STRING) and period (STRING) as denormalized attributes. Currency rates in SAP are period-specific (monthly average rates, period-end rates). Adding fiscal_period_id FK n']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the currency rate record was first created in the system.']
  currency_from varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency being converted from.']
  currency_rate_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional context or notes about the rate (e.g., special market conditions).']
  currency_to varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency being converted to.']
  is_historical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rate is a historical record (true) or a current/forecast rate (false).']
  rate_date date [note: 'Calendar date to which the exchange rate applies (e.g., daily rate).']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate: spot, average, period‑end, or planning.']
  rate_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Numeric exchange rate value expressed as the amount of target currency per one unit of source currency.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the rate data, such as European Central Bank, Bloomberg, or internal treasury.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the currency rate record.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Start date of the period during which the rate is valid.']
  valid_to date [note: 'End date of the period during which the rate is valid; null if open‑ended.']

  Note: 'Foreign exchange (FX) currency rate reference record capturing daily, monthly average, and period-end exchange rates used for multi-currency financial reporting and transaction conversion. Captures currency pair (from/to), rate type (spot, average, period-end, planning), valid date, exchange rate, rate source (ECB, Bloomberg, internal treasury), and company code applicability. Supports IFRS translation of foreign subsidiary financials, transaction currency conversion, and FX exposure reporting.'
}

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the WBS record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual completion date of the WBS element.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to external systems (e.g., ERP document number).']

  is_capex boolean [note: 'True if the element captures capital expenditure, false otherwise.']
  is_r_and_d boolean [note: 'True if the element is part of research & development projects.']

  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the WBS element.']
  planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted cost amount originally planned for the WBS element.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location or site name of the plant.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the overall project to which the WBS element belongs.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the WBS element record.']
  start_date date [note: 'Effective start date when the WBS element becomes active.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the WBS record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the WBS record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the WBS element, typically used in SAP PS and project documentation.']
  wbs_element_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the WBS element.']
  wbs_hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Delimited path representing the full hierarchy (e.g., 1.2.3).']
  wbs_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the element within the WBS tree (1 = top level).']
  wbs_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the WBS element.']
  wbs_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the WBS element by business purpose.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the WBS record.']

  Note: 'Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element master record used for project-based cost tracking including CapEx projects, new model launch programs (SOP/EOP), EV/autonomous R&D programs, and plant expansion projects. Aligned with SAP PS (Project System). Captures WBS code, project definition, WBS level, responsible cost center, planned cost, actual cost, committed cost, project status, start/end dates, and investment program linkage. Enables granular CapEx spend tracking and R&D capitalization under IFRS IAS 38.'
}

Homologation records must reference the exact configuration certified. body_style, dri']
  jurisdiction_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.jurisdiction. Business justification: homologation_record.market_jurisdiction is a denormalized string representing the regulatory jurisdiction for the type approval. The jurisdiction table is the authoritative reference master for all re']
  model_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.model. Business justification: Homologation records certify a specific vehicle model for market approval; FK to model ties approvals to the correct model definition.']
  powertrain_variant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vehicle.powertrain_variant. Business justification: EU type approval and UNECE regulations certify specific powertrain variants. Homologation records reference engine displacement, fuel type, and transmission — all powertrain_variant attributes. engine']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: homologation_record.regulatory_reference is a denormalized string referencing the applicable regulation (e.g., FMVSS 208, WLTP, Euro 6). The regulatory_requirement table is the authoritative reference']
  vehicle_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.vehicle_program. Business justification: Homologation records certify specific vehicle programs for market entry. The homologation_record has vehicle_model and model_year as denormalized plain-text fields. Direct FK to vehicle_program normal']
  approval_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the homologation approval.']
  approval_number varchar [note: 'Official approval number or certificate identifier issued by the regulatory authority.']
  approval_status_date date [note: 'Date when the current status was assigned to the approval.']
  approval_type varchar [note: 'Category of the approval (e.g., type approval, certification, test, homologation).']
  certification_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic copy of the certification document.']
  co2_emissions_g_per_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured CO2 emissions in grams per kilometer.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes regarding special conditions, exemptions, or observations.']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the certification document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the approval is effective for sales.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the approval expires or must be renewed.']
